Title: Data Analysts
Description:
We are looking for 2 Data Analysts who will be embedded within the Operations department, supporting the business with day-to-day data needs and ad hoc analytical requests. The role combines close collaboration with operational teams and the development of reporting solutions, helping translate business questions into actionable insights and data-driven decision-making. While the tools and deliverables remain similar, the focus is on operational support rather than project-based change requests.

We are recruiting for two similar positions, requiring largely the same capabilities and experience. For one role, the emphasis is on Qlik reporting and dashboard development. For the second role, experience with Qlik remains important, but there is a stronger focus on Snowflake, including data modelling, data sourcing and working within the Snowflake environment.

Responsibilities


Collaborate closely with business stakeholders to clarify needs, define business rules, and align on requirements
Perform analysis and translate business questions into clear data requirements
Design data solutions and define the blueprint for implementation
Build and implement data solutions within the existing data platform
Deliver reporting, dashboards, and insights to the business and external stakeholders
Support both ad hoc and structural reporting needs, including regulatory reporting
Ensure validation and alignment of delivered solutions with business expectations


Requirements & Qualifications


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field such as Data, IT, Engineering, or Business Analytics
Proven experience in data analysis and data solution delivery
Experience working in a business-facing role with stakeholder interaction
Ability to work in environments with limited predefined requirements and high ambiguity.


Must Have Skills


Strong knowledge of Snowflake (data platform)
Solid SQL skills (views, models, tables)
Experience with Qlik
Experience working with data frameworks and principles within Snowflake
Ability to translate business needs into technical solutions
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Comfortable working in a dynamic environment with changing priorities
End-to-end ownership mindset


Nice To Have Skills


Experience with Power BI (or similar BI tools)
Exposure to building dashboards and reporting solutions in business environments

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Wil jij met data échte impact maken op het onderwijs van morgen?

Als Data Analist help je onze organisatie betere beslissingen nemen door data om te zetten in heldere inzichten. Je werkt met een moderne data stack (SQL, Power BI, Snowflake, ...) en krijgt veel autonomie om end-to-end oplossingen uit te bouwen die ook écht gebruikt worden.

Je komt terecht in een mensgerichte organisatie waar data steeds belangrijker wordt en waar jouw werk zichtbaar bijdraagt aan maatschappelijke impact.




Jouw team

Je wordt onderdeel van het Data & Analytics team, waar je samenwerkt met één andere data-analist in België. Daarnaast heb je regelmatig contact met een groter team van data-analisten en experts in Nederland.

Via kennisdeling en best practices versterk je je expertise en draag je mee bij aan de verdere uitbouw van een datagedreven organisatie. Af en toe werk je ook samen op locatie in Den Bosch.




Wat ga je doen?

Als Data Analist werk je van businessvraag tot bruikbaar inzicht. Je fungeert als brug tussen data en besluitvorming en helpt collega's data beter te begrijpen en te gebruiken.




Concreet betekent dit dat je:

• Businessvragen scherp stelt en vertaalt naar analyses en datavereisten.

• Data verzamelt, transformeert en modelleert uit verschillende bronnen.

• End-to-end oplossingen bouwt met SQL en Snowflake; ervaring met Power BI en dbt is een mooie plus.

• Heldere, actiegerichte dashboards en rapporten ontwikkelt die effectief gebruikt worden.

• Datakwaliteit, governance en betrouwbaarheid mee bewaakt.

• Inzichten en aanbevelingen duidelijk communiceert naar zowel technische als niet-technische stakeholders.

• Business teams ondersteunt in projecten en besluitvorming.

• Meebouwt aan de verdere professionalisering van processen, data-infrastructuur en het dataplatform.

• Data literacy binnen de organisatie versterkt via begeleiding, workshops of kennisdeling.




Wie zoeken wij?

Je hoeft niet alles al perfect te beheersen; leergierigheid en analytisch denkvermogen zijn minstens zo belangrijk.




• Je hebt een bachelor diploma of gelijkwaardige ervaring.

• Je hebt 2 tot 4 jaar ervaring in data-analyse, BI of insights.

• Je werkt vlot met SQL en BI-tools.

• Ervaring met dbt, Power BI en Snowflake is een sterke troef, of je wil je hierin snel verdiepen.

• Kennis van Python is een plus.

• Je kan complexe informatie helder uitleggen aan verschillende doelgroepen.

• Je werkt gestructureerd, denkt analytisch en neemt ownership.

• Je bent een teamspeler die kennis deelt en graag bijleert.

• Je volgt trends in data, AI en analytics en denkt mee over toepassingen.

• Je kan professioneel communiceren in het Frans of bent bereid dit verder te versterken.




Wat mag je van ons verwachten?

• Een mensgerichte cultuur met veel aandacht voor welzijn en werkgeluk.

• Een betekenisvolle job met maatschappelijke impact.

• Werken binnen een sterke internationale groep (Sanoma).

• Ruimte om te groeien, leren en initiatief te nemen.

• Samenwerking met ervaren data-professionals in België en Nederland.

• Een competitief loon aangevuld met extralegale voordelen.

• Hybride werken met meerdere dagen per week thuiswerk.

• Moderne kantoren in Wommelgem en Mont-Saint-Guibert, volledig uitgerust voor hybride samenwerking.

Title: HR Data Analyst
Description:
Wat ga je doen

As HR Data Analyst, you increase insight into how our associates experience HR and our key HR KPI’s. You will help lead our HR community to drive data-driven decision making across our organization. You will design and create (where needed together with our BI or IT teams) operational/tactical metrics and dashboards that will assist in making decisions on different HR domains. You will be partnering across teams to ensure that our HR metrics are consistent and aligned to our strategic goals. You will be responsible for digging into the details to improve data quality, show new insights and make new recommendations that will impact decision making.


Profound expertise on HR data, HR reporting’s (operational & strategic) and HR dashboard(s) : With your profound expertise on HR data, reporting and dashboards you easily identify possible impacts and are able to create ad-hoc reports.
Drive data-driven decision making : You help lead our HR community to drive data-driven decision making across our organization. You provide HR related insights to our HR Community and organization.
HR Data analytics roadmap : Take ownership of our HR Data analytics roadmap by identifying, designing and deploying HR metrics and dashboards.
Stakeholder management : Identify and support stakeholders to become data-minded. Take initiatives to increase data awareness.
Predictive analytics : Co-deliver, together with the BI team, predictive analytics for HR.


Wie ben jij


You have a master degree in Econometrics, Mathemathics, Statistics, Computer Sciences or other relevant fields of study around Data, Math/Statistics.
You have a minimum of 3 years of relevant experience.
You have experience with Power BI, and ideally other data analytics tools.
Ideally, you have experience with SAP HR and SuccessFactors.
You have proven to be convincing and to be able to build trust and alignment on all levels in and outside the organization.
You should be passionate about and have experience with data and reportings and have a passion to learn and master new data analysis techniques and data science methodologies. You follow pro-actively trends in the business.
You are skilled at providing clear insights within a demanding environment.
You bring strong interpersonal communication skills.
You have the ability to work cross-functionally.
You have proven to be creative in finding solutions for complex issues with detail-oriented and simplicity mindset.
You have a strong analytical profile and ability to document requirements, functional specifications, decisions made, etc.
You have project management skills.
You manage change and drive process improvement.
You are fluent in Dutch or French, with a good knowledge of English.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
An organisation is looking for 2 driven data analysts, with a strong affinity and interest in news, sports, or online streaming content.

Data Analyst — Sports

Covers sports content for both dedicated fans and casual followers, through a differentiated, multi-channel, multimedia offering. To bring this content even closer to the audience, we're looking for a Data Analyst who will drive the team toward more data-driven decisions on content and distribution — reaching, informing, and inspiring more people with quality, tailored content.

Data Analyst — News

Delivers daily coverage across multiple digital platforms, for a broad audience at various points throughout the day. Here too, we're looking for a Data Analyst to support the team in making more data-driven decisions on content and distribution.

Data Analyst — Streaming

Offers an extensive range of video and audio content. As Data Analyst, you'll use data and insights to drive the platform's tactical and strategic choices across all touchpoints (site, app, social media, …).

What We Expect From You

As Data Analyst, you play a key role between the central Data Intelligence team and the content teams, acting as the go-to person for insights on content and user behaviour across the relevant touchpoints (website, app, social media channels, …).

You know how to translate data analysis into an accessible, actionable format for content teams and the wider organisation, and can support, advise, and guide them toward working with relevant data themselves.

Responsibilities:


Analysing user behaviour and content consumption across touchpoints, including social media channels
Measuring, analysing, and reporting on the impact of content, marketing, SEO, and CRM initiatives, and proactively proposing optimisations and actionable recommendations
Recurring KPI reporting (weekly, monthly, quarterly) and actionable insights to content teams and management
Collaborating with other data analysts, UX, and Marketing to optimise the user experience
Supporting the execution of A/B tests and implementing improvements
Convincing content teams and stakeholders to take data-driven action, and guiding them toward greater autonomy with data (dashboards, reports, segmentation manager)
Working closely with other disciplines within the data team and staying up to date on the latest developments in data
Willingness to work on-site 3 days per wee

Covers sports content for both dedicated fans and casual followers, through a differentiated, multi-channel, multimedia offering. To bring this content even closer to the audience, we're looking for a Data Analyst who will drive the team toward more data-driven decisions on content and distribution — reaching, informing, and inspiring more people with quality, tailored content.

Data Analyst — News

Delivers daily coverage across multiple digital platforms, for a broad audience at various points throughout the day. Here too, we're looking for a Data Analyst to support the team in making more data-driven decisions on content and distribution.

Data Analyst — Streaming

Offers an extensive range of video and audio content. As Data Analyst, you'll use data and insights to drive the platform's tactical and strategic choices across all touchpoints (site, app, social media, …).

What We Expect From You

As Data Analyst, you play a key role between the central Data Intelligence team and the content teams, acting as the go-to person for insights on content and user behaviour across the relevant touchpoints (website, app, social media channels, …).

You know how to translate data analysis into an accessible, actionable format for content teams and the wider organisation, and can support, advise, and guide them toward working with relevant data themselves.

Responsibilities:

  • Analysing user behaviour and content consumption across touchpoints, including social media channels
  • Measuring, analysing, and reporting on the impact of content, marketing, SEO, and CRM initiatives, and proactively proposing optimisations and actionable recommendations
  • Recurring KPI reporting (weekly, monthly, quarterly) and actionable insights to content teams and management
  • Collaborating with other data analysts, UX, and Marketing to optimise the user experience
  • Supporting the execution of A/B tests and implementing improvements
  • Convincing content teams and stakeholders to take data-driven action, and guiding them toward greater autonomy with data (dashboards, reports, segmentation manager)
  • Working closely with other disciplines within the data team and staying up to date on the latest developments in data
  • Willingness to work on-site 3 days per wee

Title: Data-analist Business Intelligence
Description:
Wie zijn wij?

AZ Glorieux en AZ Oudenaarde bevinden zich in een belangrijke strategische transformatie. In het kader van het pre-fusieakkoord bouwen we aan een geïntegreerde, futureproof ziekenhuisgroep waar data, digitalisering en artificiële intelligentie centraal staan.

Voor de verdere uitbouw van onze BI-cel zoeken we een profiel dat ons team versterkt: iemand die sterk is in BI engineering, data-analyse en visualisatie, en tegelijk vlot schakelt met uiteenlopende stakeholders binnen de organisatie.

Ben jij iemand die technische BI-expertise moeiteloos combineert met businessinzicht en sterk stakeholdermanagement? Krijg je energie van het bouwen van betrouwbare datasets, datamodellen en datastromen, maar ook van het scherp krijgen van noden bij interne klanten en het vertalen daarvan naar bruikbare rapportering en inzichten? Dan zoeken wij jou!




Zo ziet je dag eruit

Je komt terecht in een rol met zichtbare impact. Je helpt mee bouwen aan een BI-omgeving die de organisatie sterker maakt: inhoudelijk, technisch en in de samenwerking met de business. Je krijgt de ruimte om mee richting te geven, verbeteringen voor te stellen en echt verschil te maken.

Je bouwt en verbetert datasets, datamodellen en datastromen.
Je analyseert data uit verschillende bronnen en vertaalt die naar bruikbare inzichten.
Je zorgt voor duidelijke structuur en documentatie van definities, datasets en datastromen.
Je bewaakt datakwaliteit, validatie en consistentie tussen systemen en rapportering.
Je maakt data performant en betrouwbaar beschikbaar voor dashboards en rapporten.
Je stemt af met stakeholders, scherpt noden aan en helpt prioriteiten bepalen.
Je begeleidt gebruikers in rapportering, definities en datagedreven werken.
Je houdt overzicht over vragen, wijzigingen, afhankelijkheden en impact.
Je denkt actief mee over hoe BI-werking, intake en samenwerking slimmer en sterker kunnen.




Wie ben jij?

Je behaalde een masterdiploma in een relevant studiegebied (Toegepaste informatica, Bedrijfsmanagement, Statistiek of gelijkaardig) of gelijkwaardig door ervaring.

Je bent technisch sterk, analytisch scherp en weet vlot te schakelen met stakeholders. Je voelt je thuis op het snijvlak van business en data en combineert inhoudelijke expertise met een pragmatische aanpak.

Wat je meebrengt:

bewezen ervaring als BI/Data Analyst in end-to-end trajecten
sterke SQL-kennis
expertise in data warehousing, dimensionele modellering, data-integratie en ETL/ELT
ervaring in een complexe omgeving met meerdere databronnen, afhankelijkheden en uiteenlopende gebruikersnoden
structuur en documentatie van definities, datasets en datastromen
ervaring met Power BI en het bouwen van sterke, gebruiksvriendelijke dashboards
sterk in data-analyse en in het vertalen van businessnoden naar datalogica

Je durft richting geven aan verwachtingen en prioriteiten, en neemt ownership waar nodig. Je werkt graag samen, maar kan ook zelfstandig resultaten neerzetten. Voor jou is data pas echt waardevol als die niet alleen technisch klopt, maar ook begrepen en gedragen wordt binnen de organisatie.

Sterke pluspunten:

ervaring met Microsoft Fabric
ervaring met Azure DevOps en Git
affiniteit met zorg, finance of operationele rapportering




Ons aanbod voor jou

Een rol met zichtbare impact op rapportering, besluitvorming en werking
Een professionele omgeving met maatschappelijke relevantie
Ruimte voor autonomie, initiatief en verdere groei
Samenwerking in een team waar technische expertise en businessnabijheid elkaar versterken
Marktconform salarispakket volgens de officiële ziekenhuisbarema’s met overname van anciënniteit.
Degelijk personeelsstatuut met uitgebreide extralegale voordelen: hospitalisatieverzekering, personeelstarief in het restaurant, gratis parking, tussenkomst in vervoersonkosten, fiets- en/of stapvergoeding, Benefits at Work (kortingen in pretparken, winkels …). Daarnaast geniet je ook van bijkomende verlofdagen.
Jouw welzijn, work-lifebalance en ontplooiing zijn belangrijk voor ons. Zo kan je o.a. gratis zwemmen in Ronse alsook deelnemen aan workshops en lezingen.
We ondersteunen duurzame mobiliteit met ons fietsleasingprogramma.
Een job vlakbij in een filevrije regio.




Vragen?

Wil jij mee bouwen aan een BI-werking die technisch sterk staat én dichtbij de interne klant werkt? Dan maken we graag kennis met jou.

Om te solliciteren, klik je op Solliciteer hier. Voor verdere vragen kan contact opgenomen worden met Laura Kerkhove, HR Recruiter (055 23 36 04).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
ACENSI is looking for a Data Analyst for one of their client based un Brussels :




Context

On top of the development of the grid, asset and system management and market facilitation above mentioned, the TSO in Belgium is also responsible to conduct several security of supply studies (short, medium and long term), vision studies, cost benefit analysis of future grid projects... Those studies are based on economic dispatch tools, which evaluate the need for additional capacity, the economic outputs or the margin available in the system for every hour of the year.




Performing these studies are amongst the main tasks of the team ‘SMA – Scenarios, Market & Adequacy analysis’ department.




This RFP aims to contract one consultant (that will work on site) to support the team in realizing several tasks focused on enhancing the transparency of data published by SMA and developing clear, insightful, and impactful visuals from it.




Our client is therefore looking for a pro-active, “data geek” and “energy geek”. The candidate should have knowledge of data mining, data gathering/processing (mainly in Python), data visualization (PowerBI, React.js…) and ideally an economic dispatch tool such as ANTARES. This assignment also requires a minimum knowledge of energy systems.




Requirements

The consultant will help to perform different tasks within the SMA team :

Improve data management process at SMA (set up data warehouse, data pipelines ;
Explore large amount of data coming out of the simulations ;
Benchmarking visualizations from other equivalent studies
Interactive dashboard), RTE (Analyses et données),...
Benchmarking visualization already available within SMA ;
Create visuals from given simulation output, including writing and updating appropriate scripts ;
Present and iterate upon the created visuals with key stakeholders/management to ensure they fulfill their intended purpose
Create visuals to compare different studies together or with historical data; - Create/Update scripts to identify inconsistencies in input/output data from the simulations ;
Propose user-friendly interface to dynamically explore the data, that could be publicly shared




Service requirements (hard skills and soft skils)

Hard skills

Python (must have) Python is extensively used in existing tools at SMA. Python is a general-purpose programming language whose design philosophy emphasizes code readability. The outcome of the project must be compatible with the existing toolchains
PowerBI (must have) PowerBI is the central tool used at SMA for visualisation. The project should build on the tools/visuals already available within SMA
React.js (nice to have) React.js is used within SMA for specific types of visualization. Knowledge of React would be a plus
ANTARES (mainly the input/output interface but also a general knowledge of economic dispatch tool is a ‘nice to have’) ANTARES is an economic dispatch tool developed by RTE, the French TSO. In the application’s core is an economic dispatch tool for the electricity market. Antares allows to simulate the electricity market on an hourly basis. Any experience with market simulations can be valued here, including other solvers. A minimum knowledge of energy systems is also required. Website: https://antares-simulator.org/




Soft Skills

Medior profile: 3 yrs experience minimum.
The consultant has knowledge of and interest in the energy market;
Ability to work independently and in a team;
The consultant has an analytical and innovative mind




About the profil

You like diving into data and create clear, insightful, and impactful visuals;
Interested to deliver high quality tools to improve the team’s functioning;
You are looking to invest a team on the medium term where you could face challenging projects and grow an expertise

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

We’re Victaulic !

With more than 6,500 employees and 55 international facilities, Victaulic helps customers since 1919 in over 120 countries succeed in the global construction industry. From the tallest buildings to the deepest mines, customers trust our products to increase overall system durability in the most demanding construction projects and operating conditions. Learn more about how our innovative piping products and design services can engineer confidence into your build by visiting our website.

The Role

We are seeking a meticulous and detail-oriented Master Data Analyst to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for creating, maintaining and validating part codes, Bills of Materials (BOM) and product-related data to support Supply Chain, Customer Care, Product Management and Operations. This role is critical in enabling efficient business processes by ensuring reliable and structured master data while supporting product launches, change management and daily operational needs.

What You Will Do


Master Data Management:
Create and maintain part codes, Bills of Materials (BOM), item-language data, cross-references, and aliases in M3.
Process daily part code requests and new product launch requirements accurately and in a timely manner.
Ensure product descriptions, attributes, and master data structures are technically and commercially accurate.


Product Configuration & System Maintenance:
Maintain product structures, drawings, and specifications in M3, in alignment with GDP revisions and updates.
Action Change Control Documents (CCD) related to master data and/or BOM updates, ensuring all required system changes are completed.
Lead M3 implementation activities for product obsolescence and part code deletions.


Data Quality & Governance:
Monitor and review part code and BOM inputs created in M3 for accuracy, completeness, and correct definition.
Identify master data errors, investigate root causes, and coordinate required corrections with relevant data owners or creators.
Measure part code and BOM accuracy against departmental goals and support continuous improvement actions.


Operational Support/ Cross-functional Collaboration:
Support Supply Chain, Customer Care, Manufacturing, Procurement, Planning, Inventory Management, and other departments with master data-related requests.
Notify other part code creators when corrections, updates, or alignment are required.
Provide master data support for Engineered Product requests where new or updated part codes, BOMs, or product structures are required.
Provide guidance to stakeholders on correct master data usage, structure, and system requirements.
Share process knowledge and support consistent execution of master data activities across stakeholders.

About You


Bachelor’s degree in Information Management, Business Administration, Data Science, Supply Chain or a related field.
Experience in master data management, product configuration or a similar data-focused role
Strong knowledge of data governance, data quality, and ERP/data management tools; M3 experience is an advantage.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and attention-to-detail skills.
Strong communication skills with professional proficiency in English; other European languages are an asset.
Ability to work independently while contributing effectively within a cross-functional team environment.


What Working At Victaulic Offers


Attractive remuneration package – including a health insurance, pension plan, meal vouchers and eco vouchers.
Personal growth - Cultivate personal growth through a well-defined career path offering annual progression opportunities. Receive extensive training and guidance.
We focus on well-being by creating a positive team atmosphere where working, learning and caring go hand in hand. We do this by facilitating the organizations of various teambuilding and afterwork activities.
Inspiring global work environment - working with teams across the globe and collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds — both professionally and cultural.

Title: Data Quality Consultant
Description:
Introduction

For one of our fastest growing practices we are looking for a Data Quality Assurance Specialist. 

Do you have 3 to 5 years of experience of which at least 2 to 3 years in the field of data analysis and/or data quality? And the prospect of helping to grow a company that believes in together creating an impact with Data at our customers sounds good? 

Then we are delighted to receive your personal application and we’ll contact you for an interview and a (virtual) cup of coffee.

Your Responsibilities


Support clients in the execution of activities of the data quality cycle: data analysis, data quality assessment, impact assessment, etc. Allowing them to independently monitor, prevent and remediate emerging critical data quality issues.
Evaluate data from data sources to identify and interpret trends. Identify data quality issues, determine business impact, find out the root cause, collaborate to resolve, and make recommendations for long-term solutions.
Gather data from data sources to identify and interpret trends, optimize its efficiency and quality by analyzing and cleansing it, as well as resolving data quality issues.
Run data queries to identify coding issues and data exceptions.
Support clients in their journey towards becoming data-driven by bridging the gap between business and technology and advising them on best practices, tooling, implementation processes, ...
Implement data quality principles, processes, and standards and embed them into the clients’ daily operating rhythm to improve trust in both business and technical data domains.
Collaborate with business and database/application developers to improve processes, system- and database designs.
Follow-up on the latest developments and trends in data quality analysis and tooling.



Your Qualifications


Bachelor’s or master’s degree in a relevant field (engineering, IT, information management, etc.) or equivalent in experience.
2 to 5 years of experience in consulting or service-oriented environments, and at least 2 years of experience in data analysis.
Experience with data quality tooling e.g., Collibra DQ, Soda, Great Expectations, Informatica CDQ, etc..
Knowledge of data landscapes within an organization, understanding of end-to-end data flows (source to consumer), data architecture, and data engineering / -science principles.
Affinity with use cases such as Business Glossary, Data Catalog, Data Lineage or Reporting Governance is a plus.
Proven success in contributing to a team-oriented environment.
Excellent leadership, communication (written and oral) and interpersonal skills.
Fluent in Dutch and/or French and English.



Skillset


Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proficiency in SQL supplemented with at least one other scripting language (e.g., Python, Groovy, R, etc.)
Experience with Data Quality tooling.
Experience with data governance solutions (Collibra, Datahub, Purview, etc.) is a plus.



Your Growth Path

Datashift is a firm believer in growing through it's people. We will guide and support you providing a multitude of opportunities to develop and grow in the direction you want. However we equally believe that it's you who takes ownership of your growth. That's why we give you the tools and the driving seat, it's up to you to do seize the opportunity.

One thing is for sure, surrounded by an entrepreneurial team, many opportunities will present themself to deepen your expertise knowledge or broaden your responsibilities within a fast growing scale-up.

Our offer


A highly entrepreneurial team where initiatives are highly valued and room is given to grow;
A no-nonsense working environment where you will have a direct impact and deliver meaningful results (whilst having a lot of fun);
Excellent terms of employment;
Joining a fast growing company means growing together with it: learn new skills, take a variety of responsibilities and opportunities that continuously present themselves
Fringe benefits including continuous training that builds and extends professional, technical and management skills in all areas;
The opportunity to grow together with Datashift in terms of capabilities as well as financial benefits.



Interested?

Don't hesitate to apply via the link below!

  • Collaborate with business and database/application developers to improve processes, system- and database designs.
  • Follow-up on the latest developments and trends in data quality analysis and tooling.


Your Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s or master’s degree in a relevant field (engineering, IT, information management, etc.) or equivalent in experience.
  • 2 to 5 years of experience in consulting or service-oriented environments, and at least 2 years of experience in data analysis.
  • Experience with data quality tooling e.g., Collibra DQ, Soda, Great Expectations, Informatica CDQ, etc..
  • Knowledge of data landscapes within an organization, understanding of end-to-end data flows (source to consumer), data architecture, and data engineering / -science principles.
  • Affinity with use cases such as Business Glossary, Data Catalog, Data Lineage or Reporting Governance is a plus.
  • Proven success in contributing to a team-oriented environment.
  • Excellent leadership, communication (written and oral) and interpersonal skills.
  • Fluent in Dutch and/or French and English.


Skillset

  • Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Proficiency in SQL supplemented with at least one other scripting language (e.g., Python, Groovy, R, etc.)
  • Experience with Data Quality tooling.
  • Experience with data governance solutions (Collibra, Datahub, Purview, etc.) is a plus.


Your Growth Path

Datashift is a firm believer in growing through it's people. We will guide and support you providing a multitude of opportunities to develop and grow in the direction you want. However we equally believe that it's you who takes ownership of your growth. That's why we give you the tools and the driving seat, it's up to you to do seize the opportunity.

One thing is for sure, surrounded by an entrepreneurial team, many opportunities will present themself to deepen your expertise knowledge or broaden your responsibilities within a fast growing scale-up.

Our offer

  • A highly entrepreneurial team where initiatives are highly valued and room is given to grow;
  • A no-nonsense working environment where you will have a direct impact and deliver meaningful results (whilst having a lot of fun);
  • Excellent terms of employment;
  • Joining a fast growing company means growing together with it: learn new skills, take a variety of responsibilities and opportunities that continuously present themselves
  • Fringe benefits including continuous training that builds and extends professional, technical and management skills in all areas;
  • The opportunity to grow together with Datashift in terms of capabilities as well as financial benefits.


Interested?

Don't hesitate to apply via the link below!

Title: Data Governance & AI Risk Analyst
Description:
EY is a global leader in assurance, tax, transaction, and advisory services, with over 230,000 professionals in more than 150 countries. Our promise is to deliver top-tier services that inspire trust and confidence across capital markets and economies worldwide. At EY, we are dedicated to shape a better working world for our people, our clients, and the wider community.

Shape the future with confidence.

The Opportunity: Your Next Adventure Awaits?

Within our Risk Management Digital team, you will contribute to visible and business-relevant priorities at the intersection of data governance, reporting, digital innovation and AI risk management. As a Data Governance & AI Risk Analyst, you will help improve reporting quality, support smarter and more efficient processes, and contribute to a more data-driven, future-ready Risk Management environment. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated professional who wants to grow, build meaningful expertise and make a tangible impact in a collaborative and evolving setting.

Your Key Responsibilities


Support data governance and data quality initiatives to enhance reporting accuracy, reliability, and data-driven decision-making.
Design, build, and maintain dashboards, KPIs, and reporting insights for management and business stakeholders.
Analyze data quality metrics and identify opportunities to improve reporting reliability, transparency, and process efficiency.
Identify and implement opportunities to streamline manual processes through automation and optimization, leveraging tools such as Power BI, SharePoint, Power Automate, and Copilot-enabled solutions.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand reporting needs and translate them into practical, scalable, and user-friendly data solutions.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of data models, dataflows, semantic layers, and overall reporting architecture.
Support AI and data-driven initiatives, including Copilot use cases, reporting enhancements, and AI governance topics.
Contribute to the design, implementation, and improvement of AI governance and risk management processes, translating compliance requirements into practical workflows, structures, and tools (e.g., SharePoint setups, intake forms, approval flows).


Your Profile


You hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree and have 2 to 6 years of relevant experience in data, reporting, governance, compliance, risk management, digital transformation, or a related field.
You have a strong interest in data governance, reporting, AI, emerging technologies, and digital transformation, and are eager to understand how these translate into practical risk and compliance processes.
You combine strong analytical thinking with a structured, hands-on approach and a keen eye for detail.
You are familiar with reporting and visualization tools, ideally including Power BI, and have some experience with collaboration platforms such as SharePoint.
Knowledge of data modelling, SQL, database structuring, Power Automate, or scripting is a strong asset.
You understand the importance of accuracy, traceability, and compliance when working with sensitive or regulated data.
You are collaborative, curious, and comfortable working across teams in a dynamic environment.
You communicate fluent in English and Dutch or French.


What’s In It For You


EY Family – you become part of the EY Family providing you with all the necessary skills, training and opportunities; enabling you to grow to your fullest potential.
Attractive remuneration package - We offer an attractive remuneration package; including a company car and charging card, iPhone plus subscription, health insurance, pension plan, meal vouchers, and eco vouchers. Thanks to our flex plan it is possible to choose what you value most.
Mobility package - You’ll be able to opt-in for the mobility budget. In return for giving up your (right to a) company car, you’ll receive a monthly budget from EY as compensation. This budget will allow you to use your employer-provided compensation for alternative transportation means or similar benefits, incl. reimbursement of housing costs (on condition you live within 10km of your main place of employment).
Flexible working arrangements - We are proud of our flexible working arrangements. We will actively support you in building a successful career and delivering excellent client service without sacrificing your personal priorities. We offer flexible working hours and working from home multiple days per week is possible.
Inspiring work environment - At EY we are dedicated to providing you with an inspiring work environment. A work environment that allows you to further develop your skills and enables you to fulfill your true potential.
Learning & personal growth - You will benefit from an onboarding program, receive extensive training and will be coached by a counselor. We offer a clear career path tailored to your unique skills with necessary guidance.
Artificial Intelligence – stay up to date with the latest AI trends through our extensive training opportunities and integrated AI systems. You’ll learn how to leverage AI in a smart and efficient way in your day-to-day work, while developing skills to identify, quantify and track value creation when integrating AI into client environments.
Fun - By joining our EY- teams, you can participate in team activities, and companywide events and enjoy a drink during our monthly after-work drink.
Sustainability - We have a fleet of hybrid and electric cars. Our flex plan makes it possible for you to choose the most sustainable option for you. You can also opt for a train subscription or lease a bike.
Diversity & Inclusion - At EY we are passionate about the inclusion and support of individuals of all groups; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, or disability status.
Location - All of our offices are easily accessible by public transport and/or by car and have parking space. They all have parking spaces and charging stations are provided.


Here at EY, you’ll have the chance to build a truly exceptional experience. We’ll empower you with the latest technology, surround you with high-performing teams, and provide the global scale and diverse and inclusive culture you need to discover your full potential. Through our coaching and training programs, you’ll develop the skillsets you need to stay relevant today and in the future – all while building a network of colleagues, mentors, and leaders who will be on the journey with you at EY and beyond.

Title: Performance Data Analyst
Description:
Ben jij een analytische professional die data niet alleen begrijpt, maar ook gebruikt om concrete businessimpact te realiseren? Wil je marketinginspanningen rechtstreeks koppelen aan salesresultaten en zo mee bouwen aan een datagedreven organisatie? Dan is dit jouw volgende stap.




Binnen BMW Financial Services en Alphabet krijg je de kans om een sleutelrol te spelen in het versterken van onze commerciële slagkracht. In deze functie verbind je marketing, sales en data tot één krachtig geheel.




Als Marketing Data Analyst zorg jij ervoor dat data richting geeft aan beslissingen. Je maakt prestaties inzichtelijk, legt verbanden bloot en vertaalt cijfers naar concrete acties. Je werkt nauw samen met marketing- en salesteams en tilt hun resultaten naar een hoger niveau.

Je bent niet alleen bezig met analyse, maar neemt ook ownership over de manier waarop data wordt ingezet en benut binnen de organisatie.




Wat doe je als Marketing Data Analyst?




Ontwerpen, bouwen en continu optimaliseren van Marketing & Sales dashboards (Tableau, Adobe Analytics, Salesforce) met een sterke focus op KPI’s, ROI en commerciële impact. Integreren van diverse databronnen om een helder, end-to-end beeld van de customer journey en funnelperformantie te creëren.
Versterken van de datafundamenten door het beheren en verder uitbouwen van de marketing data-architectuur en het datawarehouse. Actief bewaken en verbeteren van datakwaliteit via gestandaardiseerde processen, controles en datamodellen.
Analyseren en verbeteren van campagneperformantie door diepgaande data-analyse en het optimaliseren van tracking & tagging. Nauw samenwerken met marketingteams en externe partners om de meetbaarheid en effectiviteit van campagnes structureel te verhogen.
Ondersteunen van het B2C Sales team met datagedreven inzichten via gerichte, performante rapportering die directe input levert voor commerciële sturing en besluitvorming.
Vertalen van complexe data naar heldere, actiegerichte inzichten en concrete aanbevelingen die leiden tot meetbare businessimpact en betere beslissingen op alle niveaus.




Waarmee kan jij ons team versterken?




Bachelor of master in een relevante richting, of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
Minstens 3 jaar ervaring in een data-driven rol (bv. Data Analyst, Marketing Intelligence Analyst)
Sterk in Excel en vertrouwd met tools zoals Tableau, Adobe Analytics, Salesforce of vergelijkbare platformen
Analytisch scherp, gestructureerd en gefocust op impact en resultaat
Combineert strategisch denken met een hands-on, pragmatische aanpak
Communiceert helder, onderbouwd en overtuigend — zowel schriftelijk als mondeling
Affiniteit met innovatie, data en AI is een duidelijke plus
Vloeiend in Nederlands en Engels
Proactieve teamplayer met eigenaarschap en verantwoordelijkheidszin




Waarom kiezen voor BMW Group Belux?

Bij BMW Financial Services werk je in een omgeving die premium uitstraalt, van onze producten tot onze mensen. Je krijgt de kans om impact te maken binnen een internationale organisatie met een sterke focus op innovatie en duurzaamheid.




Wij bieden jou:

Een internationale werkomgeving met een duidelijke visie op de toekomst
Een warme en dynamische bedrijfscultuur waar initiatief en groei centraal staan
Ruime opleidings- en ontwikkelingsmogelijkheden
Een competitief salarispakket met mobiliteitsoplossingen en een flexibel cafetariaplan




Wil jij mee bouwen aan een datagedreven toekomst en een zichtbare impact maken op marketing en sales? Solliciteer vandaag en ontdek jouw mogelijkheden binnen BMW Group Belux!

Title: Data Full Stack
Description:
Health can’t wait.

Not for symptoms to get worse. Not for a six‑month appointment. Not for a system to catch up. But that’s exactly how healthcare works today. You wait, until you can’t.

Alan exists to end the wait.

Health is a universal right, and we believe this right can only become real when it’s coupled with prevention. We need to stop treating health as something we repair and start treating it as something we build, every day. It’s not solely a question of willpower. It’s the healthcare system itself that needs to work for everyone, in a sustainable way.

So we are building the new standard in prevention insurance. Alan is the first company that integrates insurance, prevention, and care into a single, acclaimed user experience.

We are on an incredible journey to build a global leading company, with a unique culture. We already partner with 40K+ companies of all sizes, serving more than 1M+ members, and have reached €800M+ in ARR.

Prevention as the new norm. That's what we're building with our team of 800+ people. If it speaks to you: we're hiring across France, Spain, Belgium, and Canada. And beyond.

⭐️ The Data Team


Being part of Alan's data team means you'll have a positive impact on people's health and wellbeing.
We create insights to help our colleagues focus on what is most impactful.
We build features and tools powered by data that directly help our members.
Examples include:
going deep into health consumption patterns,
understanding our product usage,
creating visibility to help our sales team perform,
automating how we reimburse our members,
building products that help our members stay healthy...

We also believe in having a full stack approach:


Each one of us contributes to all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science). This encourages strong ownership and drives impact.
We own our infrastructure. We pride ourselves on a nimble data stack that gives us the flexibility we need. We're mainly using SQL, Python, R, Airflow, Snowflake, and Metabase.
We build in-house tooling to organize our data and serve it to the rest of the company.


🎯 Expected Skills And Abilities



You would thrive at Alan if most of this sounds like you:


Passionate about owning ambitious problems and developing simple solutions for them
Demonstrated impact during a first successful experience.
Experience or willingness to grow in all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science) -- being specialized in one of these areas is good too!
Proficient in SQL, Python and/or R- Setting a high standard of rigor and precision for yourself, interested in your craft
Great listening and communication skills
Humble, kind, collaborative, and still willing to grow (no matter your seniority!)
Fluent and clear in English, both oral and written (no need to speak French!)


For this role we are looking to hire within the C1-E level range.

⚙️ Important note: we hire people, not roles.

If you're excited about this opportunity but don't check every box, we'd love to hear from you. Everyone, no matter how underrepresented, should feel free to apply, as it can only bring learnings or success.

If you identify yourself as a woman: Did you know that research shows women often apply only when meeting 100% of requirements?

Remember, this is just a guide, not a checklist.

We'll be thrilled to receive your application!

🔖 Check out our About Alan and Career pages, as well as our Medium, blog and Glassdoor page for more info.

You want to know more about Alan?


🙌 Perks & Benefits: Alaners are provided with a stimulating environment and perks ensuring they are happy, efficient and spend only high-quality time with co-workers.
🤘 A strong culture: People joining Alan are often surprised and delighted by our innovative working method. We have a set of cultural values that guide our approach to work


🔄 A quick note about the process

Note: While you're applying for a specific area, you may join a different engineering team based on where we think you'll have the most impact. Check out descriptions of other areas here.

🔄 A quick note about the process

Note: While you're applying for a specific area, you may join a different engineering team based on where we think you'll have the most impact. Check out descriptions of other areas here.

Title: Data Platform Analyst (MS Fabric) - Flanders Digital
Description:
Cegeka’s afdeling Flanders Digital bouwt samen met de Vlaamse Overheid aan moderne dataplatformen op basis van o.a. Microsoft Fabric. Deze platformen vormen de basis voor datagedreven beleid rond thema’s zoals wonen en energie. We kijken momenteel uit naar een Data Platform Analyst die ervoor zal zorgen dat deze platformen effectief waarde opleveren voor de organisatie. Je werkt op het snijvlak van gebruikers, beleid en data engineering in een productgedreven datateam.


Als brug tussen business en technologie ben je verantwoordelijk voor het vertalen van businessvragen naar concrete oplossingen op het dataplatform, en waak je erover dat deze oplossingen effectief gebruikt worden en waarde leveren.
Je analyseert vragen van business en beleidsgebruikers en je vertaalt deze noden naar duidelijke requirements voor het dataplatformteam.
Je werkt nauw samen met data engineers en BI profielen binnen een Microsoft Fabric omgeving.
Je valideert of de opgeleverde oplossingen correct en bruikbaar zijn (testing, acceptatie).
Je communiceert resultaten en inzichten naar stakeholders en je begeleidt hen in het gebruik.
Je detecteert proactief opportuniteiten waar data extra waarde kan creëren.
Je krijgt de ruimte om ownership te nemen over use cases, stakeholders mee te nemen en impact te realiseren op schaal van Vlaanderen.


Wat breng jij mee?


Je hebt ervaring in een rol op het snijvlak van business en data (functioneel analist, data analyst, product analyst, …).
Je kan businessvragen structureren en vertalen naar technische requirements.
Je begrijpt hoe dataplatformen werken (data pipelines, modellering, rapportering).
Je hebt ervaring met SQL en/ of data visualisatie (Power BI).
Je bent bekend met testing en validatie van data-oplossingen.
Je bent sterk in communicatie en stakeholder management.
Ervaring met Microsoft Fabric, Azure of gelijkaardige platformen (bv. Databricks, Synapse, Snowflake, Cloudera) is een pluspunt.
Deed je al ervaring op in een overheidscontext of werkte je al met beleidsdata? Ook dat is voor deze functie een pluspunt.
Je werkt 1 dag/ week vanuit het Cegeka-kantoor in Leuven, en 1 dag/ week ben je onsite bij de klant in Brussel. Op de overige dagen is er de mogelijkheid tot thuiswerk.


Wat hebben wij jou te bieden?


Je komt terecht bij een echte Top Employer
Cegeka bewijst jaar na jaar in het Whitelane Research dat we een betrouwbare en toonaangevende partner zijn op vlak van IT-dienstverlening, met een eerste plaats in 2026 voor Security Services (vierde jaar op rij), een gedeelde tweede plaats voor Cloud & Infrastructure Services en Network & Connectivity
We vragen heel wat (talenten) van jou. Gelukkig krijg je er ook heel wat voor terug! Competenties en karakter zijn voor ons essentieel, naast ervaring en ambities. Cegeka investeert voortdurend in talent management opdat alle medewerkers hun potentieel kunnen realiseren
Wij hanteren een framework met het oog op eerlijke verloning, waarbij medewerkers worden verloond op basis van hun functie, ervaring en prestaties, onafhankelijk van gender of andere niet-werkgerelateerde kenmerken. Een elektrische wagen met laadpas of een mobiliteitsbudget, maaltijdcheques, ecocheques, een interessante groeps- en hospitalisatieverzekering, een gsm-abonnement, een forfaitaire onkostenvergoeding, een krachtige laptop en de mogelijkheid om je salarispakket een eigen accent te geven dankzij ons Flex Reward Plan
Flexibele werkuren én een mooie work/life balans
Een top team: waarin nieuwe frisse ideeën en initiatieven steeds welkom zijn! Team-events, happy hours en een steeds legendarisch bedrijfsfeest horen daar ook bij
Continu bijleren: knowledge sharing meetings, deelname aan conferenties en externe opleidingen. Kennisdeling en bijleren staan centraal! Afhankelijk van je eigen interesses en initiatieven, biedt Cegeka verschillende horizontale en verticale carrièrepaden
We hechten waarde aan duurzaamheid door CO2-neutraliteit na te streven met de elektrificatie van ons wagenpark en een milieuvriendelijk datacenter. We ontwikkelen innovatieve oplossingen met een positieve impact op milieu, mens en maatschappij, en streven naar een inclusieve en diverse werkomgeving waarin iedereen gewaardeerd en gehoord wordt.


Onze voordelen

Marktconform salaris met aantrekkelijke extralegale voordelen.

Continuous learning via hackathons, leesgroepen, events,...

Flexibele werktijden voor een betere balans tussen werk en privé.

Carrièregroei met horizontale en verticale ontwikkelpaden.

Hybride werkmodel dat efficiënt kantoor- en thuiswerk combineert.

Persoonlijke begeleiding door managers, coaches en sparringpartners.

ESG: lagere emissies, bevorderen van inclusie en duurzaamheid in IT.

Cegeka draagt met trots het Top Employer-certificaat.

Teamevenementen, happy hours, sport en grandioos bedrijfsfeest.

Title: Data Analyst Confirmé (H/F)
Description:
Description Du Poste

Vous serez intégré(e) dans une équipe de consultants innovants et motivés, composée de Business Analysts, Data Scientists et Data Engineers spécialisés sur les problématiques Data Analytics. À ce titre, vous interviendrez de façon opérationnelle sur des missions de conseil autour des sujets de transformation Data Analytics, de retraitement et modélisation de données et de représentation visuelle de la donnée pour adresser des problématiques métiers.

Vous serez notamment en charge de :


Comprendre les problématiques métier,
Collecter et traiter les données en vue de l'analyse, incluant notamment la création de variables agrégées et complémentaires aux données brutes,
Automatiser et d’industrialiser les retraitements effectués sur les données,
Représenter les résultats dans un outil de restitution visuelle de façon ergonomique et intuitive,
Restituer les analyses au client notamment pas la création d’indicateurs pertinents pour les questions stratégiques des entreprises


PROFIL RECHERCHE

Diplômé d'une école d'ingénieurs ou équivalent (Bac +5), vous justifiez d’a minima 2 ans d’expérience réussis en tant que data analyst :


Vous possédez une bonne connaissance des outils et méthodologies de Data Analytics, incluant notamment des bases techniques (modèles de données, langage SQL, …) mais également méthodologiques,
Vous possédez une connaissance approfondie d'Excel et maitrisez les langages statistiques tels que R, Python ou SAS,
Une connaissance d'un logiciel ou langage dédié à la Data Visualization (Tableau, Qliksense ou Qlickview, Microsoft Power BI…) ainsi que des compétences en automatisation (VBA par exemple) seraient un plus.


PROCESSUS DE RECRUTEMENT


Échangez avec un ou une chargée de recrutement pour un premier contact téléphonique
Rencontrez un ou une Ingénieure d’Affaires (Business Manager) sur vos souhaits d’évolution
Approfondissez avec nos responsables et directeurs d’agence pour connaître la vision de l'agence


Selon votre profil, des tests ou entretiens techniques pourront vous être demandés. Tout au long du processus, vous pourrez échanger avec nos consultants pour en savoir + sur leur quotidien chez nous !

PACKAGE SALARIAL

Chez Extia Belgique IT, la transparence fait partie intégrante de l'expérience positive, voici un aperçu des éléments de package salarial dont peuvent profiter nos collaborateurs, sous réserve d'éligibilité

👛 Voiture de société ou budget mobilité ✨ Chèques repas & Ecocheque 🎁 Assurance groupe 🎉 Frais de représentation 📈 Plan cafétéria / Flex Income Plan 🚀 Primes d'apport d'affaire, de cooptation et de formation 🌍 20 jours de congés légaux + 12 jours de récupération

Ces informations sont communiquées à titre indicatif et seront abordées plus en détails lors du processus de recrutement.

LES PETITS PLUS

🎉 250 événements par an pour rencontrer tes collègues et des locaux feel good 📢 Des formations, conférences et une plateforme d'e-learning avec LinkedIn pour monter en compétences #greatplacetolearn 👩‍ Des Communautés Métiers et du Mentorat pour partager son expertise et en apprendre sur de nombreux sujets #greatplacetoshare 🕹️ Une communauté de e-sport : Extia Gaming ! #greatplacetoplay 🌏 Envie d’évoluer ? Grâce à notre politique de mobilité interne, explorez de nouvelles opportunités en Belgique ou dans l’un de nos 7 autres pays !

Title: Data Analyst Job ID: JP054453
Description:
About the job Data Analyst Job ID: JP054453Data Analyst Overview

We are looking for a Confirmed Data Analyst to join an Agile team working on a large-scale agricultural accounting and data management platform. The role focuses on business analysis, data modeling, reporting, process optimization, and functional specification design to support decision-making, regulatory compliance, and operational efficiency.

Key Responsibilities


Collaborate with business stakeholders and analysts to gather and analyze requirements.
Identify, model, and document business data and processes.
Produce functional specifications and detailed business requirements.
Create UML and BPMN models to support system design and process improvement.
Design and develop reports, dashboards, and data visualizations.
Analyze accounting and operational data to support business insights.
Define and document data exchange requirements with external systems and web services.
Participate in testing, quality assurance, and validation activities.
Produce technical and functional documentation.
Contribute to Agile ceremonies and continuous improvement initiatives.


Required Skills & Experience


Proven experience in business and data analysis.
Strong knowledge of UML (especially class diagrams) and BPMN process modeling.
Experience gathering requirements and translating them into functional specifications.
Experience generating reports, dashboards, and data visualizations.
Knowledge of accounting or analytical accounting principles.
Experience consuming and integrating web services and data exchange interfaces.
Familiarity with Agile and Scrum methodologies.
Experience with Jira and Jira Xray.
Knowledge of testing and quality assurance practices.
Strong documentation and specification writing skills.


Soft Skills


Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Autonomous and proactive mindset.
Pragmatic and results-oriented approach.
Effective collaboration within multidisciplinary Agile teams.


Languages


French: Professional proficiency (C2).


Location


Namur, Belgium.
Hybrid working model with a minimum onsite presence of 50%.


Nice to Have


Experience within public sector organizations.
Knowledge of software architecture and scalable information systems.
Experience working with accounting, financial, or regulatory reporting solutions.
Experience supporting data migration and digital transformation initiatives.

Title: Revenue Analytics Engineer
Description:
Context & Impact

As Lansweeper scales its SaaS business across new products and markets, revenue analytics has become a critical function for steering executive and board-level decisions. This role exists to bring structure, trust, and clarity to how Lansweeper measures and understands its revenue.

You will be the dedicated Revenue Analytics Engineer within a 10-person Data & Analytics team — the first hire focused entirely on revenue — reporting directly to the Data Platform Manager. Your work will directly shape how C-level leaders and the board understand growth, and how sales and finance teams plan and act.

In the near term, your goals are to establish a single source of truth for revenue KPIs, build board-ready reporting, and deliver forecasting models that drive go-to-market decisions.

Challenge

The Main Challenges You'll Face Are


Speed vs. complexity: The sales organization moves fast and needs reliable revenue facts quickly — but revenue data is sourced from multiple systems (CRM, billing, ERP) with historic complexity from acquisitions and system migrations, and data quality that varies over time.
Keeping up with a changing business:** As Lansweeper's product and market evolve, sales and marketing allocation must follow. You'll need to continuously adapt revenue models to reflect new business processes and priorities.
Being the first and only:** You are the sole analytics engineer focused on revenue. You'll need to build foundations from scratch, establish trust in the numbers, and earn your seat at the table with finance and executive stakeholders — all at the same time.


Key Responsibilities


Build, maintain, and improve revenue data models** that power executive-level reporting and board-ready metrics across ARR, MRR, churn, expansion, contraction, and renewal cycles
Reconcile revenue data across systems of record** — CRM, billing, and ERP — and establish a single, trusted source of truth for financial KPIs
Design and deliver dashboards and reports** that translate complex revenue data into clear, actionable insights for sales, finance, and leadership
Partner with sales operations and finance** to understand evolving business processes and reflect them accurately in revenue analytics models
Develop and maintain forecasting models** for key revenue metrics to support go-to-market planning and strategic decision-making
Investigate and explain trends, anomalies, and shifts** in revenue data, linking them back to underlying sales and finance drivers
Proactively improve data quality and integrity** across revenue-related data pipelines to ensure consistent, reliable reporting


Key Requirements

Hard Skills


Financial metrics reporting — solid understanding of how revenue, bookings, churn, ARR, MRR, expansion, contraction, and renewal KPIs are defined and measured in a SaaS context
Strong SQL skills — ability to write, optimize, and debug complex queries against large datasets
Systems reconciliation — hands-on experience resolving data mismatches between CRM, billing, and finance systems to produce a reliable single source of truth
Data modelling — familiarity with best practices such as dimensional modelling and slowly changing dimensions; experience with Snowflake and dbt is a strong plus
Dashboard and reporting delivery— experience building clear, business-facing reports; experience with **Power BI** is a strong plus


Soft Skills


Analytical storytelling — you translate data into business narratives that support decisions, not just numbers on a screen
Stakeholder collaboration— you work confidently with finance, sales ops, and executive audiences, adapting your communication to each
Ownership mindset — you take initiative, work independently, and drive outcomes without waiting to be directed


AI-Specific Skills

Lansweeper is an AI-native company. In this role, AI fluency is a hard requirement — not a nice-to-have.

You are expected to use AI tooling as a standard part of your day-to-day workflow: this includes AI-assisted SQL generation and debugging, using AI to accelerate data modelling and anomaly investigation, and leveraging AI features within your analytics stack (such as Snowflake Cortex or dbt-integrated tooling). You should be comfortable prompting AI tools effectively, evaluating their outputs critically, and integrating AI into repeatable workflows — from data pipeline development to generating draft narrative summaries for executive reporting. General AI fluency across platforms such as ChatGPT or Copilot is expected.

Team Info

You'll join the Insights & Intelligence team, part of the broader Data & Analytics function at Lansweeper. The team has 10 people and is led by the **Data Platform Manager**, who you will report to directly. You will work closely with sales operations, finance, and data engineers across the organization.

As the **only analytics engineer focused on revenue**, you will own this domain end-to-end — a rare opportunity to define how a growing SaaS company understands and steers its revenue.

The team is based across Ghent, Belgium and Alicante, Spain, with a hybrid working model at both locations.

Our Offer

Salary

See Pay Transparency section below.

Benefits — Belgium


Meal vouchers
Eco vouchers
Hospitalisation insurance
Group insurance
Company car or mobility budget
Phone plan
Internet allowance


Benefits — Spain


Health insurance
Meal allowance
Flexible remuneration plan


What Else You Get


A high-impact, high-autonomy role at the intersection of data, finance, and go-to-market strategy
Hybrid working from either **Ghent, Belgium or Alicante, Spain** — flexibility built in by design
A pragmatic, collaborative data team that values ownership and real-world impact over process for process's sake
The opportunity to **build the revenue analytics function from the ground up** in a scaling SaaS company
A front-row seat to C-level and board decision-making, with your work directly influencing strategic direction


About Lansweeper

Lansweeper is the AI Cyber Asset Intelligence platform helping IT and Security teams gain full visibility, reduce cyber risk, and scale automation with confidence.

In today's complex IT, OT, cloud, and IoT environments, fragmented asset data slows decisions and increases risk. We transform raw asset data into a continuously validated, trusted source of truth — so teams can move faster and act with certainty.

With Lansweeper, Organizations Can

See – Truly complete visibility across hybrid environments

Know– Enriched asset intelligence with lifecycle and risk context

Act – Automate workflows, coordinate remediation, and enforce policy at scale

From universal asset discovery to AI-powered intelligence, we provide the shared foundation modern IT Operations, Cybersecurity, and Digital Transformation teams rely on.

Our Culture — Built On Four Core Values


One Team – United across boundaries
We Care – Customers and people at the centre
We Grow – Learning, sharing, improving
We Deliver – Focusing on what truly matters


Pay Transparency

At Lansweeper, every role is assigned to a level defined by three things: the expertise it requires, the complexity it involves, and the scope of impact it carries.

Each level maps to a salary band based on salary benchmarks from the Software/SaaS industry of your country, so your pay reflects what the market pays for comparable roles.

The gross salary range for this role is €107,000 – €174,000 (EUR) (Belgium) or €75,000 – €123,000 (EUR) (Spain) (based on full-time employment).

Where you land within that range depends on the expertise you bring, the complexity you have navigated, the scope of impact you have owned, and your track record of delivering results in comparable roles — the same criteria applied consistently to every candidate.

We welcome applicants of all backgrounds, regardless of gender, religion, ethnic origin, age, sexual orientation, or disability.

How AI Is Used During The Hiring Process

HTML:
Context & Impact

As Lansweeper scales its SaaS business across new products and markets, revenue analytics has become a critical function for steering executive and board-level decisions. This role exists to bring structure, trust, and clarity to how Lansweeper measures and understands its revenue.

You will be the dedicated Revenue Analytics Engineer within a 10-person Data & Analytics team — the first hire focused entirely on revenue — reporting directly to the Data Platform Manager. Your work will directly shape how C-level leaders and the board understand growth, and how sales and finance teams plan and act.

In the near term, your goals are to establish a single source of truth for revenue KPIs, build board-ready reporting, and deliver forecasting models that drive go-to-market decisions.

Challenge

The Main Challenges You'll Face Are

  • Speed vs. complexity: The sales organization moves fast and needs reliable revenue facts quickly — but revenue data is sourced from multiple systems (CRM, billing, ERP) with historic complexity from acquisitions and system migrations, and data quality that varies over time.
  • Keeping up with a changing business:** As Lansweeper's product and market evolve, sales and marketing allocation must follow. You'll need to continuously adapt revenue models to reflect new business processes and priorities.
  • Being the first and only:** You are the sole analytics engineer focused on revenue. You'll need to build foundations from scratch, establish trust in the numbers, and earn your seat at the table with finance and executive stakeholders — all at the same time.

Key Responsibilities

  • Build, maintain, and improve revenue data models** that power executive-level reporting and board-ready metrics across ARR, MRR, churn, expansion, contraction, and renewal cycles
  • Reconcile revenue data across systems of record** — CRM, billing, and ERP — and establish a single, trusted source of truth for financial KPIs
  • Design and deliver dashboards and reports** that translate complex revenue data into clear, actionable insights for sales, finance, and leadership
  • Partner with sales operations and finance** to understand evolving business processes and reflect them accurately in revenue analytics models
  • Develop and maintain forecasting models** for key revenue metrics to support go-to-market planning and strategic decision-making
  • Investigate and explain trends, anomalies, and shifts** in revenue data, linking them back to underlying sales and finance drivers
  • Proactively improve data quality and integrity** across revenue-related data pipelines to ensure consistent, reliable reporting

Key Requirements

Hard Skills

  • Financial metrics reporting — solid understanding of how revenue, bookings, churn, ARR, MRR, expansion, contraction, and renewal KPIs are defined and measured in a SaaS context
  • Strong SQL skills — ability to write, optimize, and debug complex queries against large datasets
  • Systems reconciliation — hands-on experience resolving data mismatches between CRM, billing, and finance systems to produce a reliable single source of truth
  • Data modelling — familiarity with best practices such as dimensional modelling and slowly changing dimensions; experience with Snowflake and dbt is a strong plus
  • Dashboard and reporting delivery— experience building clear, business-facing reports; experience with **Power BI** is a strong plus

Soft Skills

  • Analytical storytelling — you translate data into business narratives that support decisions, not just numbers on a screen
  • Stakeholder collaboration— you work confidently with finance, sales ops, and executive audiences, adapting your communication to each
  • Ownership mindset — you take initiative, work independently, and drive outcomes without waiting to be directed

AI-Specific Skills

Lansweeper is an AI-native company. In this role, AI fluency is a hard requirement — not a nice-to-have.

You are expected to use AI tooling as a standard part of your day-to-day workflow: this includes AI-assisted SQL generation and debugging, using AI to accelerate data modelling and anomaly investigation, and leveraging AI features within your analytics stack (such as Snowflake Cortex or dbt-integrated tooling). You should be comfortable prompting AI tools effectively, evaluating their outputs critically, and integrating AI into repeatable workflows — from data pipeline development to generating draft narrative summaries for executive reporting. General AI fluency across platforms such as ChatGPT or Copilot is expected.

Team Info

You'll join the Insights & Intelligence team, part of the broader Data & Analytics function at Lansweeper. The team has 10 people and is led by the **Data Platform Manager**, who you will report to directly. You will work closely with sales operations, finance, and data engineers across the organization.

As the **only analytics engineer focused on revenue**, you will own this domain end-to-end — a rare opportunity to define how a growing SaaS company understands and steers its revenue.

The team is based across Ghent, Belgium and Alicante, Spain, with a hybrid working model at both locations.

Our Offer

Salary

See Pay Transparency section below.

Benefits — Belgium

  • Meal vouchers
  • Eco vouchers
  • Hospitalisation insurance
  • Group insurance
  • Company car or mobility budget
  • Phone plan
  • Internet allowance

Benefits — Spain

  • Health insurance
  • Meal allowance
  • Flexible remuneration plan

What Else You Get

  • A high-impact, high-autonomy role at the intersection of data, finance, and go-to-market strategy
  • Hybrid working from either **Ghent, Belgium or Alicante, Spain** — flexibility built in by design
  • A pragmatic, collaborative data team that values ownership and real-world impact over process for process's sake
  • The opportunity to **build the revenue analytics function from the ground up** in a scaling SaaS company
  • A front-row seat to C-level and board decision-making, with your work directly influencing strategic direction

About Lansweeper

Lansweeper is the AI Cyber Asset Intelligence platform helping IT and Security teams gain full visibility, reduce cyber risk, and scale automation with confidence.

In today's complex IT, OT, cloud, and IoT environments, fragmented asset data slows decisions and increases risk. We transform raw asset data into a continuously validated, trusted source of truth — so teams can move faster and act with certainty.

With Lansweeper, Organizations Can

See – Truly complete visibility across hybrid environments

Know– Enriched asset intelligence with lifecycle and risk context

Act – Automate workflows, coordinate remediation, and enforce policy at scale

From universal asset discovery to AI-powered intelligence, we provide the shared foundation modern IT Operations, Cybersecurity, and Digital Transformation teams rely on.

Our Culture — Built On Four Core Values

  • One Team – United across boundaries
  • We Care – Customers and people at the centre
  • We Grow – Learning, sharing, improving
  • We Deliver – Focusing on what truly matters

Pay Transparency

At Lansweeper, every role is assigned to a level defined by three things: the expertise it requires, the complexity it involves, and the scope of impact it carries.

Each level maps to a salary band based on salary benchmarks from the Software/SaaS industry of your country, so your pay reflects what the market pays for comparable roles.

The gross salary range for this role is €107,000 – €174,000 (EUR) (Belgium) or €75,000 – €123,000 (EUR) (Spain) (based on full-time employment).

Where you land within that range depends on the expertise you bring, the complexity you have navigated, the scope of impact you have owned, and your track record of delivering results in comparable roles — the same criteria applied consistently to every candidate.

We welcome applicants of all backgrounds, regardless of gender, religion, ethnic origin, age, sexual orientation, or disability.

Title: Data Analyst Consultant
Description:
Morrison Finance is a European-based leading consultancy company. We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst Consultant to work within the Program Management Life Department of one of our blue chip client in insurance industry. This team is crucial for managing projects and tools that enhance the efficiency of operational activities. A key challenge they face is effectively managing and representing increasing volumes of data.

The cconsultant will support a newly launched workflow tool that requires expertise in Power BI to gather and visualize the necessary information for Operations teams. The role will involve several upcoming projects where data collection, analysis, and the presentation of insights will be essential for maintaining focus and driving performance.

Collaboration with Operations departments and IT colleagues is vital, as reconciling diverse interests will be a significant aspect of the role. We are looking for a Data Analyst and Power BI expert who will translate operational data into analytical models, elevate the data skills of team members, and play a pivotal role in conceptualizing data architecture.




Key Responsibilities:

Collaborate with internal stakeholders to understand data needs and provide tailored solutions.
Gather, analyze, and interpret complex data sets to deliver actionable insights.
Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports to visualize data and support informed decision-making.
Ensure data quality and integrity through regular verifications and validation processes.
Propose and implement initiatives to enhance awareness of data analytics within the department.
Identify and implement analytics models based on operational data.
Support and mentor one or two colleagues in the Process & Organisation team.
Assist in establishing the conceptual architecture for data management.
Apply AI solutions to improve the creation of sophisticated and insightful Power BI dashboards.




Required Knowledge and Experience




Personal Skills (Soft Skills):

Structured, conscientious, and flexible
Hands-on approach
Excellent communication skills
Team player
Critical and analytical thinking
Ability to work independently and meet tight deadlines
Strong documentation skills
Awareness of technological trends in the BI field




Business Experience Required :

Senior profile with concrete experience in data analysis and Power BI
Deep understanding of data analytics and visualization techniques
Experience in the insurance or financial industry




Technical Experience Required:

Proficiency in MS Office 365
Mastery of Power BI, including advanced features like Power Query and DAX
Data analysis and modeling skills
Expertise in data visualization techniques

Preferable:

Knowledge of Python
Familiarity with Copilot M365
Experience with time series modeling (e.g., ARIMA) in Power BI
Understanding of Azure Cognitive Services




Level of Education

Master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or Business), with an emphasis on data analysis.




Language Requirement

Native-level proficiency in Dutch and French, with strong written and verbal communication skills.
Fluent in English




What We Offer:

Being part of a dynamic, energetic, and opportunity-filled company
Gaining valuable professional experience within a fast-growing company
Continuous learning, with extensive training in both technical and soft skills
A workplace where everyone feels respected, valued, and free to be themselves
Inspiring and supportive colleagues
Forward-looking, highly accessible leadership
A flexible work schedule
An attractive salary and benefits package
