Title: Power BI Specialist / Data Analyst – GJA 16
Description:
Role Overview

As a Power BI Specialist / Functional Data Analyst at ING, you will play a key role in turning data made available to you into clear, reliable and actionable business insights. Your work will help teams, management and front-office stakeholders make faster, smarter and better-informed decisions.

In This Role, You Will


Translate business questions into relevant views, datasets and data products that make insights easy to access and use.
Maintain, design and optimize Power BI data models to deliver high-performing, scalable and trusted reporting solutions for management and front-office teams.
Develop, automate and enhance Power BI dashboards, ensuring that available data is transformed into meaningful insights that support performance steering, prioritization and decision-making.
Create compelling visualizations and narratives that translate complex data into clear, intuitive and business-relevant stories.


Key Responsibilities

Data Modeling and Collaboration:


Partner with business stakeholders to understand their objectives, clarify problem statements and define how available data should be transformed, enriched and presented for maximum impact.
Design and optimize Power BI data models that are robust, performant and intuitive, enabling advanced features such as hierarchical filtering, drill-down analysis and multi-level organizational structures.


Reporting And Automation


Contribute to the development of a Power BI Center of Expertise by promoting best practices in dashboard design, data modeling, performance optimization and governance.
Develop and automate the underlying processes required for recurring reports, including weekly, monthly, quarterly and annual reporting cycles.
Build interactive dashboards and reports that deliver actionable insights, highlight key trends and enable users to explore the data independently.


As a Power BI Specialist / Functional Data Analyst, you should have the following qualifications and competences:

Qualifications


Master’s degree in Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, Information Management or alike, and/or equivalent experience of 5 years.
Minimum 5 years relevant experience in data analytics
Experienced in SQL.
Strong expertise in Power BI data modeling, advanced DAX, report performance optimization and dashboard development.
Advanced experience in developing, automating and maintaining reporting solutions using Power BI.
Experience with tools such as SAS is a plus
Experience in analytical work with large datasets and relational databases.


Core Competencies


To be customer driven and focused, loving to collaborate with different people.
An entrepreneurial attitude – taking initiative and making things happen.
Be analytical and insight-driven: curious about banking and business performance, able to identify patterns in data and translate them into concise, relevant conclusions.
Open to learn other dashboarding solutions
Banking knowledge is a plus
Be able to influence and convince peers and stakeholders by clearly explaining analytical results, business implications and recommended actions.
Fluent in English and Dutch or French

Title: Data Full Stack
Description:
Health can’t wait.

Not for symptoms to get worse. Not for a six‑month appointment. Not for a system to catch up. But that’s exactly how healthcare works today. You wait, until you can’t.

Alan exists to end the wait.

Health is a universal right, and we believe this right can only become real when it’s coupled with prevention. We need to stop treating health as something we repair and start treating it as something we build, every day. It’s not solely a question of willpower. It’s the healthcare system itself that needs to work for everyone, in a sustainable way.

So we are building the new standard in prevention insurance. Alan is the first company that integrates insurance, prevention, and care into a single, acclaimed user experience.

We are on an incredible journey to build a global leading company, with a unique culture. We already partner with 40K+ companies of all sizes, serving more than 1M+ members, and have reached €800M+ in ARR.

Prevention as the new norm. That's what we're building with our team of 800+ people. If it speaks to you: we're hiring across France, Spain, Belgium, and Canada. And beyond.

⭐️ The Data Team


Being part of Alan's data team means you'll have a positive impact on people's health and wellbeing.
We create insights to help our colleagues focus on what is most impactful.
We build features and tools powered by data that directly help our members.
Examples include:
going deep into health consumption patterns,
understanding our product usage,
creating visibility to help our sales team perform,
automating how we reimburse our members,
building products that help our members stay healthy...

We also believe in having a full stack approach:


Each one of us contributes to all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science). This encourages strong ownership and drives impact.
We own our infrastructure. We pride ourselves on a nimble data stack that gives us the flexibility we need. We're mainly using SQL, Python, R, Airflow, Snowflake, and Metabase.
We build in-house tooling to organize our data and serve it to the rest of the company.


🎯 Expected Skills And Abilities



You would thrive at Alan if most of this sounds like you:


Passionate about owning ambitious problems and developing simple solutions for them
Demonstrated impact during a first successful experience.
Experience or willingness to grow in all 3 aspects of the data spectrum (analytics, engineering, science) -- being specialized in one of these areas is good too!
Proficient in SQL, Python and/or R- Setting a high standard of rigor and precision for yourself, interested in your craft
Great listening and communication skills
Humble, kind, collaborative, and still willing to grow (no matter your seniority!)
Fluent and clear in English, both oral and written (no need to speak French!)


For this role we are looking to hire within the C1-E level range.

⚙️ Important note: we hire people, not roles.

If you're excited about this opportunity but don't check every box, we'd love to hear from you. Everyone, no matter how underrepresented, should feel free to apply, as it can only bring learnings or success.

If you identify yourself as a woman: Did you know that research shows women often apply only when meeting 100% of requirements?

Remember, this is just a guide, not a checklist.

We'll be thrilled to receive your application!

🔖 Check out our About Alan and Career pages, as well as our Medium, blog and Glassdoor page for more info.

You want to know more about Alan?


🙌 Perks & Benefits: Alaners are provided with a stimulating environment and perks ensuring they are happy, efficient and spend only high-quality time with co-workers.
🤘 A strong culture: People joining Alan are often surprised and delighted by our innovative working method. We have a set of cultural values that guide our approach to work


🔄 A quick note about the process

Note: While you're applying for a specific area, you may join a different engineering team based on where we think you'll have the most impact. Check out descriptions of other areas here.

Title: BE - BI Consultant
Description:
Overview

Who are we?

CBTW is a global tech solutions company.

We empower businesses with tailored tech and business solutions that drive innovation and sustainable growth.

With over 2 500 experts across 21 countries, we deliver results through flexible engagement models like: whether augmenting teams, managing complex projects, or building long-term operations.

Thanks to our 5 service lines, we specialize in:


AI & Data Platforms,
Cloud & Enterprise Platforms,
Cybersecurity,
Software Engineering,
Strategy, Product & Transformation.


More info on our website: https://cbtw.tech/

What service line will you be part of?

As a B.I. Consultant, you will join our AI & Data Platforms expertise.

AI & Data Platforms is CBTW's service line dedicated to supporting organizations in leveraging their data and fulfilling their AI ambitions to achieve tangible results. We focus on adoption and measurable impact from day one, combining our comprehensive, end-to-end expertise with our ecosystem of leading partners such as SAS, Snowflake, Microsoft and Databricks.

Our clients rely on us to support with:


Data Platform modernisation, migration and optimisation
AI-driven Business Insights & Customer Analytics
Cutting-edge GenAI products & Agentic AI automation
Unified Data Platform & Cloud Infrastructure
Data & AI Governance
BI & Analytics for business teams


Responsibilities

What is your role?

You will be part of a journey where you will have two impacts: a client impact and an internal impact . For the internal impact, based on your skills and time, you will be joining our internal data communit y at CBTW, to share knowledge and best practices . For the client impact , t asks can vary depending on the client and the project, but as a BI Consultant, you will mainly :


Create insightful information and help managers make better data-driven decisions
Be able to participate from A to Z on the BI chain: identify , collect, integrate, analyze data & present reports
Develop ETL, do the modelization of data warehouses and build reports for the business


Qualifications

What is your profile?

You :


Have at least a bachelor's degree
Have at least 3 years of experience on a similar project
Speak fluently French or Dutch and English
Have an hands-on experience with tools and technologies such as SQL, Oracle, Informatica, Microsoft (SSIS, SSRS, SSAS), Talend, Alteryx, Power BI, Tableau, Qlik, IBM ( Datastage , Cognos), Kimball, DataVault, SAP…
Will be an outstanding candidate if you share our values: Positive Thinking, Collaboration, Evolution & Commitment.


What are we offering?

EVOLUTION:


A career development expert will follow up on your professional path within the company and support you in shaping and growing your career,
Catalogue with a wide variety of trainings for hard and soft skills, private language classes, unlimited access to an E-learning platform (Udemy, Code Academy... ) and possibility to get certified every year,
Training days credits.


COLLABORATION:


You will join a community of [job title] experts where colleagues support each other on projects, share knowledge through workshops and events to keep learning and evolving, and build international connections, while also enjoying great moments and drinks together,
Participate in internal projects to innovate and develop our solutions portfolio,
Join one of our collectives (Wellness, Agile, RSE, Gaming ...) and share your interests with other positive thinkers,
You’ll also enjoy several unforgettable company parties throughout the year. Events that CBTW has truly mastered the art of organizing.


ON TOP OF THAT:


We offer a competitive salary with a flexible mobility package that allows you to choose options such as having mobility benefits (electric cars, electric bikes, public transportation subscription).


Your next adventure starts at CBTW!

Do you see a match? We would love to see your application coming in! 😉

We focus on adoption and measurable impact from day one, combining our comprehensive, end-to-end expertise with our ecosystem of leading partners such as SAS, Snowflake, Microsoft and Databricks.

Our clients rely on us to support with:

  • Data Platform modernisation, migration and optimisation
  • AI-driven Business Insights & Customer Analytics
  • Cutting-edge GenAI products & Agentic AI automation
  • Unified Data Platform & Cloud Infrastructure
  • Data & AI Governance
  • BI & Analytics for business teams

Responsibilities

 What is your role?

You will be part of a journey where you will have two impacts: a client impact and an internal impact . For the internal impact, based on your skills and time, you will be joining our internal data communit y at CBTW, to share knowledge and best practices . For the client impact , t asks can vary depending on the client and the project, but as a BI Consultant, you will mainly :

  • Create insightful information and help managers make better data-driven decisions
  • Be able to participate from A to Z on the BI chain: identify , collect, integrate, analyze data & present reports
  • Develop ETL, do the modelization of data warehouses and build reports for the business

Qualifications

 What is your profile?

You :

  • Have at least a bachelor's degree
  • Have at least 3 years of experience on a similar project
  • Speak fluently French or Dutch and English
  • Have an hands-on experience with tools and technologies such as SQL, Oracle, Informatica, Microsoft (SSIS, SSRS, SSAS), Talend, Alteryx, Power BI, Tableau, Qlik, IBM ( Datastage , Cognos), Kimball, DataVault, SAP…
  • Will be an outstanding candidate if you share our values: Positive Thinking, Collaboration, Evolution & Commitment.

What are we offering?

EVOLUTION:

  • A career development expert will follow up on your professional path within the company and support you in shaping and growing your career,
  • Catalogue with a wide variety of trainings for hard and soft skills, private language classes, unlimited access to an E-learning platform (Udemy, Code Academy... ) and possibility to get certified every year,
  • Training days credits.

COLLABORATION:

  • You will join a community of [job title] experts where colleagues support each other on projects, share knowledge through workshops and events to keep learning and evolving, and build international connections, while also enjoying great moments and drinks together,
  • Participate in internal projects to innovate and develop our solutions portfolio,
  • Join one of our collectives (Wellness, Agile, RSE, Gaming ...) and share your interests with other positive thinkers,
  • You’ll also enjoy several unforgettable company parties throughout the year. Events that CBTW has truly mastered the art of organizing.

ON TOP OF THAT:

  • We offer a competitive salary with a flexible mobility package that allows you to choose options such as having mobility benefits (electric cars, electric bikes, public transportation subscription).

Your next adventure starts at CBTW!

Do you see a match? We would love to see your application coming in! 😉

Title: Data & AI Associate – Advanced Analytics & Machine Learning
Description:
Our client, a major player in the international energy infrastructure sector, is seeking a strategic Data & AI Associate to join their commercial team. This role focuses on managing the commercial data ecosystem, spearheading machine learning initiatives, and implementing AI-driven business processes to support operational excellence.

Responsibilities


Own and manage the evolution of the commercial data warehouse and analytics ecosystem.
Lead the development of advanced dashboards and decision-support tools using Power BI and Azure technologies.
Translate complex business challenges into scalable data solutions that deliver measurable commercial value.
Act as the Product Owner for the internal business analytics and AI platform, defining the roadmap for new capabilities.
Develop and optimize Machine Learning auction prediction models to improve forecasting accuracy and commercial decision-making.
Shape the long-term data strategy and operating plan while establishing clear governance and documentation standards.
Collaborate with internal stakeholders and external technology partners to bring cutting-edge AI use cases into production.


Requirements


You possess a Master’s degree or MBA in Data Science, Business Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Engineering, or a related quantitative field.
You have 1 to 3 years proven experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or machine learning, preferably within a data-intensive industry.
You bring practical experience using Python and SQL for developing predictive models and advanced analytics solutions.
You have a strong understanding of Machine Learning frameworks and data analysis techniques.
You possess advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and reporting tools such as Power BI.
You have a solid understanding of data governance, security, and quality management principles.
You're able to bridge the gap between business strategy and technical implementation to maximize value from data.
You are proactive and solution-oriented with excellent communication and presentation skills.
You are fluent in English


Nice to Haves


Experience within energy markets, utilities, or financial services.
Knowledge of Azure data platform architecture, including security and integration.
Knowledge of other languages like Dutch or French would be nice to have
Willingness to travel occasionally between international office locations, mainly UK and Germany.


Offer


Start date: ASAP
Duration: 1 year (with possibility of extension)
Work regime: Full-time
Location: Brussels
Working model: Hybrid (1-2 days per week onsite)
Contract: open to both permanent employees and freelancers

Title: Cat Pricing Analyst
Description:
Our organization




Ageas is a listed international insurance Group with a heritage spanning of 200 years, offering Retail and Business customers Life and Non-Life insurance, and is also engaged in reinsurance activities.

As an international insurance company, Ageas concentrates its activities in Europe and Asia through a combination of wholly owned subsidiaries and long-term partnerships with strong financial institutions and key distributors.

Ageas ranks among the market leaders in the countries in which it operates. It represents a staff force of about 50,000 people and reported annual inflows of over EUR 18,5 billion in 2024.




Ageas aims to be a Great place to Grow for all its people, while always staying true to its four values: care, share, deliver and dare. It fosters an inclusive culture, that values diversity and encourages everyone to get the best out of their talents.




Ageas Re

Ageas Re is organized as a Business Unit within ageas SA/NV, the Group holding, on whose balance sheet it underwrites third-party and intra-group reinsurance under the trade name Ageas Re.

The Ageas Re Operations & Claims team is dedicated to overseeing all day-to-day operational aspects of reinsurance, ensuring accurate technical and financial record keeping for both internal and third party reinsurance agreements, with a focus on delivering exceptional service and maintaining strong client relationships.




Your function




Design and maintain pricing models for reinsurance products using actuarial and statistical techniques.
Maintain awareness of the impact pricing decisions have on the insurance portfolio, enabling informed management decisions through a clear understanding of risk, profitability, and strategic outcomes.
Analyze historical claims data, exposure metrics, and market trends to inform pricing decisions.
Support underwriting teams with pricing guidance and scenario modeling.
Conduct profitability analysis and portfolio segmentation.
Price and present pricing recommendations (mostly) independently to senior stakeholders and contribute to pricing governance.
Ensure compliance with regulatory standards and internal pricing frameworks.
Design and maintain pricing models for reinsurance products using actuarial and statistical techniques.
Independently manage documentation and communication processes to meet audit and external department requirements;
Lead line-of-business projects such as market studies and tooling improvements—driving innovation through research, recommendations, and proactive development of pricing techniques beyond standard user-level;




Your profile




Education & Experience

Master’s degree in a quantitative discipline such as natural science, data science, engineering, or a related field.
Technical (pricing) expertise in one or more Ageas Re business lines
Progress toward actuarial qualifications
3-5 years experience in reinsurance pricing is a plus




Skills & Competencies

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills;
Sharp analytical skills;
Proactive problem solving skills;
Able to manage and meet deadlines and work under pressure;
Strong proficiency in one or more programming language such as VBA, R, Python, SQL.




Language

Full professional proficiency in English. Any other language is considered a plus




Our offer?




The possibility of (maximum 50%) home working;
A dynamic, multicultural working environment;
An interesting and extensive salary package (including meal vouchers, eco vouchers, hospitalization, disability and group insurance, additional child benefit, mobile allowance and intervention in the internet reimbursement, ...);
The possibility to optimize part of your salary package through a flexible income plan;
A corporate culture where you are encouraged to share your views and opinions;
A flat organizational structure with close collaboration and communication with management and cross departments;
An extensive range of learning & development opportunities through our online learning platform;

The opportunity to further develop yourself and to be part of a great team at a Top employer in Belgium. Find out more about it here.




Interested?

Please apply online. We are looking forward to talking to you!

Title: Data Management Specialist
Description:
Ben jij gepassioneerd door data en HR?
Wil je graag werken in een dynamische overheidsomgeving?




Dan is deze vacature iets voor jou!




Als HR Data Specialist help je de organsatie met het verbeteren en sturen van HR-processen op een weloverwogen, datagedreven manier. Een greep uit het takenpakket:

Ontwikkelen en optimaliseren van het HR- datamodel
Waken over datakwaliteit via rapporten, dashboards en analyses
Implementeren van data management principes en -procedures en zorgen voor de naleving ervan
Beheren van data-gerelateerde problemen en zoeken naar structurele verbeteringen
Identificeren van kansen voor procesoptimalisatie en automatisering en bijdragen aan de implementatie ervan
Vertegenwoordigen van HR binnen de data governance organen van het bedrijf




In welke organisatie kom je terecht?

Dit bedrijf is dynamisch en verandert voortdurend, wat betekent dat je altijd nieuwe uitdagingen zult aangaan. Ze waarderen diversiteit en inclusie en moedigen hun medewerkers aan om zichzelf te zijn.




Profiel

Masterdiploma of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
Enkele jaren relevante ervaring met datamanagement of -governance
Analytisch en kritisch ingesteld
Leergierige teamplayer
Autonoom werken en deadlines halen
Stressbestendig en comfortabel in een dynamische werkomgeving
Goede communicatieve vaardigheden
Vloeiend in het Nederlands, Frans en Engels
Kennis van SAP SuccessFactors is een pluspunt




Aanbod

Aantrekkelijk salaris aangevuld met een 13e maand, onkostenvergoeding, maaltijdcheques en een bonus
Flexibel budget voor fietslease, lease auto, terugbetaling pensioensparen, enz.
Hospitalisatie- en groepsverzekering
Flexibele werkuren, de mogelijkheid om twee dagen per week te telewerken en 33 vakantiedagen per jaar
Daarnaast geniet je van voordelen voor openbaar vervoer.

Title: Data Scientist Grid Data Analytics,
Description:
Excited to work for a key Belgian player in the energy landscape?

The Belgian and European energy landscape is in full swing. Did you know that Elia Belgium plays a crucial role in this? As part of Elia Group, a European transmission system operator, Elia Belgium ensures the reliability and sustainability of the electricity supply in our country.

Side-by-side with your talented colleagues, you will play a part in building a sustainable, green and reliable energy infrastructure that stimulates economic growth and reduces our ecological footprint.

Only together can we make the biggest impact possible! We believe in the power of diversity we are convinced that an inclusive and diverse work environment leads to more innovation, creativity and better results.

Data Scientist Grid Data Analytics

Requisition ID 11173

Posting Start Date 13/07/2026

Function

Help build smart data applications for the electricity grid of the future

Do you want to harness AI, data science and advanced analytics to generate social impact?

Within Grid Data Analytics, you will develop innovative solutions that support the reliability, quality and performance of Belgium's high-voltage grid.

Together with experts from various disciplines, you will translate complex business challenges into smart data solutions that make a difference every day.

Your impact

As Data Scientist, you will design and develop advanced data and AI solutions for various domains within Grid Solutions.

You will combine data science, AI, software development and business insight to produce solutions that are scalable and generate real added value.

You will work closely with business stakeholders, data experts and specialists from Division-D.

Examples of projects include


automatic asset recognition via computer vision;
prediction and analysis of grid load based on historical data;
AI-driven analysis tools for technical simulations;
smart applications for data mapping between different systems within the company.


Your Responsibilities


You will translate business requirements into tangible solutions that deliver data‑driven insights with clear added value.
You will present data and insights in a clear, understandable and powerful way.
You will conduct advanced data analyses (such as trend detection and benchmarking).
You will propose ways to improve (on a cross-cutting basis) data quality across various products and data consumers.
You will actively contribute to the realisation of specific data-related backlog items within the context of project or product teams.
You will optimise data management processes, thereby boosting efficiency and quality.
You will work closely with a broad group of stakeholders from various domains.
You will actively monitor market trends and technological developments and analyse how these can help enhance solutions (e.g. automation of information processing, data science and AI), both within the Grid Data Analytics team and beyond.
You will utilise the latest data and AI technologies and tools within Elia (as provided by the chapters) to deliver better solutions for the business, applying the early-adopter principle.


Why choose Elia?

Elia gives you the opportunity to apply your technical expertise in pursuit of a socially relevant goal the reliability and future of the Belgian grid. You will work on innovative projects in which data, AI and digitalisation play a key role in the energy transition.

You will join an organisation that invests in its employees, fosters collaboration and gives you room to grow. Together with experts from various disciplines, you will help create sustainable solutions that make a difference both today and tomorrow.

Your Profile


You have a degree in computer science, business administration, engineering or a similar field (or equivalent experience), possibly complemented by a degree in AI or data science.
You have a good understanding of Elia's activities and the energy market (with a focus on asset management, engineering, system operations and system planning) or are willing to quickly learn more about them.
You have some initial experience
as a data scientist and/or AI expert, with excellent knowledge of the Python ecosystem;
with DevOps principles, preferably within an Azure environment;
in training, deploying and operationalising AI models (computer vision, natural language and/or time series), ideally on an industrial scale (MLOps);
in establishing data quality frameworks and applications;
with data architectures such as data lakehouses (e.g. Databricks).
You have a good understanding of the basic principles of data governance (e.g. data quality, traceability, data ownership, data domains, data dictionary, data transformation best practices).
You have proven experience in
operational data management processes;
data mesh, data products and data contracts;
data quality KPIs and associated tools.
You are familiar with Agile methodologies, are tech-savvy and are curious about new technologies.
You possess strong communication and presentation skills.
You are solution-oriented, think outside the box and are willing to adopt a hands-on approach.
You work efficiently in multi-functional teams.
You have a strong command of French, Dutch and English (being at C1 level in at least one language and at at least B2 level in the others).

Offer


Salaris Competitief loonpakket, representatievergoeding, eindejaarspremie, dubbel vakantiegeld, maaltijdcheques (€10 per werkdag), ecocheques, sport & cultuur cheques, bonussen op basis van individuele en collectieve resultaten.
Verzekering Groepsverzekering, hospitalisatieverzekering, verzekering voor ambulante zorgen voor het gezin alsook een ongevallenverzekering privéleven.
Vakantie Je hebt recht op 20 verlofdagen en 5 compensatieverlofdagen, 6 lokale dagen, 4 kaderdagen (na 1 jaar in dienst) en tot maximum 5 anciënniteitsdagen (1 dag per 3 jaar in dienst).
Sociaal fonds Eindejaarcheques, premie voor geboorte of huwelijk, en bij de aanschaf van een bril, gebitsprothese of andere soortgelijke behoeften vergoeden we een deel van de kosten.
Communicatie Je ontvangt een iPhone met abonnement (ook voor privégebruik), terugbetaling internet en laptop.
Kortingen Je geniet 30% korting op je gas- en elektriciteitsrekening.
Mobiliteit We bieden je een bedrijfswagen en openbaar vervoer of mobiliteitsbudget
Elia aandelen Elia biedt je de mogelijkheid om in te schrijven op aandelen met een korting van 16.66% op de gemiddelde koers van het aandeel.


Location

Avenue de Vilvorde 126, 1000 Brussels

There’s something electric in the air with this job offer!

In exchange for your work and dedication, you enjoy the following benefits


A job full of variety with a top employer Elia encourages innovation and collaboration. Within our company you will be at the forefront of the energy transition, and you will have the chance to contribute to ground-breaking technologies designed to accelerate this transition. It is a one-of-a-kind opportunity to further develop your knowledge and skills.
Focus on personal growth and well-being at work Discover a wide range of professional training and coaching opportunities that are guaranteed to give your personal growth a solid boost. At the same time, as a top employer, we put maximum effort into a positive work culture in which your well-being is central.
Development opportunities for a challenging career At Elia, we encourage you to seek out and seize new professional opportunities. You will enjoy complete freedom to shape your career on your own terms.
A diverse team of passionate colleagues Elia puts a high value on diversity and inclusion. You will join a diverse and passionate team that enjoys sharing knowledge and leading the way towards a more sustainable future together.


These are just a few of the benefits that distinguish Elia as a Top Employer. We are convinced that your contribution to our team will be invaluable and look forward to welcoming you soon!

Apply now

  • Kortingen Je geniet 30% korting op je gas- en elektriciteitsrekening.
  • Mobiliteit We bieden je een bedrijfswagen en openbaar vervoer of mobiliteitsbudget
  • Elia aandelen Elia biedt je de mogelijkheid om in te schrijven op aandelen met een korting van 16.66% op de gemiddelde koers van het aandeel.

Location

Avenue de Vilvorde 126, 1000 Brussels

There’s something electric in the air with this job offer!

In exchange for your work and dedication, you enjoy the following benefits

  • A job full of variety with a top employer Elia encourages innovation and collaboration. Within our company you will be at the forefront of the energy transition, and you will have the chance to contribute to ground-breaking technologies designed to accelerate this transition. It is a one-of-a-kind opportunity to further develop your knowledge and skills.
  • Focus on personal growth and well-being at work Discover a wide range of professional training and coaching opportunities that are guaranteed to give your personal growth a solid boost. At the same time, as a top employer, we put maximum effort into a positive work culture in which your well-being is central.
  • Development opportunities for a challenging career At Elia, we encourage you to seek out and seize new professional opportunities. You will enjoy complete freedom to shape your career on your own terms.
  • A diverse team of passionate colleagues Elia puts a high value on diversity and inclusion. You will join a diverse and passionate team that enjoys sharing knowledge and leading the way towards a more sustainable future together.

These are just a few of the benefits that distinguish Elia as a Top Employer. We are convinced that your contribution to our team will be invaluable and look forward to welcoming you soon!

 Apply now

Title: CRO Specialist
Description:
Wat je gaat doen
Zie jij overal kansen om websites te verbeteren en kun je dat nog eens met data staven ook? Perfect! Als CRO Specialist bij iO maak je met jouw datagedreven optimalisaties écht impact op de conversies van websites en portalen. Jij vormt de brug tussen onze marketeers en developers en geeft hen de juiste input om mee aan de slag te gaan.

Op basis van jouw aanbevelingen uit verschillende onderzoeksmethodes, zet je proactief initiatieven op die de gebruikservaring en conversies een boost geven.

Jouw dagelijkse uitdagingen:

Van snelle audits tot diepgaande analyses: Je duikt in de data voor een brede waaier aan klanten. Je voert CRO-analyses en audits uit, brengt de pijnpunten in de customer journey bloot en vertaalt je inzichten naar concrete, impactvolle aanbevelingen.
Diepgaande optimalisatietrajecten: Je bijt je vast in complexe optimalisatieprogramma's. Hierin focus je toegewijd op het continu verbeteren van de gebruikerservaring en het maximaliseren van conversies


Dit alles doe je natuurlijk niet alleen. Je werkt nauw samen met een team van data- en analytics specialisten, UX-designers, marketeers en strategen. Je voert A/B-tests uit, analyseert de resultaten en verwerkt je bevindingen in professionele presentaties. Jouw inzichten giet je in overtuigende verhalen waarmee je zowel klanten als collega’s inspireert en aanzet tot actie.

Bij iO staan we nooit stil. We investeren volop in jouw technische kennis en persoonlijke ontwikkeling. Want wij weten als geen ander: samen staan we sterker.

Daarom ben jij de perfecte CRO Specialist

Je hebt een sterk analytisch vermogen, bent van nature nieuwsgierig, werkt nauwkeurig en hebt een proactieve houding
Online personalisatietools zoals VWO, Spotler, Adobe of Blueconic hebben voor jou geen geheimen
Je bent een krak in het toepassen van CRO-principes (zoals Cialdini en andere overtuigingstechnieken) op diverse businessmodellen (B2B, B2C, e-commerce)
Met minstens 2 jaar ervaring op de teller weet jij perfect hoe je een succesvol optimalisatieplan opstelt en uitvoert
Je vindt moeiteloos je weg in tools als Clarity en Hotjar
Complexe data uit Google Looker Studio, Google Analytics en PowerBI zet jij om in overtuigende en heldere inzichten
Je hebt ervaring met HTML en CSS. Kennis van JavaScript is een dikke plus!
Je weet hoe je duidelijke en overtuigende presentaties maakt en kunt je bevindingen uitstekend onderbouwen


Dit krijg jij van ons

32 vakantiedagen
Op basis van een 40-urige werkweek heb je 20 vakantiedagen en 12 ADV-dagen.

Mobiliteit & eindejaarspremie
Na 6 maanden in dienst kun je bij iO rekenen op een eindejaarspremie. En op mobiliteitsvlak bieden we verschillende mogelijkheden, passend bij jouw job of voorkeur.

Hospitalisatieverzekering
Heb je eens iets voor? Dan is extra stress rond kosten het laatste wat je kunt gebruiken. Iedereen geniet bij ons van een hospitalisatieverzekering bij Axa.

Hybride werken
Een goede werk-privébalans is essentieel. Samen met je team maak je afspraken over wanneer én waar je werkt.

Gezonde snacks & lunch
Op elke iO campus pak je de hele dag een gezond tussendoortje en lunch je voor een kleine vergoeding gezond én gevarieerd.

Persoonlijk ontwikkelbudget
Je krijgt elk jaar € 3.000,- om jezelf te ontwikkelen, in je vak of als persoon, met trainingen, events of cursussen.

Samen vieren
Als we iets te vieren hebben, dan doen we het goed! Het partyteam op jouw campus staat op z'n tijd dan ook garant voor de leukste (thema)feestjes.

Title: Digital Analytics and CRO Consultant
Description:
Why Keyrus, Why Now!

Keyrus is an international group of 2,800 consultants and experts across 28 countries, built on a single conviction: AI does not transform businesses. Architected intelligence does. For more than 30 years, we have been building the data foundations that make intelligent systems work — designing the Operating System of the intelligent enterprise, where intelligence is embedded into the core of business processes to create sustainable value: we operationalise intelligence.

AI does not replace humans. It repositions us to a place no system can follow: understanding, deciding, designing, and creating. At Keyrus, you will not just develop skills - you will develop judgment. Your expertise sharpens with every system you architect, every client challenge you solve, every deployment that compounds on the last.

Over time, you grow into one of the rarest professionals of the intelligence era: someone who bridges data, AI, and human decision-making at scale, across industries and geographies. This is not a role you fill. It is a discipline you master and a story you help write to become a Keyrus Architect of Intelligence.

Technology amplifies. Keyrus culture differentiates. Industrial discipline connects the two.

What You'll Architect

This role focuses on turning customer behavior into measurable, continuously improving performance systems. You design the feedback loops between data, experimentation, and experience.


Design measurement frameworks across websites and apps to capture reliable user behavior data
Implement and manage tagging strategies (e.g. Google Tag Manager, Adobe Launch, Tealium)
Analyse customer journeys by combining data across analytics, transactional, and behavioral sources
Translate data insights into hypotheses that improve user experience and marketing performance
Design, run, and evaluate experimentation programs (A/B tests, CRO initiatives)
Build dashboards and visualisations that support decision-making across teams
Leverage platforms such as Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics, or Contentsquare to drive insights
Query and structure data using SQL across environments such as BigQuery, Azure or Databricks
Collaborate with stakeholders to ensure accurate measurement and alignment on performance goals
Contribute to building scalable customer intelligence systems combining analytics, UX, and experimentation


Who You Are


You understand that analytics is not about reporting — it’s about improving decisions and experiences
You think in loops: data → insight → hypothesis → test → improvement
You combine analytical depth with curiosity about user behavior and digital experience
You are comfortable working across tracking, data analysis, and experimentation
You bring structure to complex customer journeys and fragmented data sources


What You Bring

Qualifications / Experience


3+ years of experience in Digital Analytics, CRO, or Digital Marketing Analytics
Experience analysing digital performance across web and/or app environments
Professional working proficiency in Dutch or French and English


Technical & Functional Skills


Experience with tagging and data collection (Google Tag Manager, Adobe Launch, Tealium)
Strong knowledge of digital analytics tools (Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics, Contentsquare)
Experience with SQL and data querying across platforms (BigQuery, Azure, Databricks)
Ability to combine multiple data sources into coherent analysis models
Understanding of UX principles, SEO, and digital marketing performance
Experience with A/B testing, experimentation design, and CRO methodologies
Experience with data visualisation and dashboarding for business users


Nice-to-haves


Exposure to machine learning or advanced analytics in marketing


Rewards - What We Offer At Keyrus

Growth

At Keyrus we have created an environment where people are supported, challenged and connected to the people around them.

Working on impactful Data & Digital projects you will develop a broad and versatile skill set and stay ahead in a fast-evolving, AI-driven landscape.

Our digital learning platform, with over 30,000 courses including in-house trainings developed by our experts, is a perfect way to stay on top of your game

A generous training budget is available to help foster a culture where learning, feedback and continuous improvement is the norm

You will have a dedicated coach, benefit from regular check-ins, learn via different communities and have clear alignment with your team and company goals

We want you to grow with confidence. We therefore place a strong emphasis on ownership, open feedback and continuous development.

When people feel trusted, connected and inspired, they do their best work!

Rewards

Mobility budget? Car with fuel or charging car? We offer both so you can choose!

On top of that we also have daily and monthly net allowances, eco vouchers, hospitalisation (DKV) insurance and group insurance, as well as a generous bonus scheme that you can use on our flexible reward plan.

What We Stand For

Collective Intelligence — Collaboration across expertise, functions, and geographies makes it possible to combine know-how and deliver more comprehensive responses to client challenges.

Reliability — The ability to deliver complex projects with rigour is one of the pillars of our client relationship.

Pragmatism — Prioritising concrete impact and measurable value over abstract technological discourse.

Entrepreneurial Spirit — Curiosity, energy, and freedom are the foundations of our culture; they enable initiative and sustained innovation.

About Keyrus

At Keyrus, we help organizations move from experimental AI to industrialized AI, from isolated agents to orchestrated systems, and from insight to execution. This is the discipline we call being an Architect of Intelligence. Designing the Operating System of the intelligent enterprise, where intelligence is embedded into the core of business processes to create sustainable value: we operationalize intelligence.

Powered by our proprietary Human Orchestrated Model™ (HOM), we architect reliable:


Intelligence Foundations,
Human in Command governance,
and Performance Steering,


To create intelligent environments where technology amplifies human capabilities and performance compounds over time.

With 30+ years of expertise and 2,800 employees across 28 countries, we help organizations go beyond transformation: to build adaptive, resilient, and continuously improving intelligent organizations.

AI does not transform businesses. Architected intelligence does.

For over 30 years, Keyrus have been Architects of intelligent systems. We have combined engineering, business consulting, data architecture, and AI in real operational environments. We have done this whilst never forgetting a more human enterprise of allowing humans to design, supervise, and shape what only they can create.. Find out more on our services on keyrus.com.

Title: Market & Business Intelligence Expert
Description:
Wil jij met data écht impact maken op strategische beslissingen?

Als Market & Business Intelligence Expert bij Securex vertaal je complexe markt-, klant- en bedrijfsdata naar heldere inzichten en concrete acties. Zo help je mee bepalen waar we groeien, hoe we ons onderscheiden en welke keuzes het meeste verschil maken.

De functie

Als Market & Business Intelligence Expert Ben Je Een Cruciale Schakel Tussen Data En Strategie. Jij Zorgt Ervoor Dat Beslissingen Binnen Securex Onderbouwd, Relevant En Toekomstgericht Zijn. Je Verantwoordelijkheden Zijn Veelzijdig En Impactvol

Markt-, klant- en concurrentieanalyse


Je volgt trends in de markt, het gedrag van klanten en prospects en relevante macro-economische evoluties op
Je voert kwalitatieve en kwantitatieve analyses, bevragingen en benchmarkstudies uit
Je detecteert opportuniteiten en risico’s op basis van data en onderzoek


Business Intelligence & data-analyse


Je analyseert interne data en vertaalt die naar bruikbare inzichten
Je ontwikkelt KPI’s en werkt samen met data teams om dashboards en rapporteringen op te zetten
Je brengt complexe data terug tot heldere, actiegerichte inzichten


Strategische ondersteuning


Je ondersteunt strategieontwikkeling en business cases met onderbouwde analyses (o.a. marktaandeel, segmentatie, groeipotentieel)
Je werkt mee aan foresight-oefeningen en scenario-analyses
Je identificeert kansen voor optimalisatie, groei en klantwaarde


Stakeholdermanagement & communicatie


Je presenteert inzichten en aanbevelingen aan interne en externe stakeholders (management, business units, Raad van Bestuur, …)
Je fungeert als neutrale sparringpartner bij strategische keuzes
Je werkt nauw samen met o.a. Strategy, Marketing & Sales, Finance, Data Analytics en externe partners
Uitbouw & onderhoud van een AI-ondersteund kennisplatform dat alle rapporten, studies en analyses verbindt tot bevraagbare, actiegerichte inzichten voor interne stakeholders.


Je rapporteert aan de Director Intelligence en speelt een sleutelrol in het verbinden van data, business en strategie.

Jouw profiel

Je bent een analytische denker met een sterke business mindset en het talent om data te vertalen naar heldere verhalen.

Achtergrond & ervaring


Masterdiploma (economie, statistiek, psychologie, bedrijfskunde, …) of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
3 tot 5 jaar ervaring in market intelligence, business intelligence of consultancy
Ervaring in een complexe organisatie met meerdere stakeholders is een plus


Analytische & Technische Skills


Sterke kennis van marktonderzoeksmethodes en strategische analysetechnieken (SWOT, PESTEL, Porter, …)
Ervaring met dataverwerking en analyse (Excel, SQL; kennis van SPSS, Python of R is een plus)
Je weet inzichten visueel en begrijpelijk te maken
Praktische kennis van AI om bovenstaande vaardigheden naar een hoger niveau te tillen.


Persoonlijke competenties


Sterk conceptueel en analytisch denkvermogen
Je communiceert helder en overtuigend, ook naar niet-dataspecialisten
Proactief, resultaatgericht en autonoom
Samenwerkingsgericht met oog voor het bredere organisatiedoel
Discreet en betrouwbaar in omgang met vertrouwelijke informatie
Je beweegt je vlot in een omgeving met senior stakeholders
Je spreekt zeer goed Nederlands of Frans met een goede kennis van de andere landstaal en beheerst perfect het Engels.


Waarom kiezen voor Securex?

Bij Securex draagt elke medewerker bij aan het succes van onze klanten. Daarom is het onze missie om onze medewerkers te laten schitteren in hun job! Dat begint met een sterk onboardingproces. Gedurende je hele loopbaan bieden we je de vrijheid om je persoonlijke ontwikkeling in eigen handen te nemen dankzij uitgebreide opleidingsmogelijkheden, interne mobiliteit en allerlei soorten projecten.

We bieden je een aantrekkelijk loonpakket met tal van extra voordelen, zoals maaltijdcheques, hospitalisatie-, groeps- en invaliditeitsverzekering en aantrekkelijke kortingen. Werknemers die geen bedrijfswagen hebben, kunnen ook tegen een voordelig tarief een wagen leasen. Je kan rekenen op minimaal 27 vakantiedagen in een werkweek van 38,5 uur, aangevuld met 4 extra verlofdagen (of flexibele werktijden) en 4 verlofdagen op basis van anciënniteit.

Met onze 'New Way Of Working' positioneren we ons als een flexibele werkgever met een dynamische werkomgeving. Als je functie het toelaat, kan je werken op kantoor combineren met thuiswerken. Al onze medewerkers delen een passie voor onze klanten, bouwen positieve relaties op, nemen verantwoordelijkheid en werken met een digitale mindset. We zetten ons volledig in voor maatschappelijk verantwoord ondernemen. Kiezen voor Securex betekent kiezen voor een duurzame werkgever, onderneming en partner.

  • Ervaring met dataverwerking en analyse (Excel, SQL; kennis van SPSS, Python of R is een plus)
  • Je weet inzichten visueel en begrijpelijk te maken
  • Praktische kennis van AI om bovenstaande vaardigheden naar een hoger niveau te tillen.

Persoonlijke competenties

  • Sterk conceptueel en analytisch denkvermogen
  • Je communiceert helder en overtuigend, ook naar niet-dataspecialisten
  • Proactief, resultaatgericht en autonoom
  • Samenwerkingsgericht met oog voor het bredere organisatiedoel
  • Discreet en betrouwbaar in omgang met vertrouwelijke informatie
  • Je beweegt je vlot in een omgeving met senior stakeholders
  • Je spreekt zeer goed Nederlands of Frans met een goede kennis van de andere landstaal en beheerst perfect het Engels.

Waarom kiezen voor Securex?

Bij Securex draagt elke medewerker bij aan het succes van onze klanten. Daarom is het onze missie om onze medewerkers te laten schitteren in hun job! Dat begint met een sterk onboardingproces. Gedurende je hele loopbaan bieden we je de vrijheid om je persoonlijke ontwikkeling in eigen handen te nemen dankzij uitgebreide opleidingsmogelijkheden, interne mobiliteit en allerlei soorten projecten.

We bieden je een aantrekkelijk loonpakket met tal van extra voordelen, zoals maaltijdcheques, hospitalisatie-, groeps- en invaliditeitsverzekering en aantrekkelijke kortingen. Werknemers die geen bedrijfswagen hebben, kunnen ook tegen een voordelig tarief een wagen leasen. Je kan rekenen op minimaal 27 vakantiedagen in een werkweek van 38,5 uur, aangevuld met 4 extra verlofdagen (of flexibele werktijden) en 4 verlofdagen op basis van anciënniteit.

Met onze 'New Way Of Working' positioneren we ons als een flexibele werkgever met een dynamische werkomgeving. Als je functie het toelaat, kan je werken op kantoor combineren met thuiswerken. Al onze medewerkers delen een passie voor onze klanten, bouwen positieve relaties op, nemen verantwoordelijkheid en werken met een digitale mindset. We zetten ons volledig in voor maatschappelijk verantwoord ondernemen. Kiezen voor Securex betekent kiezen voor een duurzame werkgever, onderneming en partner.

Title: Data Analyst - BI & Automation
Description:
Do you enjoy working with data, systems, dashboards, and business insights? Are you comfortable connecting different data sources, checking accuracy, and helping teams make better decisions? Do you like creating structure where data flows are complex? Then this role is for you.

We are looking for a detail-oriented and technically skilled Data Analyst to join our international team. In this role, you will build and improve reporting processes, dashboards, and analytical tools, working closely with finance, management, operations, and technical teams to ensure business data is accurate, consistent, well-structured, and transformed into useful insights for decision-making.

In this role, you will act as a key link between business stakeholders and technical teams, helping translate operational, financial, and management needs into reliable reports, dashboards, data flows, and analytical tools.

 

About PHILIPP PLEIN

PHILIPP PLEIN is an international luxury fashion and lifestyle group headquartered in Switzerland, operating across multiple brands and global markets. Known for bold creativity, innovation, and a strong international presence, the company continues to grow through retail, strategic partnerships, and digital commerce.

As we continue to strengthen our data-driven operations, this role will play an important part in improving visibility, accuracy, reporting quality, and decision-making across finance, operations, management, and key business teams.

 

Key Responsibilities

Build, maintain, and improve reports, dashboards, and analytical tools.
Work with databases, APIs, internal systems, and external data sources.
Extract, clean, map, transform, and validate data from multiple sources.
Monitor data connections, data flows, and reporting accuracy.
Identify data inconsistencies, investigate root causes, and suggest corrections.
Support finance with reporting, analysis, reconciliation, and performance tracking.
Create data visualizations using BI tools or custom-built dashboard solutions.
Automate reporting processes where possible.
Document data logic, report structures, sources, mappings, and calculations.
Translate business needs into clear reporting and data requirements.

 

Required Skills & Experience

Experience in data analysis, business analysis, reporting, BI, or financial analysis.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with databases.
Good knowledge of Python for data analysis, including pandas or similar libraries.
Experience with APIs, data integrations, data mapping, and multiple data sources.
Ability to design and build custom dashboards or internal reporting tools.
Strong Excel skills.
Understanding of data cleaning, validation, transformation, and accuracy checks.
Experience with financial or business performance data.
Experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio, or similar.
Knowledge of ETL processes, data pipelines, or reporting automation.
Ability to communicate clearly with finance, management, and technical teams.

 

Nice to Have

Experience with ERP, CRM, accounting, or operational systems.
Microsoft Power Automate experience.
Experience with AI tools such as Claude Code or Microsoft Copilot.

 

Ideal Profile

Analytical, precise, and highly detail-oriented.
Comfortable working with raw and imperfect data.
Able to check, challenge, and verify numbers.
Proactive in finding issues and improving reporting processes.
Business-minded and able to explain data clearly to non-technical users.
Independent, structured, and reliable.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
About TCS:

Tata Consultancy Services is an IT services, consulting and business solutions organization that has been partnering with many of the world’s largest businesses in their transformation journeys for over 50 years. TCS offers a consulting-led, cognitive powered, integrated portfolio of business, technology and engineering services and solutions. This is delivered through its unique Location Independent Agile™ delivery model, recognized as a benchmark of excellence in software development.




TCS has been serving customers in Switzerland since 1985. The company’s presence in Switzerland consists of offices in Zurich, Basel and Nyon. TCS Switzerland offers a broad spectrum of IT Services, Consulting and Financial Solutions. TCS has over 600+ consultants working in Switzerland and over 11000 associates supporting Swiss Clients from Offshore.




About this role:

Serve as a strategic advisor to client stakeholders and technical teams.
Drive business development by identifying and pursuing opportunities to sell data analytics services to Chief Data Officers and other key decision-makers.
Develop and present compelling presentations to senior customer executives and stakeholders
Work with customer to drive workshops and create data analytics charters and business cases.
Demand Creation: Work with customers to generate data analytics project opportunities
Customer Advisory: Lead Data & Analytics Assessment, Strategy and Roadmap engagements and drive customer workshops for analytics use cases
PreSales Leadership: Own stages of the presales cycle, RFI, RFP, and contribute to the development of solutions for presales




Desired Competencies:

Proven experience in a leadership role within data analytics.
Extensive experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP, Snowflake, Databricks) and Lakehouse architecture.
Demonstrated ability to connect with customer business stakeholders to articulate business problem and approach for data analytics solutions.
Deep understanding of entire data life cycle including ingestion, modelling, data quality, data governance,Analytics solutions
Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to engage senior-level audiences.
Able to work with various levels of business organization to coordinate between stakeholders on pre-sales activities
Analyzing, understanding and documenting the business processes in detail where required to guide use case development.
Experience in manufacturing Industry
Strong ability to plan and manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.




Does that sound interesting?




You are welcome to submit your application consisting of your CV, personal letter, relevant degrees or certifications details, professional references, etc. We work with ongoing recruitment so send in your application as soon as possible.




We look forward to receiving your application via LinkedIn or mail to pallavi.malhotra@tcs.com




https://www.tcs.com/who-we-are/legal/tcs-privacy-notice/tcs-privacy-notice-european-union

Title: Marketing Analyst, Data Intelligence, EMEA- 2-year contract
Description:
Ralph Lauren Corporation (NYSE:RL) is a global leader in the design, marketing and distribution of premium lifestyle products in five categories: apparel, accessories, home, fragrances, and hospitality. For more than 50 years, Ralph Lauren's reputation and distinctive image have been consistently developed across an expanding number of products, brands and international markets. The Company's brand names, which include Ralph Lauren, Ralph Lauren Collection, Ralph Lauren Purple Label, Polo Ralph Lauren, Double RL, Lauren Ralph Lauren, Polo Ralph Lauren Children, Chaps, among others, constitute one of the world's most widely recognized families of consumer brands.

At Ralph Lauren, we unite and inspire the communities within our company as well as those in which we serve by amplifying voices and perspectives to create a culture of belonging, ensuring inclusion, and fairness for all. We foster a culture of inclusion through: Talent, Education & Communication, Employee Groups and Celebration.

,

2-years contract

We are looking for a data-driven, curious, and collaborative Analyst to join the Marketing Data Intelligence team. This role will support the development of actionable insights across market intelligence, brand health, consumer research, and customer insights, helping drive better decision-making across Brand Growth and Omnichannel marketing teams

,

Data Analysis & Insights Generation


Analyze performance data across brand, omnichannel, and market dimensions to generate clear, actionable insights
Support the team in identifying trends, risks, and opportunities through data-driven analysis
Contribute to market intelligence by tracking industry, competitor, and consumer trends


Consumer & Market Insights


Support the design, execution, and analysis of market research and consumer insights projects
Consolidate multiple data sources (quantitative and qualitative) into clear narratives
Develop a strong understanding of consumer behavior and regional market dynamics


Reporting & Operational Excellence


Own and maintain recurring reports and dashboards
Ensure accuracy, consistency, and timeliness of data delivery
Support / promote ad hoc analyses to respond to business needs


Omnichannel & Stakeholder Support


Contribute to the integration of data into omnichannel and leadership presentations
Act as a key support for cross-functional teams by providing relevant data and insights
Collaborate closely with Marketing, Business Development, and other stakeholders


Success Factors

To Excel In This Role, You Will Need


A strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
High attention to detail and a structured, organized way of working
Curiosity and a proactive approach to understanding business questions
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment


,

The Ideal Candidate Will Possess


Strong data appetite with advanced analytical capabilities
Experience in consumer insights, market research, or performance analysis, preferably in fashion/luxury retail or fast-moving consumer goods.
Ability to work with large datasets and synthesize complex information clearly
Strong written and oral communication skills, with the ability to present insights in a concise and impactful way, adapted to the audience
Collaborative mindset and ability to work / coordinate projects with multiple stakeholders
Proactive approach with the ability to anticipate business needs, identify emerging questions, and translate them into relevant analytical approaches and insights
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment while managing multiple tasks effectively
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Outlook, Word, PowerPoint & Excel) and AI tools. Knowledge of data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) is a plus.
Fluency in English, any other language (especially French) is a bonus

Title: Data Analyst Excel - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Data Analyst Excel

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct in-depth data analysis using Microsoft Excel to extract actionable insights and support decision-making across teams.
Collaborate with project stakeholders to understand data requirements and translate them into meaningful analyses.
Review, clean, and validate datasets to ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity.
Develop clear, concise reports and visualizations to communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities in complex datasets, contributing to strategic initiatives.
Document methodologies, results, and recommendations with meticulous attention to detail.
Contribute expertise to enhance data-driven decision-making processes within the customer's team.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience performing data analysis in a business environment using Microsoft Excel as the primary tool.
Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy in data processing and reporting.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate analytical findings.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across teams to drive business outcomes.
Solid understanding of Excel functions, formulas, and data visualization tools.
Proficiency in identifying data inconsistencies and implementing corrective measures.
Experience working in fast-paced, data-driven organizations.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting cross-functional teams with data insights to inform business strategy.
Background in presenting analytical outcomes to diverse audiences.
Familiarity with additional data analysis tools or business intelligence platforms.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
La Fondation IPT, créée en 1972 par des entrepreneurs, est une fondation de droit privé, d’utilité publique et sans but lucratif. Elle est active dans l’insertion, la réinsertion et l’orientation professionnelle et travaille, en tant que partenaire national, avec les assurances sociales publiques ou privées, les entreprises, les institutions et les médecins.




Diversité et inclusion sont au cœur de notre mission. Nous encourageons chacun·e à réaliser son potentiel.




Afin d’accompagner l’évolution de nos outils et pratiques de travail, nous créons un nouveau poste au sein de notre équipe informatique basée à la Direction générale à Vevey, de :




Data Analyst (80-100%)




Nous recherchons une personne capable d’exploiter, d’analyser et de valoriser les données issues de nos systèmes actuels afin de répondre aux besoins de pilotage, de suivi et de reporting de la Fondation. Le poste sera centré sur l’extraction de données et la mise à disposition de reportings à partir de nos systèmes actuels. En parallèle, nous menons une réflexion sur l’évolution de nos outils et de nos pratiques en matière de données. Le ou la Data Analyst prendra ainsi une part active à cette transformation, avec l’opportunité de contribuer aux choix, aux améliorations et au développement des futurs outils de pilotage.




Ta mission :




Transformer les données opérationnelles liées aux prestations métier en informations fiables et exploitables
Assurer la qualité, la cohérence et l'exploitabilité des données opérationnelles
Produire et mettre à disposition des Directions cantonales les extractions de données nécessaires à leurs analyses et reportings
Soutenir les services supports de la Direction générale en matière de traitement de données, indicateurs et reportings
Concevoir et maintenir des tableaux de bord et reportings adaptés aux besoins des utilisateur·trice·s
Identifier les opportunités d’amélioration et contribuer à l’optimisation des processus




Ton profil :




Formation supérieure en informatique de gestion, data analytics, statistiques, mathématiques, économie d'entreprise ou domaine similaire
Connaissances des bases de données relationnelles (idéalement SQL), d'outils de reporting et de visualisation de données (p.ex. Power BI) et maitrise d'Excel
Maîtrise d’environnements digitaux et collaboratifs (p. ex. Microsoft 365, SharePoint, ERP, GED)
Première expérience en data analyse appréciée, mais pas indispensable si tu as les compétences clés
Véritable intérêt pour l’analyse de données, avec un esprit analytique développé et une curiosité naturelle pour comprendre ce que révèlent les données
Aisance relationnelle et capacité à interagir avec des interlocuteurs variés
Rigueur, esprit de synthèse et exigence de qualité dans les livrables




Nous t’offrons :




La possibilité de mettre à profit et de développer vos compétences au sein d’une entreprise engagée
Un poste à forte autonomie, des systèmes informatiques sains sur des équipements modernes et en constante évolution
Un environnement de travail bienveillant, aligné sur les valeurs de la Fondation
Une culture d'écoute et de valorisation des idées et initiatives
Une grande autonomie de travail, un horaire flexible et la possibilité de télétravailler
Un lieu de travail proche des transports public (bus, train)




Intéressé·e à nous rejoindre ?




Partagez-nous ton dossier de candidature complet par e-mail à : candidatures@fondation-ipt.ch en mentionnant dans l’objet de l’email « IPT DG – Data Analyst ».




Délai de candidature : 2 août 2026

  • Une grande autonomie de travail, un horaire flexible et la possibilité de télétravailler

Partagez-nous ton dossier de candidature complet par e-mail à : candidatures@fondation-ipt.ch en mentionnant dans l’objet de l’email « IPT DG – Data Analyst ».

Title: Data Analyst Intern (H/F/X)
Description:
Data Analyst Intern (H/F/X) - 6-month paid internship, starting September 1, 2026, Lausanne (hybrid)

LB&Partners SA is a Swiss firm specializing in optimizing the performance of industrial manufacturers. We work with major international groups to improve their EBIT through a unique combination of business expertise, advanced methodology, and in-house technology solutions.




What you will do

In collaboration with the Programme team, you will collect, consolidate, and clean data from various sources (ERP, Excel, client databases); structure and model datasets; conduct statistical and exploratory analyses to identify trends and anomalies; build and maintain dashboards and KPI reports using BI tools; contribute to the automation of recurring reports; and prepare clear presentations for internal teams and clients.




Your profile

Bachelor's/Master's student or recent graduate in Statistics, Economics, Management, Computer Science, Data Analytics, or a related field. A first professional experience (internship included). Good command of Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables). Fluent French and professional English (C1). German is a real asset, as are Power BI (or equivalent), SQL, and Python basics.




We are looking for someone rigorous, curious, and organized, who enjoys teamwork, shows professional maturity, and is motivated to continue with us after the internship - there is a real prospect of a permanent position, depending on performance and business needs.




What we offer

A paid internship with real responsibilities on high-impact industrial data projects; flexible hours and remote work 1–2 days per week; 5 weeks of vacation per year (pro-rated); modern offices in the heart of Lausanne; a multicultural, dynamic, and supportive team.




How to apply

Send your complete application (CV, cover letter, diplomas) to hr@lb.partners.




Personal data submitted with your application is processed by LB&Partners SA exclusively for recruitment purposes, in accordance with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP) and our privacy policy available at www.lb.partners.

Title: Stagiaire Data Analyst
Description:
Description de l'entreprise

Le présent et le futur d’Audemars Piguet se construisent grâce à la contribution de tous nos talents. Inspirés par la richesse de notre passé, nous sommes enthousiastes à l’idée de ce que nous créerons dans le futur. Ensemble, nous sommes résolument tournés vers l’avenir et en quête continue d’excellence dans tous nos métiers.

Si notre mission vous inspire, venez poursuivre votre parcours au sein de notre famille pour que l’on continue de créer l'extraordinaire. Écrivons le prochain chapitre de votre carrière ensemble !

Description du poste

Dans le cadre d’un projet stratégique de digitalisation de nos ateliers de production, nous proposons un stage de 6 mois, à partir de septembre 2026, centré sur la collecte et l’exploitation des données issues des moyens de mesure utilisés dans le flux de production horloger. Le projet s’inscrit au cœur de problématiques industrielles concrètes et aura un impact direct sur la robustesse et la performance de nos processus.

Dans ce cadre, vous serez en charge des principales tâches suivantes :


Structurer et consolider les données provenant de nos ateliers de production
Développer des outils analytiques permettant de créer de la valeur et de sécuriser notre production
Mettre en application ces outils au sein de notre production afin de proposer des réglages initiaux optimisés


Qualifications


Étudiant·e de Master en Data Science, Microtechnique, Génie mécanique, Informatique, ou domaine apparenté
Connaissances approfondies en programmation (Python ou MATLAB)
Maîtrise de MS office
Intérêt pour les systèmes de mesure et/ou la mécanique horlogère
Capacité à travailler de manière rigoureuse, structurée et méthodique
Esprit d’équipe, sens du terrain et motivation pour interagir de manière transverse

Title: Business Analyst - Data & Analytics
Description:
Business Analyst – Data & Analytics




We are looking for a proactive Business Analyst with a strong understanding of Data & Analytics to bridge the gap between business and technology. You will work closely with stakeholders to identify, prioritize and shape analytics capabilities, translating business needs into scalable end-to-end analytics solutions.

We are looking for someone who understands the broader analytics landscape, enjoys working closely with the business, and can help design effective data-driven solutions.




In this role, you will:

Partner closely with business stakeholders to gather, analyse and challenge business requirements for analytics solutions.
Translate business needs into clear functional requirements and scalable end-to-end solution designs.
Collaborate with technical teams to deliver analytics and reporting solutions.
Support multiple analytics initiatives by identifying business priorities and helping shape the analytics roadmap.
Coordinate testing, business validation and successful deployment of new analytics capabilities.
Proactively identify opportunities to improve existing analytics solutions and processes.
Act as the key interface between business and technology, building strong stakeholder relationships and driving collaboration.
Stay up to date with modern analytics technologies and best practices.




Requirements

3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst in Data & Analytics, Business Intelligence or a similar domain.
Strong understanding of data, analytics and reporting, with the ability to design end-to-end business solutions.
Ability to translate business requirements into technical specifications and communicate effectively with both business and technical stakeholders.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Dynamic, hands-on and proactive personality with a strong ownership mindset.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with multiple parallel initiatives.

Title: Data Analyst Trainee
Description:
Make your mark for patients

Nous recherchons un/e Stagiaire en Ingénierie de données qui est dynamique et autonome pour rejoindre notre service digital encré au sein d’amélioration continue, basé dans notre site de production de Bulle en Suisse.

Stage en Informatique - Ingénierie des données

À propos du rôle

En tant que coordinateur de données stagiaire chez UCB, vous contribuerez activement à la valorisation des données métiers utilisées sur un site de production biopharmaceutique. Vous évoluerez au sein d’un environnement où qualité et transformation digitale sont au centre des priorités.

Votre mission consistera à faire le lien les équipes opérationnelles, les équipes digitales et les systèmes d’information, afin de garantir que les données soient exploitable en analysant les besoins et en modélisant les processus. Vous concevez des dashboards stratégiques qui créent de la valeur et servent de base aux prises de décision.

Vous évoluerez dans un environnement où la qualité des données est un prérequis essentiel pour la prise de décision, la performance opérationnelle et la conformité.

Ce que vous ferez

Qualifier et documenter les données métiers


Identifier les sources de données (process, systèmes, outils)
Documenter les jeux de données (définition, usage, fiabilité)
Contribuer à la création d’un langage commun autour de la donnée


Faire le lien entre métier et digital


Recueillir les besoins des utilisateurs (ateliers, interviews, terrain)
Traduire les besoins métiers en exigences data compréhensibles
Sensibiliser les équipes à la bonne utilisation des données


Contribuer aux initiatives de transformation digitale


Supporter le Digital Transformation Lead sur les projets liés à la donnée
Mise en place de solutions de suivi et de visualisation (Power BI ou Qlik sense)
Contribuer à l’automatisation et à la standardisation des flux data (Power Platform)


Intéressé(e) ? Pour ce rôle, nous recherchons


Bachelor et Master (obtenu/en cours) en Informatique et systems de communication
Bonne compréhension de la modélisation de processus.
Intérêt marqué pour la gouvernance des données, la qualité et la structuration de l’information,
Capacité d'analyse, esprit critique, curiosité.
Aisance relationnelle et capacité à collaborer avec des équipes multidisciplinaires.
Connaissance des outils digitaux : Power BI, QlikSense, Power Apps, Excel avancé.
La connaissance du milieu biotechnologique & pharmaceutique est un atout, mais pas obligatoire.
Bonne compréhension de l’anglais


Nous offrons un stage rémunéré d’une durée jusqu’à 6 mois ; date d’entrée en fonction : courant 2026, négociable selon les disponibilités.

Are you ready to ‘go beyond’ to create value and make your mark for patients? If this sounds like you, then we would love to hear from you!

About Us

UCB is a global biopharmaceutical company, focusing on neurology and immunology. We are over 9.000 people in all four corners of the globe, inspired by patients and driven by science.

Why work with us?

At UCB, we don’t just complete tasks, we create value. We aren’t afraid to push forward, collaborate, and innovate to make our mark for patients. We have a caring, supportive culture where everyone feels included, respected, and has equal opportunities to do their best work. We ‘go beyond’ to create value for our patients, and always with a human focus, whether that’s on our patients, our employees, or our planet. Working for us, you will discover a place where you can grow, and have the freedom to carve your own career path to achieve your full potential.

At UCB, we’ve embraced a hybrid-first approach to work, bringing teams together in local hubs to foster collaborative curiosity. Unless expressly stated in the description or precluded by the nature of the position, roles are hybrid with 40% of your time spent in the office.

UCB is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions will be made without regard to any characteristic protected by applicable laws.

Should you require any adjustments to our process to assist you in demonstrating your strengths and capabilities contact us on EMEA-Reasonable_Accommodation@ucb.com. Please note should your enquiry not relate to adjustments; we will not be able to support you through this channel.
