Title: Data & Marketing Analyst
Description:
At A-Players, we are building a new AI product in the mental health and mindfulness space - and we are looking for a Data & Marketing Analyst who will own the analytics layer from day one.

We are not building analytics after the fact. We are building it before the first ad goes live. That means clean tracking from day one, attribution that reflects reality, and dashboards the performance team opens every morning - not once a quarter.

In practice, you will set up the full tracking infrastructure from scratch, own the attribution model, connect marketing spend to real revenue, and keep LTV and retention metrics current and trustworthy. You will work side by side with the performance marketing team.

You will be the dedicated analyst on this product. Around you is a mature data team of 10+ people across Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Science, BI, and AI, as well as shared infrastructure including BigQuery, dbt, Tableau, and GA4.

This role is a fit if you have navigated the full analytics stack at a fast-moving web subscription product, know what it takes to build trust in data from scratch, and want to do meaningful work on something that is just getting started.

Responsibilities


Build the web tracking foundation for the product. Set up GA4 and GTM, both client-side and server-side, from scratch: UTM conventions, dataLayer schema, event deduplication, and attribution windows.
Implement server-side conversions for paid channels, including Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok Events API.
Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.


What We Look For


3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
Experience working with performance marketing data.
Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
High level of ownership and initiative.


Nice to have


Hands-on experience with GA4.
Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
Experience working with PSP data.
A/B testing experience.
Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
Experience with dbt.


We Offer


Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
Medical insurance.
Learning and development budget.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
ITERIS est un cabinet spécialisé en recrutement IT à Genève, au service des PME technologiques, des grandes organisations et des environnements internationaux de Suisse romande (banque, industrie, pharma, organisations internationales).




Nous constituons en continu notre réseau de spécialistes de la donnée, en amont des mandats que nos clients nous confient. Cette annonce ne correspond pas à un poste unique : elle nous permet d'identifier, en amont, les professionnels avec qui nous souhaiterons échanger dès qu'un mandat correspond.




Le profil que nous suivons en priorité : Data Engineer

Conception et industrialisation de pipelines de données (ETL / ELT).
Python, PySpark / Spark, SQL avancé.
Environnements cloud : Azure (Data Factory, Synapse), AWS ou GCP.
Plateformes et outils : Databricks, Snowflake, dbt, Airflow, Kafka.
Data Lake / Lakehouse (Delta, Parquet), qualité et gouvernance de la donnée, CI/CD et bonnes pratiques DevOps.




Profils adjacents également bienvenus : Analytics Engineer, BI, MLOps et mise en production de modèles.




Le profil : une expérience confirmée sur ces environnements, du profil solide au profil très senior - nous accompagnons aussi bien des rôles opérationnels que des postes d'architecture ou de lead. Nous constituons en continu notre réseau de spécialistes de la donnée, en amont des mandats que nos clients nous confient. Title: Intern - Data Science
Description:
Internship – Data Science

Role Overview

To support our Technology Department in the Advanced Materials division, we are looking for an Intern in Data Science. The position is a 6-month internship based at our Advanced Materials Innovation Center in Basel, Switzerland. The preferred start date is September 2026.

This role has been created to support the digital transformation of the Technology Department and contribute to ongoing knowledge management initiatives. We are looking for someone who is curious, analytical, and eager to apply data science, automation, and AI-related technologies in a multidisciplinary environment.

Do you think you’re the right fit for this opportunity? Apply today!

Role Responsibilities

As an Intern in Data Science, you will be responsible for:


Compile and analyze experimental data from archived projects to be utilized in data science initiatives
Build, maintain, and optimize end-to-end data pipelines that acquire, ingest, and process data from multiple sources into the enterprise Generative AI Platform.
Develop, prototype, and test LLM-based architectures and agentic systems for processing large-scale structured and unstructured datasets.
Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team to translate domain-specific problems into computational solutions, such as retrieval, ranking, and reasoning, in the fields of chemistry and materials science.
Assist in the automation of manual processes & dashboards.
Participate in code reviews and agile development cycles.
Document data processing scripts, data workflows and generate technical reports.
Work closely with a multidisciplinary team within Huntsman Advanced Materials, reporting into the Technology Department.


Experience And Competencies

We are looking for candidates with a strong interest in data science, digital transformation, and applied AI technologies, who are motivated to learn and contribute in an international, collaborative environment.

What We Are Looking For


Bachelor’s degree completed or currently enrolled in a Master’s programme in computer science, applied mathematics, engineering, or statistics.
Strong programming skills in Python; JavaScript is a plus.
Familiarity with LLMs, vector databases, and prompt engineering concepts.
Familiarity with cloud service platforms, such as Azure.
Basic knowledge of web development frameworks and SQL.
Basic knowledge of machine learning is a plus.
Knowledge of chemistry or materials science is a plus.
Passion to learn and a collaborative mindset.
Fluent in English.
A proactive attitude, strong communication skills, and motivation to contribute to digital transformation in a technology-driven environment.


What next?

If you would like to join an innovative, collaborative and multicultural team, please apply now. Your application will be forwarded to a Talent Acquisition Partner, who will strive to get back to you as soon as they can. We understand that applying for a new job is a big decision and we will work hard to keep you updated and support you along the way.

About Us

At Huntsman, we pride ourselves on being a people-oriented organization.

Our family-like atmosphere is cultivated by our diverse groups of team members around the world.

We welcome the talent, experience and fresh ideas that employees at all stages of their careers, from interns to seasoned professionals, bring to Huntsman.

In return, we offer you the opportunity to become an integral part of a dynamic, industry-leading company, where safety and ethics always come first.

Title: Ticketing Data Coordinator
Description:
Job information

Division/Unit: UEFA Events SA / Ticketing

Contract type: Max-term

Location: Nyon (WFH up to one day per week)

Start date: 01.10.2026

End date: 31.07.2029

Main goal

The Ticketing Data Coordinator works with the Ticketing Data Analyst to deliver actionable ticketing data analytics and insights for UEFA club and national team competition finals, including UEFA EURO 2028 and UEFA Women’s EURO 2029.

The role primarily focuses on mobile ticketing data, while also supporting overall ticketing data quality and analysis to optimise sales operations, mobile ticket distribution and the fan experience.

Key Responsibilities


Developing, maintaining and optimising ticketing data sources within Snowflake and reporting dashboards in Tableau, or similar systems
Ensuring ticketing data quality and integrity across multiple platforms (SSO, ticketing sales system, UEFA Mobile Tickets app, etc.)
Analysing ticket sales and mobile distribution trends to identify patterns, risks and opportunities
Identifying and tracking KPIs to measure ticketing performance and operational success delivery
Working with multiple stakeholders, in particular internal ICT colleagues as well as external providers
Supporting operational tasks in relation to the distribution of mobile tickets, including monitoring, performance and incident tracking
Gaining sufficient knowledge on ticketing processes from end to end, including venue configuration, inventory management, ticket sales, mobile ticketing, access control and custom
Supporting and performing other ticketing functions as required

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in computer science, data analytics, engineering or a related field
From 1 to 3 years in data analysis using Tableau or Power BI and working with Snowflake or similar data warehouse platforms


Languages:


Fluent in English


Other requirements:


Data-driven and analytical mindset
Interest in mobile applications on Android and iOS
Experience in fraud prevention is an advantage
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-pressure event environment
Strong organisational skills and excellent attention to detail
Proactive problem solver with a collaborative team-player approach
Comfortable working with a high degree of flexibility, autonomy and responsibility.

UEFA welcomes applications from all, actively working to ensure that we are an inclusive employer that finds the best candidate for our positions, regardless of aspects such as sex, gender identification, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion, ethnicity or nationality.

Title: Junior Data Engineer (m/f/d)
Description:
About Hilo By Aktiia

High blood pressure is the world's most common disease, causing 18 million deaths each year. At Hilo by Aktiia, our vision is a world where no lives are lost or damaged from the effects of high blood pressure, and our mission is to build the technology that helps people control it.

We are a venture-backed scale-up that has raised over $96M. Our technology, rooted in 18 years of research at the Swiss Center for Electronics and Microtechnology (CSEM), is the world's only medically accurate, cuffless, continuous blood pressure monitor for daily life. It is CE Marked as a Class IIa medical device and was FDA-cleared in 2026 as the first cuffless OTC blood pressure monitor in the United States. We are remote-first, headquartered in Neuchâtel, Switzerland.

Role Overview

Imagine using your data engineering skills to improve the way cardiovascular health is monitored and managed. Hilo by Aktiia is redefining blood pressure monitoring through AI-driven optical technology built on more than 20 years of research at the Swiss Center for Electronics and Microtechnology (CSEM). Their solution combines a wearable device (Hilo), a mobile app, and a cloud-based platform for healthcare professionals — empowering users and physicians with continuous, actionable insights into blood-pressure patterns. With more than 200,000 users, over $120M in funding, and a CE-certified medical device already available across several markets, Aktiia is continuing to scale its technology, product ecosystem, and international presence.

As Aktiia expands its data capabilities, we're looking for a Junior Data Engineer who will help scale a Databricks-based medallion Lakehouse from a primarily R&D/Core Tech setup into a broader company-wide data platform. Working closely with and learning directly from an experienced Senior Data Engineer, you will support the ingestion, centralization, documentation, and validation of clinical, legacy, and operational data. Your work will help transform complex and sometimes messy data sources into clean, reliable, and model-ready pipelines used by Data Science, ML, Algorithm, and Core Tech teams.

Your Tasks


Data Ingestion & Lakehouse Development: Design, build, and maintain data ingestion and processing pipelines within Aktiia's Databricks-hosted medallion Lakehouse, working under senior guidance while gradually taking on more ownership
Clinical & Legacy Data Integration: Take an active role in ingesting, centralizing, and documenting clinical data currently spread across EDC platforms such as Castor and RedCap, databases, standalone archives, and other historically grown sources
Data Exploration & Practical Data Archaeology: Work hands-on with unfamiliar and sometimes messy datasets, identify structures and inconsistencies, and turn unstructured situations into reliable, usable data assets
Pipeline Development & Preprocessing: Build preprocessing and ETL/ELT pipelines that provide clean, structured, and model-ready datasets for Algorithm Development, Core Tech, Machine Learning, and Data Science teams
Data Quality, Validation & Documentation: Define and apply practical standards for data quality, validation, traceability, and documentation — especially for sensitive and clinically relevant datasets
Observability & Engineering Practices: Implement logging, validation checks, alerting, and basic observability for new pipelines, while contributing to shared codebase practices such as Git, code reviews, CI/CD, and testing
Platform Scaling & Collaboration: Support the evolution of the lakehouse from selected technical use cases toward a company-wide data infrastructure, working closely with the Senior Data Engineer, ML Engineers, Data Scientists, and cross-functional stakeholders


Requirements


Academic Background: Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Data Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
Professional Expertise: At least 1+ year of practical post-study experience in data engineering, data infrastructure, data ingestion, or a similar hands-on technical role. You may still be early in your career, but you have already worked with real data pipelines, production-oriented data workflows, or collaborative data platforms
Technical Experience: You have solid hands-on experience with Python and SQL and bring practical experience with Databricks. You are familiar with cloud environments — ideally AWS. You understand the basics of ETL/ELT pipeline design, data ingestion patterns, and data modelling, and you have worked with shared codebases using Git, code reviews, CI/CD, or testing practices. Experience with Spark, Delta Lake, or Parquet is a strong plus
Industry Fit: Ideally, you have gained experience in a start-up, scale-up, or technically demanding environment where you worked hands-on across the data pipeline. Exposure to regulated or data-sensitive industries such as MedTech, Pharma, FinTech, or healthcare is a plus, especially when it comes to data quality, validation, and documentation
Language Skills: English at a highly proficient level is a must. French is advantageous
Personality: A curious, pragmatic, and hands-on problem-solver, you enjoy bringing structure into complex data environments. You work independently, ask the right questions, stay well organized, and collaborate openly with Data Engineering, Data Science, ML, Algorithm, Core Tech, and Product stakeholders. You are based in Switzerland and are comfortable working in a hybrid setup with one day per week regular presence in Neuchâtel


We're looking for a pragmatic, hands-on data builder who enjoys working with real-world clinical and company data — someone who is excited to bring structure into scattered datasets, build reliable pipelines, and help create a modern data foundation for advanced ML and digital health applications.

Do you want to grow your expertise in Data Engineering and cloud-based lakehouse architectures while contributing to health technology that has a direct impact on hundreds of thousands of people? Then we're excited to meet you!

Benefits

Why Join Us?


Hilo by Aktiia is becoming one of the most important med-tech companies, and you can be part of this exciting story
Work on a mission that matters: transforming cardiovascular health at global scale
Be part of a high-impact, fast-growing, and venture-backed scale-up company
Collaborate with a diverse, passionate, and talented team
Competitive compensation and other benefits (depending on location)

Title: Data Analyst for Beam Instrumentation (SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP)
Description:
Job Description

As an experienced Data Analyst with a solid background in physics, you will join the team responsible for the LHC Longitudinal Density Monitor (commonly known as the BSRL: Beam Synchrotron Radiation Longitudinal monitor) within the Beam Instrumentation Group.

In this role, you will lead the work on the online and offline data processing pipelines, which are fully implemented in Python. The analysis results from this instrument are mission-critical, directly impacting the calibration of the LHC experiments, facilitating the troubleshooting of operational issues, and enabling detailed beam physics analysis.

Additionally, you will play an important role in R&D and optimisation, conducting advanced studies to shape the upcoming upgrades of the BSRL instrument.

Your Responsibilities


Lead the redesign and optimisation of the current online and offline Python data analysis software for the BSRL instrument to ensure scalability, performance and reproducibility.
Develop specialised offline analysis routines using BSRL data, cross-correlating with other beam instrumentation where necessary, to meet the requirements of stakeholders.
Extend the online and offline diagnostics tools, architecting automated notification systems to detect early signs of instrument degradation, anomalies, or malfunctions.
Design and implement the specific software tools, calibration routines, and data pipelines required for the rapid commissioning of the renovated system after the Long Shutdown 3 (LS3).
Contribute to the validation and performance verification of the associated upgraded hardware planned for deployment during LS3.
Evaluate and benchmark different upgrade scenarios for LS3 by combining advanced data analysis of historical run data with physics-based simulation techniques.


Your profile


Proven background in the advanced analysis of complex experimental data, preferably from physics or large-scale scientific installations.
Extensive experience in software development with a strong emphasis on clean, maintainable code.
Basic experience in Monte Carlo-based physics simulation techniques.
Basic experience with laboratory work.


Skills


In-depth knowledge of advanced statistical methods, data modelling, and data analysis techniques applied to scientific datasets.
Proven proficiency in Python, including familiarity with the standard scientific stack (such as NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, etc) for high-performance processing.
Knowledge of version control systems (Git/Gilab) and continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD)
Basic knowledge of photon counting techniques would be advantage.
Spoken and written English, with a commitment to learn French.


Eligibility criteria:


You are a national of a CERN Member or Associate Member State.
You have a professional background in Applied Physics, Data Analytics (or a related field) and have either:
a Master's degree with 2 to 6 years of post-graduation professional experience;
or a PhD with no more than 3 years of post-graduation professional experience.
You have never had a CERN fellow or graduate contract before.


Additional Information

Job closing date: 24.08.2026 at 23:59 CEST.

Contract duration: 24 months, with a possible extension up to 36 months maximum.

Working hours: 40 hours per week

Job flexibility: Hybrid

Target start date: 01-December-2026

This position involves:


Work during nights, Sundays and official holidays, when required by the needs of the Organization.


Job reference: SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP

Field of work: Data Science & Data Analytics

Benchmark job: 200140 - Applied Physicist

Global Benefits


A monthly stipend between 6372-7004 Swiss Francs per month (tax free) depending on your degree.
30 days of paid leave per year plus 2 weeks annual closure.
Coverage by CERN’s comprehensive health insurance scheme (for yourself, your spouse and children), and membership of the CERN Pension Fund.
Family, child and infant monthly allowances depending on your individual circumstances.
A relocation package (installation grant and travel expenses) depending on your individual circumstances.
Possibility to extend your contract up to 36 months.
On-the-job and formal training including language classes.


Overview of CERN - Discover a world where the impossible is made possible!

At CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, we are pushing the frontiers of science and technology. Our groundbreaking work brings together not only physicists but also a diverse range of professionals from engineering, technical, scientific, and administrative fields. Together, we foster an environment where innovation and collaboration thrive.

Every day, we face exciting new challenges and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research that shapes our understanding of the universe. We meet these challenges through the diverse perspectives within our teams, ensuring every contribution is valued and driving our shared sense of inclusion and purpose. Diversity is a core value of CERN since its foundation, and it remains central to our mission and continued success.

If you are ready to be part of a dynamic, inclusive community pushing the boundaries of knowledge, CERN is the place where your curiosity and skills can thrive. Be part of our mission to uncover what lies at the heart of the universe! TAKE PART!

More information about us, here: careers.cern

  • Work during nights, Sundays and official holidays, when required by the needs of the Organization.

Job reference: SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP

Field of work: Data Science & Data Analytics

Benchmark job: 200140 - Applied Physicist

Global Benefits

  • A monthly stipend between 6372-7004 Swiss Francs per month (tax free) depending on your degree.
  • 30 days of paid leave per year plus 2 weeks annual closure.
  • Coverage by CERN’s comprehensive health insurance scheme (for yourself, your spouse and children), and membership of the CERN Pension Fund.
  • Family, child and infant monthly allowances depending on your individual circumstances.
  • A relocation package (installation grant and travel expenses) depending on your individual circumstances.
  • Possibility to extend your contract up to 36 months.
  • On-the-job and formal training including language classes.

Overview of CERN - Discover a world where the impossible is made possible!

At CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, we are pushing the frontiers of science and technology. Our groundbreaking work brings together not only physicists but also a diverse range of professionals from engineering, technical, scientific, and administrative fields. Together, we foster an environment where innovation and collaboration thrive.

Every day, we face exciting new challenges and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research that shapes our understanding of the universe. We meet these challenges through the diverse perspectives within our teams, ensuring every contribution is valued and driving our shared sense of inclusion and purpose. Diversity is a core value of CERN since its foundation, and it remains central to our mission and continued success.

If you are ready to be part of a dynamic, inclusive community pushing the boundaries of knowledge, CERN is the place where your curiosity and skills can thrive. Be part of our mission to uncover what lies at the heart of the universe! TAKE PART!

More information about us, here: careers.cern

Title: Analytics & Business Operations Associate
Description:
About Viscadia

Viscadia is a consulting firm whose mission is to empower life sciences companies with clarity and confidence through forecasting. With offices in the United States, Switzerland, and India, we are well-equipped to provide the support that global pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies need to make decisions quickly and with confidence. We are deeply committed to a vision of shared growth and success for our clients, colleagues, and communities.




Role Overview

The Analytics and Business Operations Associate will support the Viscadia's European and global needs across three areas: Global analytics and project delivery, business development and marketing support, and day-to-day Swiss business operations. Primary responsibilities may vary based on business priorities, but are expected to be approximately:

Global analytics & project delivery: ~50%
Business development & marketing support: ~30%
Swiss business operations: ~20%




Based in Lucerne, this is a broad, hands-on role for a professional who combines strong analytical ability with a proactive, well-organized approach. The incumbent will support global life sciences consulting engagements across forecasting and commercial insights while helping build and operate Viscadia's growing European presence. As part of a lean and growing Swiss team, this role suits a proactive, hands-on go-getter who takes ownership, is comfortable wearing multiple hats, and thrives with limited oversight in a fast-moving environment.




This position reports directly to the Associate Principal based in Lucerne, who leads Viscadia's presence in the region.




Key Responsibilities:




Global Analytics & Project Delivery

Work as part of active client engagement teams, supporting the development and maintenance of forecasting and analytics models for client engagements, including data gathering, structuring, and quality checks.
Conduct secondary research and synthesize insights on therapeutic areas, disease states, treatment paradigms, competitive landscapes, and pharmaceutical market dynamics into clear, actionable summaries.
Build and format analyses, charts, and presentations that translate findings into clear, evidence-based narratives for client and internal audiences.
Support engagement managers and senior leadership on project tasks, ensuring accuracy, thoroughness, and on-time delivery.
Collaborate with global delivery teams in the United States and India across time zones to provide seamless, round-the-clock client support.




Business Development & Marketing Support

Research pharmaceutical companies, therapeutic areas, licensing opportunities and strategic accounts to support business development initiatives across Europe.
Support the preparation of proposals, pitch materials, case studies, and capability demonstrations.
Maintain CRM records and track the pipeline of leads, opportunities, and follow-ups.
Support marketing activities, including content, collateral, conference planning and execution, LinkedIn and digital presence, and webinar coordination.
Help organize client meetings, conferences, and regional networking activities.




Operations & Administration

Coordinate and support the day-to-day operations of the Swiss office, ensuring efficient and reliable running.
Support compliance, documentation, and coordination with local authorities and service providers.
Maintain organized records and support scheduling, travel, and general administrative needs.
Liaise with banks, accountants, legal counsel, government authorities, immigration partners and other external service providers.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, Life Sciences, Data Analytics or a related quantitative field.
Master’s degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, Life Sciences, Data Analytics or a related quantitative field will be preferred.
1-3 years of relevant experience in consulting, analytics, forecasting, pharma or life sciences, data, or related environments. Internship experience is valued.
Strong analytical and structured problem-solving capabilities.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Working familiarity with Python, SQL, VBA, dashboarding tools, or analytics platforms is preferred.
Exposure to generative AI and productivity tools to improve research, analysis, presentation development and operational efficiency.
Interest in healthcare, pharmaceuticals, forecasting, analytics, or consulting environments.
Excellent organization, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities independently in a fast-paced environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to explain complex analyses clearly.
Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment while demonstrating initiative and ownership.
Self-starter with a strong work ethic, curiosity, adaptability, and eagerness to learn.
German is advantageous but not required for local business development and administration.
Authorized to work in Switzerland requiring no visa sponsorships.




Employment Terms

Location: Lucerne, Switzerland (On-site with hybrid working arrangement with an expectation of 3 days regular presence in office).
Part-time 75%, with the possibility of increasing to 100% subject to business needs and individual performance.
Occasional travel within Europe to support client meetings, conferences and business development activities.




Why Join Viscadia?

A high-impact, intellectually stimulating environment with direct exposure to senior leadership and global life sciences clients.
Gain exposure to forecasting, commercial strategy, business development, and Swiss business operations.
Develop consulting, analytics, and client-facing skills in a high-growth environment.
Competitive compensation complemented by performance-based recognition that rewards excellence, innovation, and ownership
Global exposure and a collaborative culture, working with cross-functional teams across the United States, Switzerland, and India
Structured opportunities to grow across analytics, commercial, and operations, with a clear pathway to broaden scope and responsibility over time.




Viscadia is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We encourage applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, marital status, disability, or veteran status.

Title: Controller & Data Analyst junior (m/f/x)
Description:
Your Responsibilities

As a Junior Controller & Data Analyst (m/f/x), you monitor expenses by cost center,
You analyze and comment on profit and loss variances (financial and analytical),
You support the preparation of budgets by cost center,
You participate in monthly closings as well as in the preparation of future plans,
You develop KPIs and performance BI reports/tools in collaboration with the company’s functional managers,
You carry out specific analyses and drive optimization initiatives,


Your Profil

You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, or a related field,
You have at least 2 to 3 years of experience in controlling or data analysis, ideally in an industrial environment,
You are fluent in French and English and have good knowledge of German,
You are proficient in the MS Office suite and have solid knowledge of an ERP system,
You are autonomous, flexible, and able to work effectively within a team,
You stand out through your analytical skills, communication abilities, and your capacity to assert yourself.


Our Offer
Can we inspire you to take on a responsible position in a dynamic and independent family business? We offer you room for ideas, a secure job, modern and family-friendly employment conditions such as flexible working hours, home office, a company restaurant, training opportunities as well as many benefits. The workplace is located in Bulle, surrounded by the wonderful nature of the Gruyère region. Start planning your personal and professional development with us.
In order to ensure a fair application process, we cannot accept applications by post or email.
This position is subject to a background check process.

Have we awoken your interest? Then we look forward to receiving your online application. If you have any questions, please contact Nadege MALAN.
One Passion. Many Opportunities.

The Company
Liebherr Machines Bulle SA is the centre of excellence for combustion engines (diesel and gas engines) and hydraulic components (axial piston pumps and motors). The company develops and produces various products, which find their use in building construction and civil engineering, mining, agriculture, decentralised energy, machine and plant engineering, as well as aerospace.

Location
Liebherr Machines Bulle SA
7, Rue Hans-Liebherr
1630 Bulle
Switzerland (CH)

Contact
Nadege MALAN
026 913 31 11

Title: Junior Data/AI Engineer
Description:
JUNIOR DATA/AI ENGINEER

Fresh out of school, itching to ship real things? Read on.

12-month fixed-term contract - a full year to learn fast, protect real systems, and build a foundation for your career.

Location: Geneva or Lausanne - You choose




About Alpian

We're building the next generation of private banking, digital, personalized, and actually meaningful.

Alpian is the first Swiss digital bank, combining wealth management and everyday banking in a single mobile app. Our mission? Make investing and banking simple, intuitive, and accessible without ever compromising on security, trust, or excellence.

We're a small team of bold thinkers and pragmatic engineers who like solving hard problems for real users, in production. No ivory towers, no endless demos that go nowhere.




Why this role is different

Most junior jobs hand you a ticket queue and a "good luck." Not here.

You'll learn to build AI systems that thousands of real customers actually use, not toy chatbots, but agents and data pipelines running in a regulated Swiss bank. You'll sit at the intersection of LLMs, analytics, and cloud infrastructure, mentored by people who've taken AI from proof-of-concept to production (and lived to tell the war stories).

If you graduated recently and you're the kind of person who breaks things to understand them, debugs your own code before asking for the answer, and gets a little too excited about a clean data model , you'll feel right at home.




The AI part

Here's the deal: you're joining at the exact moment AI stops being hype and starts being infrastructure.

You'll help us build agents that reason over financial data, RAG pipelines that actually ground their answers, and LLM systems that have to be correct, explainable, and auditable, because "the model said so" doesn't fly when a regulator asks.

You won't just use AI. You'll learn to make it trustworthy. That's the rare skill, and it's the one that will define your career.




What you'll be doing

You'll grow into this, we don't expect all of it on day one:

Write clean, tested Python that ends up in production (not in a drawer)
Help build agentic workflows: tool calling, retries, handling the inevitable failures
Work on RAG pipelines, chunking, embeddings, retrieval that actually retrieves the right thing
Query and model data in BigQuery, and learn why analytical correctness matters
Add the unglamorous-but-critical stuff: tracing, evaluations, cost and latency monitoring
Learn to handle customer data the right way, PII, access control, auditability
Document what you build and explain your trade-offs clearly (bonus points for zero buzzwords)




What we're looking for

We're hiring for potential and attitude, not a decade of experience.

A recent degree in Computer Science, Data, Engineering, or a related field (or a self-taught path that proves you can build)
Solid Python foundations, you've written real programs, not just notebook snippets
Genuine curiosity about AI, LLMs, and data, some hands-on tinkering (projects, internships, hackathons, side projects) counts a lot
Comfort with SQL, or eagerness to get there fast
The instinct to debug your own code and figure things out before reaching for help
Clear communication and the humility to ask good questions
You care about why something works, not just that it works




Nice to have (truly optional)

A personal project involving an LLM, an API, or a dataset you found interesting
Any exposure to Google Cloud, containers, or Git workflows
A hackathon, open-source contribution, or that one weekend project you can't stop talking about
Curiosity about regulated environments and why "move fast and break things" doesn't always apply to banks




Our stack

Google ADK · Vertex AI · BigQuery · Grafana · Python (async, APIs, testing) · vector databases & embedding models · GCP · CI/CD, containers, IAM, secrets management

Don't know all of these? Neither did we, once. You'll learn them here.




What you'll get

Real ownership of real systems, early
Mentorship from senior engineers who actually want to teach
A front-row seat to production AI in a domain where the stakes are real
A small, ambitious team where your work is visible and your ideas count







Ready to build the future of banking? We'd love to see what you've made, projects, repos, and enthusiasm welcome alongside your CV.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Tasks & Responsibilities

Analytics & Solution Development

Develop innovative analytical solutions to address complex business challenges.
Explore large datasets to identify trends, opportunities, and actionable insights.
Apply statistical and AI-based techniques to support decision-making processes.

Business Collaboration

Work closely with technical teams and business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into analytical solutions.
Present findings and recommendations in a clear and understandable manner.
Support departments in identifying new opportunities to leverage data effectively.

Technology & Continuous Improvement

Contribute to the design, implementation, and enhancement of analytical applications.
Evaluate emerging technologies and recommend improvements to existing approaches.
Help establish best practices for analytics development and knowledge sharing within the team.




Must-Have

University degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, Data Analytics, or a related field.
Professional experience delivering analytical or AI-based solutions in a business environment.
Strong programming skills in Python.
Experience working with SQL and relational databases.
Knowledge of statistical analysis, machine learning techniques, or artificial intelligence concepts.
Experience using common Python libraries for analytics and data processing.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Fluent German and good English.




Nice-to-Have

Experience working in technology-driven or regulated environments.
Exposure to cloud-based analytics platforms.
Familiarity with software development best practices and version control.
Interest in emerging AI technologies and their practical application.

Title: Data Analyst Supply chain & Service client
HTML:
QoQa c'est avant tout près d'un million de membres de notre magnifique Qommunauté qui découvrent chaque jour des offres dénichées par nos équipes. Notre mission: émerveiller au quotidien.


Notre succès, nous le devons au formidable travail de nos plus de 240 loutres qui s'engagent quotidiennement pour proposer des expériences, des découvertes et un service au top.


Aujourd'hui, nous recherchons la perle rare pour rejoindre l'équipe Data!


Ta mission, si tu l'acceptes:

Ton objectif : transformer la donnée brute en levier stratégique afin de soutenir les départements Opérations (supply chain et service client). Tu garantiras la fiabilité des indicateurs clés pour permettre un pilotage proactif et éclairé de notre organisation.


  • Maîtrise complète de la pipeline : Tu pilotes l'intégralité du cycle de vie de la donnée, de la préparation de l'extraction (en collaboration avec les Data Engineers) depuis le DWH jusqu'à la mise en production de dashboards dans notre outil BI;
  • Partenaire Business : En étroite collaboration avec les équipes métiers, tu traduis leurs enjeux opérationnels en solutions analytiques concrètes;
  • Projets : Tu participeras également à différents projets transverses de l'équipe, selon les priorités du moment.


Parlons de toi et de tes compétences:


Ton expérience - Ta formation

  • Bachelor ou Master en informatique, gestion des données, ingénierie ou domaine équivalent;
  • Minimum 7 ans d'expérience confirmée en Data Analysis / BI, incluant une expertise approfondie des flux Supply Chain (stocks, approvisionnements) et du Service Client (KPIs de satisfaction, suivi de la performance des services de support).


Tes compétences - Tes qualités

  • Excellente maîtrise de SQL et des bases de données relationnelles;
  • Expérience confirmée avec DBT (Data Build Tool) et maîtrise de Github pour le versioning;
  • Bonne maîtrise Tableau Software, pour transformer les données en rapports clairs et percutantsM
  • Une connaissance approfondie de l'ERP Odoo (structure des tables sous-jacentes) est un atout majeur;
  • Approche rigoureuse et orientée résultats;
  • Capacité à collaborer avec les métiers, à comprendre leurs besoins et à concevoir des solutions pertinentes.


Le plus : ton appétence pour l'IA et les outils modernes est sans limite!


Pour que tu restes au taQuet, on t'offre:

  • Un environnement sain, où tu peux être toi même;
  • Une ambiance et un cadre de travail agréables dans notre QG;
  • Un temps de travail flexible, et hybride;
  • Un système de gouvernance en Holacratie;
  • Des avantages sociaux aux petits oignons (5 semaines de vacances, LPP avantageuse, congé maternité et paternité au-delà du minimum légal, etc.);
  • Des rabais et contributions financières: plan de mobilité, abonnement téléphone, repas de midi, sport, etc.);
  • La possibilité de formation continue et de développement.


Les prochaines étapes

Tu as ce qu'il faut pour relever ce défi ?


Pour entrer dans la course, envoie nous au minimum un CV et une lettre ou vidéo de motivation personnalisée. Fais parler ta créativité et raconte-nous pourquoi tu serais la personne idéale pour ce job. Quels sont tes super-pouvoirs: Surprends-nous!


Nous te remercions (et la planète aussi) de ne pas envoyer de candidature papier.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Tradition is the interdealer broking arm of Compagnie Financière Tradition and one of the world's largest interdealer brokers in over-the-counter financial and commodity related products. CFT is represented in over 28 countries, employing over 2,500 people.




Tradition’s goal is to provide superior client services. It believes its business success is a direct reflection of its employees and recruits. As such, teamwork, creativity, reliability and integrity are components of a work ethic taken very seriously since the company was founded in 1959.




Tradition is currently seeking to appoint a Data Engineer to be based in our Lausanne office.




Main responsibilities within the Data Engineer position include:




Data Modelling & Business Analysis

Translate business concepts into scalable, well-structured transformation pipelines and analytical models.
Design and build trusted, reusable, and well-documented data products that are discoverable, reliable, and support multiple use cases.
Data product mindset: ability to think in terms of reusable, well-defined, consumer-focused datasets.
Drive data quality, consistency, and modelling best practices.




Monitoring & Platform Reliability

Own and continuously improve the monitoring and observability ecosystem.
Proactively identify issues, performance bottlenecks, and data quality concerns.
Develop alerts, dashboards, and operational procedures that enable rapid issue detection and resolution.




Application Support & Incident Management

Investigate and resolve production incidents across the data platform.
Support a growing user base by responding to operational and business requests.
Collaborate with engineering teams to identify root causes and implement long-term solutions.
Help maintain the reliability and availability of critical data services




Key skills and experience required in this role:

3+ years of experience in Data Engineering, Software Engineering, or a related field.
Proven experience supporting production systems and monitoring data pipelines or applications, as well as building and maintaining analytical data models.
Strong experience with SQL, Python, dbt, Docker, Kubernetes, OpenSearch or similar logging tools, Prometheus and Grafana (or equivalent observability tools), and cloud-native data platforms and modern data architectures.
Ability to translate complex business needs into data models and products, with a strong focus on business value; knowledge of dimensional modeling and analytics engineering is a plus.
Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field.
Excellent English communication skills; proactive, collaborative, and self-driven with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.




Tradition do not accept agency CV’s. Please do not forward CV’s to our employees or Talent team. Tradition are not accountable for any fees related to unsolicited resumes. The Talent team will reach out to trusted agents when required.

Please note, due to the large volume of applications for this position, only suitable candidates will be contacted. If you have not heard from us within 14 days, unfortunately, your application has been unsuccessful.

Tradition welcome all suitable applications and are an equal opportunity employer who value diversity. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications and merit.

By applying for this role, you agree that we may retain your details on our system for a period of 6 months and may contact you for any future vacancies that may arise within the Tradition Group.

Title: Document Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Document Reviewer

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.

Key Responsibilities


Carefully review large volumes of structured and unstructured data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Identify and flag issues related to data quality, consistency, and potential risks involving Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Annotate, classify, and, where necessary, redact sensitive information to maintain privacy and compliance standards.
Follow strict guidelines, taxonomies, and workflow processes for data categorization and validation.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks to verify data integrity and validate outputs.
Maintain detailed records of reviewed documents and actions taken to ensure traceability and accountability.
Collaborate with the customer's team, providing clear and concise written and verbal communication about findings and recommendations.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience in document review, auditing, annotation, redaction, or data classification at scale.
Exceptional attention to detail with a high level of accuracy in repetitive tasks.
Familiarity with handling and protecting Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to document findings and communicate effectively with team members.
Demonstrated ability to follow complex guidelines and adhere to established taxonomies.
Experience performing QA or validation work on large data sets.
Ability to balance efficiency with precision in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in legal, compliance, privacy, auditing, compliance or information security domains.
Prior experience supporting data-driven AI or machine learning projects.
Comfort with collaboration tools and digital workflow management platforms.

Title: Data Engineer (Palantir)
Description:
Who we are




SII Group is a trusted technology partner, SII provides high value added solutions for the IT projects of many large corporations. Since its founding in 1979, the Company has been providing solutions adapted to its clients’ needs, by relying on :




> Its acknowledged expertise in various industries and sectors

> Proven 'turnkey' solutions

> An efficient quality system

> Adaptable, evolving services




Today, with a staff of more than 18'000, SII Group is supporting companies across 20 countries.




At SII Switzerland we pay attention to the personal and professional wellbeing of our employees. We place our collaborators at the heart of our actions and activities. If you are motivated by the perspective of joining a big, trusted and recognized group, then let's meet !




To develop our consulting offer and support one of our clients, we are looking for a talented Data Engineer (Palantir).




Missions

Build Pipelines in Foundry: You develop and scale data pipelines (ETL/ELT) directly within Palantir Foundry (e.g., via Code Repositories, Pipeline Builder) and integrate data from a wide variety of source systems.
Data Modeling & Ontology: You help map our data into the Foundry Ontology, making complex business logic tangible and accessible for analysts and end-users.
Data Quality & Lineage: You implement health checks, monitor data quality across the entire data lineage process, and ensure that pipelines run stably.
Code & Transformation: You write clean, efficient code (primarily in PySpark and SQL) to clean and transform large volumes of data.
Team Collaboration: You work closely with Data Scientists, Analysts, and business departments to translate their requirements into functioning data models within Foundry.




Profile

Initial professional experience in Data Engineering (as a Junior, e.g., through relevant internships/working student roles; as a Mid-Level, approx. 1–3 years in a permanent role).
Good to excellent knowledge of Python (ideally experience with PySpark or Pandas) and are highly proficient in SQL.
Prior experience with Foundry (e.g., Contour, Code Repositories, Ontology) is a massive plus. If not: You have a strong foundation in data processing and are highly motivated to dive deep into the Palantir ecosystem.
Solid basic understanding of relational databases, data architectures, and the challenges of working with distributed systems (Big Data).
Solution-oriented, think analytically, and enjoy diving into complex datasets and learning new tools.




For information

Start date : September
Location : Zurich - CH
Duration : permanent contract
Language : fluent in English, German is a plus

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Who we are




SII Group is a trusted technology partner, SII provides high value added solutions for the IT projects of many large corporations. Since its founding in 1979, the Company has been providing solutions adapted to its clients’ needs, by relying on :




> Its acknowledged expertise in various industries and sectors

At SII Switzerland we pay attention to the personal and professional wellbeing of our employees. We place our collaborators at the heart of our actions and activities. If you are motivated by the perspective of joining a big, trusted and recognized group, then let's meet !




To develop our consulting offer and support one of our clients, we are looking for a talented Data Engineer.




Missions

Develop & Scale Pipelines: You design and implement robust ETL/ELT pipelines to process large, complex volumes of data (Big Data) using both code-based and visual tools.
Semantic Data Modeling: You translate complex business processes into logical data models and help build a centralized, cross-departmental enterprise data model.
Data Quality & Lineage: You implement automated quality checks and monitor data flows from source to end product to guarantee seamless data lineage.
Code & Transformation: You write clean, high-performance code in PySpark and SQL to clean, aggregate, and prepare data for downstream analysis.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: You work closely with Data Scientists, Business Analysts, and business departments to quickly translate their requirements into production-ready data structures.




Profile

Initial practical experience in Data Engineering (as a Junior, e.g., through relevant internships/working student roles; as a Mid-Level, approx. 1–3 years of professional experience).
Solid knowledge of Python (especially in a Big Data context with PySpark) as well as proficiency in writing complex SQL queries.
Good understanding of modern data architectures (Data Lakehouse, distributed systems) and ideally experience with integrated all-in-one data platforms.
Familiar with relational and non-relational databases. You enjoy bringing order to unstructured mountains of data.
Analytical mindset, work in a structured manner, and possess a strong willingness to learn, allowing you to quickly adapt to new, complex software ecosystems.




For information

Start date : September
Location : Zurich - CH
Duration : permanent contract
Language : fluent in English, German is a plus

Title: Business Analyst / Consultant - Artificial Intelligence & Data - (AI & Insights)
Description:
Job Description

Location: Zurich, Geneva

Work Pattern Percentage: 80-100%

Help our clients navigate the future with confidence and embrace decision making with clear data-driven choices

Deloitte AI & Data helps clients unlock the power of AI and data to deliver better human-centric solutions that catalyze growth and efficiencies. We enable them to leverage data, analytics, modern platforms, and data science & AI technologies to uncover hidden relationships from vast troves of data, generate insights, and inform their decision-making.

We help clients transform their business by designing and implementing data solutions to excel in their markets. Our role is to understand their business problems and use data-driven thinking and technology to achieve outcomes that drive their business forward.

This role is open to candidates with either an AI/Analytics or a Data Engineering/Data Platform background and preference. During the interview process, we will determine the best fit for you within one of these specialized subteams.

We also welcome applications from trainee candidates with limited work experience, provided they are available for full-time work (not returning to school). After 4–6 months in a trainee role, we will reassess readiness for a Business Analyst role.

For this role, we are currently offering start dates from February 2026.

How You Can Make An Impact


Design and implement AI and data strategies and modern solutions – including data management, platforms and AI use cases – that enable clients to achieve their business objectives across regulatory, cost and revenue pillars.
Provide architectural, technical and organizational thought leadership to guide complex data and AI transformations with significant organizational implications.
Drive Data & AI use cases from business value concept to production.
Build and maintain trusted client relationships through project delivery and business development, expanding your network internally and externally.
Support business development opportunities to generate new projects.
Be a role model for people you work with by demonstrating strong work ethic, leadership, and subject matter competence.
Continuously grow your knowledge across industries and share insights with Deloitte talent.



Who We Are Looking For

Drive and Motivation:


Passionate individuals eager to build a career in AI & Data Consulting.
Strong drive to learn, master new topics and manage multiple activities independently in a fast-paced environment.



Consulting and Communication Skills:


Strong writing and Microsoft Power Point skills: Ability to design and shape meaningful presentations.
Strong Conceptual Capability: Ability to consolidate diverse requirements into coherent solution designs, simplifying complex problems.
Business-IT Translator: Ability to act as the bridge between business needs and technical solutions.
Attention to detail and ability to communicate technical specifics clearly in both written and oral forms.



Technical expertise:


First practical experience with AI & analytics solutions is highly recommended.
Familiarity with SQL and Python is a plus.
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure or GCP, including working with Large Language Models (LLMs) and related services is a plus.
Knowledge of data platforms tools (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Dataiku) and data visualisation tools (e.g., PowerBI, Tableau) is a plus.
Strong engineering or mathematics background is advantageous.



Language Proficiency:


Fluent in English; fluency in German and/or French is highly preferred to facilitate communication with diverse teams and stakeholders.



Industry Experience:


First relevant experience in consulting, tech companies, or industry-specific roles, particularly in AI & data-driven fields.
Previous experience in the Banking, Insurance, Life Science or Healthcare sector is advantageous.



Your team

Become part of a diverse and multinational team of 50+ highly motivated individuals located in our Zurich or Geneva offices. We specialise in AI & data – how to structure it, how to source it, how to transform it, how to store it, and how to consume it. Join our fast-paced and fun team, working alongside highly talented colleagues on varied and exciting projects, with access to excellent and continuous learning opportunities.

Deloitte AI & Data is the leading data & analytics consulting team in Switzerland. You will have a chance to work with outstanding colleagues and have access to variety of clients, vendors, and technologies. Our business is growing fast and there are plenty market opportunities to develop career in the direction that you want.

We will help you grow both personally and professionally: through our trainings, mentoring programmes, and on-the-job learning, but also by working with clients and vendors across different industries.

Life at Deloitte

At Deloitte, your ideas create impact and spark meaningful change for our clients, people and society. Your unique expertise, background and perspective helps us find new ways into the most complex challenges so we can brighten the futures of those our work affects. At Deloitte, you can build a career that inspires and energises you. At Deloitte, you can choose your impact.

In Switzerland, we provide industry-specific services in the areas of Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Strategy, Risk & Transactions Advisory, and Technology & Transformation. With close to 3,000 employees at six locations, we serve companies and organisations of all sizes in all industry sectors. We offer meaningful and challenging work, an inclusive and supportive environment where you can be your best self and where you will never stop growing. You will benefit from flexible and hybrid working conditions, wellbeing and parental programmes as well as many opportunities to connect, collaborate and learn.

Join us and become part of a global network of like-minded people dedicated to making a difference, whether by addressing climate change or improving 100 million futures by 2030. Want to know more about opportunities at Deloitte? Visit www.apply.deloitte.ch .

How To Apply

We look forward to hearing from you! Please click on “apply now” to submit a complete application, including CV, cover letter, references and degree certificates.

If you have any questions, please contact Aarabi at talent@deloitte.ch


We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, ethnicity, religious beliefs, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age or disability, in accordance with applicable law. Research shows that women are less likely to apply for roles unless they match all the criteria; don’t hold yourself back – apply today.



© 2026. For information, contact Deloitte AG. All rights reserved. Deloitte AG is an equal opportunities employer.

Title: CSV Engineer
Description:
Did you ever want to work for a company placing human at the heart of its DNA? Are you ready to make a difference? Do you feel excited about the opportunity to collaborate and share your expertise with our industry leaders? YES? SO DO WE!




GXP CONSULTING Switzerland is a Swiss company with a passionate team of Life Sciences professionals. Since 2014 we deliver outstanding industry’s expertise and support to our clients in the complete range of product life cycle.




In order to continue to successfully support our clients, we are currently looking for a CSV Engineer (IT/System or Automated Equipment) with solid experience in GMP environments to support implementation and lifecycle management of systems in production, laboratories, quality, or supply chain settings to work in Switzerland (Lausanne area):




About the job :




Perform risk assessments, GxP impact evaluations, and define validation strategies (CSV plans).

Title: Data Engineer (H/F)
Description:
Chez 𝗖𝗕𝗧𝗪 (Collaboration Betters The World), entreprise mondiale de conseil en technologie, la collaboration est la pierre angulaire de l’innovation et du succès, et le fondement de tout ce que nous mettons en œuvre.

Notre mission : transformer les entreprises et les communautés pour un monde meilleur.

Présents dans plus de 20 pays et 35 villes, nous allions une expertise mondiale avec une compréhension locale des enjeux de nos clients.




Au sein de la Service Line "Data Analytics & IA", nous développons une approche qui vise à accompagner nos clients dans la définition de leurs stratégies en matière de Data Science et d’IA, d’Analytics et Business Intelligence, de Data et plateformes. L’objectif est de les accompagner à chaque étape de leurs parcours afin de les aider à libérer le potentiel de leurs données.




Que vas-tu faire ?




Concrètement au sein de l'expertise Data Engineering, tu seras en charge de :




1. Conception & Architecture

Concevoir des architectures Data scalables et sécurisées : Data Lake, Data Warehouse.
Réaliser des estimations de coûts et comparer objectivement différentes technologies et architectures.
Participer aux choix technologiques en fonction des enjeux métiers et contraintes techniques.
Définir et promouvoir les bonnes pratiques d’ingénierie et d’architecture Data.




2. Développement & Industrialisation

Construire et industrialiser des pipelines batch et temps réel.
Implémenter des pratiques CI/CD, observabilité, gouvernance et data quality.







Quel est ton profil ?




Tu as une expérience significative de plus de 7 ans en data engineering.
Tu es issu d’une formation dans le domaine de la Data niveau Bac +5/Ecole d'ingénieur
Tu détiens une expertise sur une Data Platform moderne idéalement Data Bricks ou Microsoft Fabric
Tu maîtrises un cloud public (Azure, AWS ou GCP).
Tu as de très bonnes compétences en SQL et Python.
Tu disposes de solides connaissances en architecture Data moderne.
Tu as une forte aisance relationnelle notamment pour l'animation d'atelier variés (conception, ateliers agiles, retros et cadrages).




Notre processus de recrutement :

1ère étape : Rencontre avec un membre de l’équipe RH pour introduire le poste, l’environnement de travail et comprendre tes motivations ;

2ème étape : Rencontre avec les équipes de la Service Line pour faire connaissance mais aussi te challenger techniquement ;

3ème étape : Rencontre avec un membre de la direction et l’un de nos sales pour te challenger sur ta compréhension de CBTW, le partage de valeurs communes et ta motivation pour nous rejoindre.




Ce que nous te proposons :

Collaborer au sein d'une entreprise internationale dynamique et ouverte d'esprit où tes idées sont entendues et valorisées ;
Accéder à des perspectives d’évolution professionnelle tout au long de ta carrière via des formations et des certifications ;
Bénéficier d'un suivi de carrière sur mesure avec ton référent RH et des points réguliers avec ton Business Manager lors de tes missions ;
Intégrer des communautés te permettant de partager des centres d'intérêts communs ou expertises techniques ;
Créer du lien avec des collaborateurs passionnés à travers différents évènements internes (afterworks, soirées annuelles, séminaires, …).




Nous avons hâte d'échanger avec toi !

Title: Strategic Insights Specialist
Description:
At Roche you can show up as yourself, embraced for the unique qualities you bring. Our culture encourages personal expression, open dialogue, and genuine connections, where you are valued, accepted and respected for who you are, allowing you to thrive both personally and professionally. This is how we aim to prevent, stop and cure diseases and ensure everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Join Roche, where every voice matters.

The Position

The Strategy Deployment Office shapes and runs the next generation of Portfolio and Performance monitoring for PT, designing a lean, AI-enabled system that gives leadership a real-time view of how our strategic initiatives and operational performance are progressing. This is a builder role with significant room to shape the approach, adopt new technologies early, and grow your impact as the system and process scale. You will have direct exposure to senior leadership, the freedom to experiment with AI-enabled solutions, and the chance to shape how PT monitors and steers its strategy.

The Opportunity


Portfolio monitoring: Design, build and maintain a lean system to track the status, risks, and value of PT’s strategic initiatives, giving leadership clear and timely visibility
Performance monitoring: Develop and maintain KPI frameworks and dashboards that track delivery against our performance promises, enabling just-in-time reporting instead of periodic, manual updates
AI & Automation: Identify, pilot and embed AI-enabled tools to automate data collection, reporting and insight generation, reducing manual effort and increasing speed and accuracy
Community Building: Be an ambassador for a network of contributors and champions across Operations who feed, trust and use the system, ensuring data quality and broad adoption
Process simplification: Continuously assess the maturity of existing monitoring processes, removing complexity and standardizing where it adds value, without overengineering
Insights delivery: translate data into clear, actionable insights and visual storytelling for PT stakeholders
Governance Cadence Support: feed the monitoring system supporting a regular governance rhythm (e.g. monthly and quarterly reviews), ensuring leadership has the right data, timely, ahead of key decisions
Communication of insights: prepare clear concise updates and materials that translate system outputs into a narrative leadership can act on, in coordination with SDO communication needs


Who You Are

You are a motivated, self-starting talent with a lean mindset who simplifies before automating, and automates before adding resources. You are a strong communicator who turns data into clear, simple, and visual stories, and a natural connector able to energize a community around a shared system. As a team player, you collaborate easily across functions and seniority levels, bringing high attention to detail balanced with pragmatism, speed, and a deep curiosity to test new AI and digital tools in a builder environment.

Furthermore, you possess the following qualifications and experiences:


MSc in Engineering, Life Science, Business Administration, Data/Analytics or related fields
2–5 years of experience in business analytics, operations, PMO, or performance management, ideally in pharma, life science, or a related industry - Site experience is a plus
Hands-on experience with data visualization or Business Intelligence tools
Familiarity with Lean and Project Management (certification is a plus)
Exposure to or strong interest in AI and automation tools applied to reporting and process design
Experience working in a complex organization with multiple stakeholders across functions and regions, including exposure to governance cadences (e.g., monthly/quarterly business reviews)


Are you ready to make a difference and help build our portfolio monitoring from the ground up? We are looking forward to your application – join us now and become part of our journey!

Where pay transparency applies, details are provided based on the primary posting location. For this role, the primary location is Basel. If you are interested in additional locations where the role may be available, we will provide the relevant compensation details later in the hiring process.

Who we are

A healthier future drives us to innovate. Together, more than 100’000 employees across the globe are dedicated to advance science, ensuring everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Our efforts result in more than 26 million people treated with our medicines and over 30 billion tests conducted using our Diagnostics products. We empower each other to explore new possibilities, foster creativity, and keep our ambitions high, so we can deliver life-changing healthcare solutions that make a global impact.

Let’s build a healthier future, together.

Roche is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Title: Data Engineer - 884308/1
Description:
We are looking for a Data Engineer for our client in Zürich, Switzerland.




Key Information:

Project start: Immediately
Project duration: 6 months with option for extension
Location: Zürich
Workload: 80-100%, 1-2 Days Homeoffice










Responsibilities







Design, develop, and maintain data pipelines for processing production and test data.
Analyze, validate, and ensure the quality of product and process data to meet technical specifications.
Process large volumes of data generated from industrial production environments and testing systems.
Integrate, transform, and prepare structured and unstructured data sources for analysis and downstream applications.
Develop and optimize ETL processes for automated data processing and integration.
Implement Data Engineering and Data Science solutions across the entire data lifecycle.
Collaborate with production, engineering, and cross-functional teams to identify data-driven optimization opportunities.
Develop Python-based applications, tools, and scripts for data analysis, automation, and process improvement.
Ensure software quality through version control, documentation, and adherence to software development best practices.
Support the further development and optimization of the existing data architecture and big data environment.
Utilize and enhance workflow orchestration and data pipeline solutions (e.g., Apache Airflow, Apache NiFi).
Analyze complex datasets from machines, manufacturing systems, and test equipment to generate actionable and reliable insights.










Required Skills







Several years of hands-on experience in Data Engineering, Data Science, or Software Development.
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a comparable technical discipline.
Strong Python programming skills with a focus on data processing, automation, and data pipeline development.
Experience designing, implementing, and maintaining ETL processes and data integration solutions.
Proficient in Git and modern software version control systems.
Experience working with large-scale datasets and complex data environments.
Professional experience in an industrial setting, ideally within manufacturing, mechanical engineering, or electrical engineering.
Fluent English skills, both written and spoken; German language skills would be considered a strong advantage.
