Title: Junior Data Engineer (a)
Description:
Imagine using your data engineering skills to improve the way cardiovascular health is monitored and managed. Hilo by Aktiia is redefining blood pressure monitoring through AI-driven optical technology built on more than 20 years of research at the Swiss Center for Electronics and Microtechnology (CSEM). Their solution combines a wearable device (Hilo), a mobile app, and a cloud-based platform for healthcare professionals — empowering users and physicians with continuous, actionable insights into blood-pressure patterns. With more than 200,000 users, over $120M in funding, and a CE-certified medical device already available across several markets, Aktiia is continuing to scale its technology, product ecosystem, and international presence.




As Aktiia expands its data capabilities, we’re looking for a Junior Data Engineer who will help scale a Databricks-based medallion Lakehouse from a primarily R&D/Core Tech setup into a broader company-wide data platform. Working closely with and learning directly from an experienced Senior Data Engineer, you will support the ingestion, centralization, documentation, and validation of clinical, legacy, and operational data. Your work will help transform complex and sometimes messy data sources into clean, reliable, and model-ready pipelines used by Data Science, ML, Algorithm, and Core Tech teams.




Your Role

Data Ingestion & Lakehouse Development: Design, build, and maintain data ingestion and processing pipelines within Aktiia’s Databricks-hosted medallion Lakehouse, working under senior guidance while gradually taking on more ownership.
Clinical & Legacy Data Integration: Take an active role in ingesting, centralizing, and documenting clinical data currently spread across EDC platforms such as Castor and RedCap, databases, standalone archives, and other historically grown sources.
Data Exploration & Practical Data Archaeology: Work hands-on with unfamiliar and sometimes messy datasets, identify structures and inconsistencies, and turn unstructured situations into reliable, usable data assets.
Pipeline Development & Preprocessing: Build preprocessing and ETL/ELT pipelines that provide clean, structured, and model-ready datasets for Algorithm Development, Core Tech, Machine Learning, and Data Science teams.
Data Quality, Validation & Documentation: Define and apply practical standards for data quality, validation, traceability, and documentation — especially for sensitive and clinically relevant datasets.
Observability & Engineering Practices: Implement logging, validation checks, alerting, and basic observability for new pipelines, while contributing to shared codebase practices such as Git, code reviews, CI/CD, and testing.
Platform Scaling & Collaboration: Support the evolution of the lakehouse from selected technical use cases toward a company-wide data infrastructure, working closely with the Senior Data Engineer, ML Engineers, Data Scientists, and cross-functional stakeholders.




Your Profile

Academic Background: Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Data Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
Professional Expertise: At least 1+ year of practical post-study experience in data engineering, data infrastructure, data ingestion, or a similar hands-on technical role. You may still be early in your career, but you have already worked with real data pipelines, production-oriented data workflows, or collaborative data platforms.
Technical Experience: You have solid hands-on experience with Python and SQL and bring practical experience with Databricks. You are familiar with cloud environments — ideally AWS. You understand the basics of ETL/ELT pipeline design, data ingestion patterns, and data modelling, and you have worked with shared codebases using Git, code reviews, CI/CD, or testing practices. Experience with Spark, Delta Lake, or Parquet is a strong plus.
Industry Fit: Ideally, you have gained experience in a start-up, scale-up, or technically demanding environment where you worked hands-on across the data pipeline. Exposure to regulated or data-sensitive industries such as MedTech, Pharma, FinTech, or healthcare is a plus, especially when it comes to data quality, validation, and documentation.
Language Skills: English at a highly proficient level is a must. French is advantageous.
Personality: A curious, pragmatic, and hands-on problem-solver, you enjoy bringing structure into complex data environments. You work independently, ask the right questions, stay well organized, and collaborate openly with Data Engineering, Data Science, ML, Algorithm, Core Tech, and Product stakeholders. You are based in Switzerland and are comfortable working in a hybrid setup with one day per week regular presence in Neuchâtel.




We’re looking for a pragmatic, hands-on data builder who enjoys working with real-world clinical and company data — someone who is excited to bring structure into scattered datasets, build reliable pipelines, and help create a modern data foundation for advanced ML and digital health applications.




Do you want to grow your expertise in Data Engineering and cloud-based lakehouse architectures while contributing to health technology that has a direct impact on hundreds of thousands of people? Then we’re excited to meet you!

  • Data Ingestion & Lakehouse Development: Design, build, and maintain data ingestion and processing pipelines within Aktiia’s Databricks-hosted medallion Lakehouse, working under senior guidance while gradually taking on more ownership.
  • Clinical & Legacy Data Integration: Take an active role in ingesting, centralizing, and documenting clinical data currently spread across EDC platforms such as Castor and RedCap, databases, standalone archives, and other historically grown sources.
  • Data Exploration & Practical Data Archaeology: Work hands-on with unfamiliar and sometimes messy datasets, identify structures and inconsistencies, and turn unstructured situations into reliable, usable data assets.
  • Pipeline Development & Preprocessing: Build preprocessing and ETL/ELT pipelines that provide clean, structured, and model-ready datasets for Algorithm Development, Core Tech, Machine Learning, and Data Science teams.
  • Data Quality, Validation & Documentation: Define and apply practical standards for data quality, validation, traceability, and documentation — especially for sensitive and clinically relevant datasets.
  • Observability & Engineering Practices: Implement logging, validation checks, alerting, and basic observability for new pipelines, while contributing to shared codebase practices such as Git, code reviews, CI/CD, and testing.
  • Platform Scaling & Collaboration: Support the evolution of the lakehouse from selected technical use cases toward a company-wide data infrastructure, working closely with the Senior Data Engineer, ML Engineers, Data Scientists, and cross-functional stakeholders.


Your Profile

  • Academic Background: Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Data Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
  • Professional Expertise: At least 1+ year of practical post-study experience in data engineering, data infrastructure, data ingestion, or a similar hands-on technical role. You may still be early in your career, but you have already worked with real data pipelines, production-oriented data workflows, or collaborative data platforms.
  • Technical Experience: You have solid hands-on experience with Python and SQL and bring practical experience with Databricks. You are familiar with cloud environments — ideally AWS. You understand the basics of ETL/ELT pipeline design, data ingestion patterns, and data modelling, and you have worked with shared codebases using Git, code reviews, CI/CD, or testing practices. Experience with Spark, Delta Lake, or Parquet is a strong plus.
  • Industry Fit: Ideally, you have gained experience in a start-up, scale-up, or technically demanding environment where you worked hands-on across the data pipeline. Exposure to regulated or data-sensitive industries such as MedTech, Pharma, FinTech, or healthcare is a plus, especially when it comes to data quality, validation, and documentation.
  • Language Skills: English at a highly proficient level is a must. French is advantageous.
  • Personality: A curious, pragmatic, and hands-on problem-solver, you enjoy bringing structure into complex data environments. You work independently, ask the right questions, stay well organized, and collaborate openly with Data Engineering, Data Science, ML, Algorithm, Core Tech, and Product stakeholders. You are based in Switzerland and are comfortable working in a hybrid setup with one day per week regular presence in Neuchâtel.


We’re looking for a pragmatic, hands-on data builder who enjoys working with real-world clinical and company data — someone who is excited to bring structure into scattered datasets, build reliable pipelines, and help create a modern data foundation for advanced ML and digital health applications.


Do you want to grow your expertise in Data Engineering and cloud-based lakehouse architectures while contributing to health technology that has a direct impact on hundreds of thousands of people? Then we’re excited to meet you!

Title: IT & Market Data Procurement Analyst
Description:
Are you looking for a high-visibility role within a global energy trading business, sitting directly in the CIO office? This is not a traditional procurement position — it’s a commercially focused role with real ownership, influence, and exposure at a senior level.




About the Role




This sucessful candidate will be responsible for end-to-end procurement, commercial management, and financial governance across the global technology estate, with a particular focus on IT services, software, and market data.




The position combines hands-on operational execution with commercial ownership and transformation, supporting IT leadership and senior business stakeholders to optimise technology spend, manage vendor relationships, and professionalise procurement processes across a fast-paced commodity trading environment.




The role actively promotes the adoption of data, automation, and AI-enabled capabilities within procurement activities and has global scope, high visibility, and regular exposure to senior management.




Key responsibilities




IT & Market Data Procurement

Manage the full procurement lifecycle for IT hardware, software, consultancy services, and market data
Support and challenge new technology spend, ensuring alignment with commercial best practice and governance
Act as a key interface between IT, business stakeholders, and external vendors




Market Data & Software Asset Management

Own software and market data subscriptions (licenses, renewals, usage, compliance)
Optimise costs through rationalisation and a strong understanding of vendor licensing models
Support evolving business needs (new desks, usage, trading activities)




Commercial & Contract Management

Manage IT contract renewals and vendor negotiations in collaboration with IT, Finance, and Legal
Maintain accurate contract records and strong vendor relationships
Monitor market trends and supplier roadmaps to anticipate risks and opportunities




Financial Management & Reporting

Track and analyse IT spend across software, hardware, consultancy, and market data
Support IT financial reporting, forecasting, and budget visibility
Perform spend analysis to identify trends, risks, and optimisation opportunities




Governance & Stakeholder Engagement

Prepare and support PMO, procurement, and governance forums
Produce high-quality reporting and materials for senior management and C-level audiences
Liaise effectively with global internal stakeholders and external suppliers
Promote strong procurement and compliance culture across the organisation.




Transformation & Continuous Improvement

Drive the shift from reactive to proactive, value-driven procurement
Lead process automation, digitalisation, and self-service initiatives
Leverage data, analytics, and AI tools to enhance reporting, controls, decision-making, and operational efficiency
Challenge tactical purchasing behaviours in favour of strategic procurement practices




Technical expertise




+3 years of experience in a similar role
Bachelor’s degree
Proven experience in IT procurement, commercial management, or technology finance
Exposure to market data and software licensing within financial services, trading, or a similar environment is highly desirable
Experience operating in a fast-paced, high-change environment with senior stakeholders exposure
Strong commercial and analytical skills, with the ability to interpret spend data and identify optimisation opportunities
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
Interest in process improvement, automation, and AI-enabled capabilities
Fluent English




Non-technical Skills




Self-motivated and adaptable, with a strong desire to learn and develop
Comfortable working under pressure and managing competing priorities
Flexible and pragmatic in approach, suited to a trading-driven environment
Confident presenting to senior management and executive audiences
Highly organised, detail-oriented, and proactive
Able to balance hands-on execution with continuous improvement initiatives

Title: Internship Data Product Value Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
As part of the Global Data Governance team, this role has the unique opportunity to shape the future of data products and data valuation at Liebherr. The goal will be to support the development of the data product foundation and build a standardized and scalable data value framework – focusing on identifying, evaluating, and maximizing the business impact of data-driven use cases across divisions.
We are looking for a graduate student (or soon-to-graduate) who is interested in learning on the job and contributing to the fascinating topic of data product & value management. This is a temporary engagement with a duration of 12 months.

Responsibilities:
Global Data Governance

Explore the role of data governance within the Liebherr Group, i.e., goals, scope, responsibilities, operating model, communities etc.
Align the activities around data product & value management with Liebherr’s global data strategy


Data Products

Learn about data products, what it means and how it fits into Liebherr
Support the creation and implementation of the global data product framework
Co-facilitate and contribute to the cross-divisional expert group on data products
Support the onboarding & piloting of the data product concept within divisions
Move from concept to reality, i.e., train stakeholders to build and manage data products
Assist in documentation and communication activities


Data Value

Identify and estimate business impact of data-driven use cases
Support the application and optimization of the data value approach
Contribute to the standardization of templates, metrics, and valuation logic
Automate the use-case documentation and valuation process
Develop dashboards and reports for value tracking
Scale the valuation approach across the entire company



Competencies:

Strong analytical and structured thinking skills
Proactiveness and diligence in problem-solving
Impeccable communication and storytelling abilities
Advanced Excel skills (modelling, calculations)
Fluency in English, French or German would be an asset.
Experience with Power BI and Databricks or similar tools is a plus
Experience with prompting and building AI agents is a plus
Prior understanding of master data management, SAP MDG or practical experience with data & analytics governance topics would be an asset
Hands‑on mentality with a strong willingness to learn and explore new topics
Enjoys collaborating in an international environment


Our offer:

A unique opportunity to gain practical experience in a strategic data governance environment of a globally operating company
Exposure to enterprise‑scale data platforms and governance frameworks
On‑the‑job training with experts in data governance, data engineering, analytics, and compliance
Hybrid work model and modern working environment in Bulle (Fribourg region)
Benefits of a stable and globally established family-owned company



Have we awoken your interest? Then we look forward to receiving your online application. If you have any questions, please contact Regina Abou el Naga.
One Passion. Many Opportunities.

The Company
Liebherr-International AG with its head office in Bulle is the holding company of the Liebherr Group and performs various functions in the areas finance, tax, auditing, legal, insurance, HR, and corporate communication. The Liebherr Group employs more than 55,000 people worldwide in over 150 countries.

Location
Liebherr-International AG
7, Rue Hans-Liebherr
1630 Bulle
Switzerland (CH)

Contact
Regina Abou el Naga
regina.abouelnaga@liebherr.com

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Our story

At IRMOS we are on a mission to maximize the safe operational life of existing buildings and bridges. We fundamentally improve the status quo in structural analysis and maintenance planning, by combining sensor data with intelligent algorithms. We are a dynamic team of innovative engineers, with extensive expertise in structural assessment, structural health monitoring, data science, and software development. We envision safer and more sustainable infrastructure for everybody and for this we need to clearly communicate infrastructure health metrics to various stakeholders.




The opportunity

Join us as a Data Engineer and be a part of reshaping structural maintenance through innovative data-driven solutions. Your expertise will drive data-driven structural assessment and contribute to the evolution of IRMOS in our dynamic startup environment.




What you will do:

As a Data Engineer, you will be designing, building, and maintaining scalable and efficient data pipelines, architecting distributed systems, implementing big data storage solutions, developing data integration frameworks, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless data flow and delivery. Your responsibilities will include:




Data Pipelines & Orchestration: Design, build, and optimize real-time, highly available data pipelines using distributed processing frameworks. You will lead our transition into modern orchestration tools, specifically Dagster.




Data Quality & Operations: Ensure high availability, reliability, and performance of data systems through proactive monitoring, alerting, and automated unit/integration testing.




Database Management: Own our database infrastructure. You will maintain, optimize, query-tune, and scale our databases to handle increasing data volumes and velocity.




API Integration: Develop and maintain robust custom API services to seamlessly fetch, ingest, and process data from a variety of third-party vendors and sources.




Data Science Collaboration: Act as the engineering bridge for our Data Science workflows, helping to streamline data preprocessing, feature engineering pipelines, and model deployment infrastructure through MLOPs processes and tools.




Architecture & Scaling: Ensure our overall data architecture is clean, thoroughly documented, and built to scale effortlessly alongside the company’s growth.




What you will bring:




Education & Experience: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science (or a related technical field) and 3+ years of experience in Data Engineering or backend infrastructure.




Pipeline Expertise: Proven track record of designing and building scalable ELT/ETL pipelines for complex data extraction and transformation.




The Tech Stack: Deep proficiency in Python and experience with modern orchestration frameworks (with a strong preference for Dagster, or Airflow/Prefect and a desire to learn Dagster).




Database: Deep knowledge of SQL and hands-on experience optimizing/scaling relational (e.g., PostgreSQL) and non-relational databases, as well as data warehousing/data lake architectures.




Software Engineering best practices: clean code, version control (Git), CI/CD pipelines, and containerization (Docker).




Data Science foundations: A solid foundational understanding of Data Science concepts (e.g., how data features are prepared and how models are productionized).







Who you are:

Motivated to integrate into a dynamic, high-performance, and exciting start-up environment.
Passionate about technical excellence and delivering innovative, complete solutions for industry-first products that set new standards in the construction industry.
Independent, with meticulous attention to detail, and strong organizational skills.
A collaborative team player who thrives in a dynamic and inclusive work environment.
Proficient in English, both written and verbal. Other languages are a plus.




What we offer:

A small, close-knit, ambitious team where your work ships, your ideas matter, and you can see the direct impact of what you build.
Take real ownership of your work from day one, not just a cog in a machine.
Workshops, seminars, and networking events to keep you sharp and connected in Zurich's tech scene.
Manage your own time and maximize your day by taking advantage of our flexible working environment.
Grow with the company - early employees shape the culture, the product, and their own career path.
Based in the heart of Zurich's tech scene at Technopark, surrounded by like-minded builders and innovators.







Take the next step in your career by joining our dynamic company!

Title: Business Transformation Specialist - Data & Analytics
Description:
Position Overview

For over 150 years, our dedication to being the Global Hausbank for our clients has been driven by our people – in around 60 countries and across more than 150 nationalities.

Their deep understanding, insights, expertise, and passion help our clients navigate an increasingly complex world – be it in our Corporate Bank, our Private Bank, our Investment Bank or our Asset Management division. Together we can make a great impact for our clients at home and abroad, securing their lasting success and financial security.

In this role, you will support Data & Analytics initiatives within Private Bank Switzerland, acting as both Business Analyst and Project Manager across reporting, data quality, regulatory and remediation topics.

Responsibilities


Drive Data & Analytics projects across three locations as Business Analyst and Project Manager, liaising with requestors, business stakeholders and IT delivery teams.
Understand data requirements from a content perspective, drive data discovery and analytics, and work with IT colleagues to create reports and interfaces.
Perform analysis in Avaloq to understand business processes, related data and requirements; ideally also interrogate data using SQL across areas such as banking and investment products, transactions and performance.
Prepare functional specifications, including data selection criteria, for developers and perform testing of the results.
Create audit-ready documentation in collaboration with the IT delivery team.
Design appropriate data quality controls and ensure their implementation.
Analyze reported issues in databases and source systems, including erroneous or missing data, to identify functional problems and data gaps.



Skills


Experience in project management across business and technical environments.
Good knowledge of private banking products, services and processes, ideally within Wealth Management.
Proven expertise in core banking platforms, preferably Avaloq, from a business or functional perspective.
Intermediate knowledge of database querying with SQL.
Solid understanding of data analysis and technical concepts related to data warehousing.
Excellent written and spoken English.



Well-being & Benefits

Emotionally and mentally balanced

We support you in dealing with life crises, maintaining stability through illness, and maintaining good mental health.

Physically thriving

We support you managing your physical health by taking appropriate preventive measures and providing a workplace that helps you thrive.

Socially connected

We strongly believe in collaboration, inclusion and feeling connected to open up new perspectives and strengthen our self-confidence and well-being.

Financially secure

We support you to meet personal financial goals during your active career and for the future.

We strive for a culture in which we are empowered to excel together every day. This includes acting responsibly, thinking commercially, taking initiative and working collaboratively.

Together we share and celebrate the successes of our people. Together we are Deutsche Bank Group.

We welcome applications from all people and promote a positive, fair and inclusive work environment.

Title: GLP Auditor
Description:
Job Title: GLP Auditor

Location: Basel

Contract: Permanent or Temporary




About us

Adryan is an internationally operating services group with offices in The Netherlands and Switzerland. We specialize in providing a full range of services for the life sciences industry, focusing on Project Management, Technical Operations, CQV, and Compliance. Our teams work with top clients in the life sciences sector, ensuring the highest quality and commitment.




Position Summary

We are seeking a quality-oriented GLP Auditor / Data Reviewer (m/f/d). In this role, you will be responsible for reviewing and verifying scientific raw data and paper-based study documentation in accordance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP).




This role serves as a central interface between the operational laboratory and study departments and Quality Assurance. You will ensure that all data is documented in a complete, consistent, traceable, and regulatory-compliant manner before it is incorporated into reports and regulatory submissions.




Main Responsibilities

Reviewing paper-based study documentation, raw data, laboratory records, and related documents for completeness, consistency, and GLP compliance
Reconciling data from internal databases with the corresponding original documents (paper records)
Identifying, documenting, and tracking deviations, discrepancies, or missing information
Conducting data reviews in accordance with internal QA processes and GLP requirements
Assisting with internal audits and inspections
Creating and maintaining audit trail documentation, review reports, and quality records
Working closely with study directors, scientists, and operational teams to resolve data-related issues
Ensuring data integrity




Requirements:

Experience on Swiss Ordinance on Good Laboratory Practice of 18 May 2005 and the relevant OECD GLP guidelines
QC/QA experience is also considered
Demonstrated proficiency in GLP audit procedures and documentation.
Knowledge of routine analytical methodologies, with an emphasis on chromatography
Knowledge of computerized system validation
Good interpersonal skills with demonstrable tact and diplomacy
Good computer skills including Microsoft Word and Excel. Knowledge of MS-Access and SQL would be a plus
With your openness and understanding of mutuality, your active communication and continuous value assessment you foster and encourage our team mentality. You communicate and work effectively with scientific staff at all levels within the Testing Facility and you see yourself as a creative problem solver.
Good oral and written communication skills in German and English

  • Reviewing paper-based study documentation, raw data, laboratory records, and related documents for completeness, consistency, and GLP compliance
  • Reconciling data from internal databases with the corresponding original documents (paper records)
  • Identifying, documenting, and tracking deviations, discrepancies, or missing information
  • Conducting data reviews in accordance with internal QA processes and GLP requirements
  • Assisting with internal audits and inspections
  • Creating and maintaining audit trail documentation, review reports, and quality records
  • Working closely with study directors, scientists, and operational teams to resolve data-related issues
  • Ensuring data integrity


Requirements:

  • Experience on Swiss Ordinance on Good Laboratory Practice of 18 May 2005 and the relevant OECD GLP guidelines
  • QC/QA experience is also considered
  • Demonstrated proficiency in GLP audit procedures and documentation.
  • Knowledge of routine analytical methodologies, with an emphasis on chromatography
  • Knowledge of computerized system validation
  • Good interpersonal skills with demonstrable tact and diplomacy
  • Good computer skills including Microsoft Word and Excel. Knowledge of MS-Access and SQL would be a plus
  • With your openness and understanding of mutuality, your active communication and continuous value assessment you foster and encourage our team mentality. You communicate and work effectively with scientific staff at all levels within the Testing Facility and you see yourself as a creative problem solver.
  • Good oral and written communication skills in German and English

Title: Intern Data Science & Machine Learning (80-100%)
Description:
Electricity flows with us. On behalf of Switzerland and in cooperation with international partners, we do our utmost every day to develop the best solutions for the Swiss electricity grid.

Our team in Aarau is looking for an

Intern Data Science & Machine Learning (80-100%) - 1583

Power Switzerland's Energy Future – From Day One

At Swissgrid, we don't just observe the energy transition, we engineer it. As the operator of Switzerland's national transmission grid, we sit at the nexus of a rapidly evolving European electricity market. Our Market Optimization & Decision Support team develops forecasting and automation solutions that directly support grid operators in their 24/7 mission to ensure a reliable power supply. By joining us, you will contribute to impactful work at the heart of Switzerland’s electricity system.

The Internship: Where Learning Meets Real Impact

You’ll work on challenging and meaningful data science and machine learning projects. You’ll gain unique insights into the Swiss transmission system while working alongside a motivated, international team of data scientists and engineers. The work is real from day one—real data, real decisions, and real impact. You’ll have access to a modern, cloud-native tech stack and the latest AI development tools, and you’ll be encouraged to bring and run with your creative, AI-driven approaches to complex data problems.

What You Will Work On

Roll up your sleeves for challenges like these:


Build and ship forecasting models that solve real energy-sector problems with a sharp focus on safety, scalability, and production robustness. You'll experiment freely: tuning hyperparameters, benchmarking architectures, and proposing novel approaches.
Design and build data pipelines that process large-scale time-series data reliably and efficiently, forming the backbone of model training and deployment workflows.
Explore and analyze data surface hidden patterns, engineer high-signal features, and push forecasting accuracy further.
Work AI-natively – integrate AI tools into your daily development practice to sharpen analysis, accelerate iteration, smoothen workflows and improve efficiencies.
Collaborate and communicate across teams, gather feedback from stakeholders, and make sure your work connects meaningfully to the broader business mission.


What Makes You a Great Fit

We are looking for a builder – someone with the curiosity to experiment and the discipline to deliver. You'll thrive here if you bring:


Academic foundation: Currently pursuing (or recently completed) a Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field.
Strong Python skills and solid software engineering instincts – version control, clean code, and reproducible workflows are second nature to you.
Machine learning depth: A genuine understanding of classical ML, statistical methods, time-series modeling, and predictive systems. Hands-on experience with libraries like Scikit-learn, PyTorch, or MLflow is a plus; familiarity with Apache Spark is a bonus.
AI-native mindset: You use GenAI tools to build things, not just to ask questions. Show us what you've already shipped.
Energy domain curiosity: Knowledge of transmission grid operations or electricity markets is a plus – but your drive to learn matters more.
Above all, we value curiosity, agency, and a growth mindset. If you're the kind of person who gets energized by hard problems and wants your work to matter, we'd love to hear from you. 🚀


What We Offer


Unique industry access – an immersive, behind-the-scenes look at Switzerland's electricity infrastructure and the challenges of building the grid of the future.
Meaningful work – varied and exciting tasks within a company unique to Switzerland., with real ownership from the start.
Flexibility – flexible working hours as part of a motivated, welcoming team.
25 days of holiday, plus company holidays between Christmas and New Year.
A modern workplace in the immediate vicinity of Aarau, with a state-of-the-art technical environment.

Title: Ingénieur de données/Ingénieure de données
Description:
CLEEVEN est un cabinet de conseil européen fondé autour de deux convictions fondamentales :

L'excellence des ingénieurs européens doit être un moteur de la compétitivité mondiale
Notre mission humaine : accompagner nos consultants dans leur développement personnel et professionnel

Présent en Suisse, nous accompagnons nos clients dans leurs projets de transformation digitale et de valorisation de la donnée.




Contexte du projet :

Nous recherchons un Data Analyst / BI Engineer pour accompagner le développement et l'évolution de solutions de reporting et d'analyse décisionnelle.

Vous interviendrez au croisement des enjeux métiers et techniques afin de transformer des données complexes en indicateurs de pilotage et en outils d'aide à la décision. Vous participerez à l'ensemble de la chaîne de valeur de la donnée : compréhension des besoins, modélisation, construction de tableaux de bord et amélioration continue des solutions analytiques.




Objectifs du projet :

Recueillir et analyser les besoins de reporting et d'analyse des équipes métiers.
Concevoir, développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord et solutions de reporting.
Extraire, transformer et analyser des données provenant de multiples sources.
Construire et faire évoluer des modèles de données et des couches sémantiques.
Définir et suivre des KPIs permettant de piloter l'activité.
Garantir la qualité, la cohérence et la fiabilité des données.
Accompagner les utilisateurs dans l'exploitation des outils décisionnels.
Participer à l'amélioration continue de l'écosystème BI et Data de l'entreprise.




Compétences clés :

Solides compétences en SQL et en bases de données relationnelles.
Expérience sur des outils de Business Intelligence et de visualisation de données.
Bonne maîtrise des concepts de modélisation de données.
Capacité à traduire des besoins métiers en solutions de reporting concrètes.
Excellentes capacités d'analyse et de résolution de problèmes.
Aisance dans les échanges avec des interlocuteurs métiers et techniques.
Bon niveau d'anglais professionnel.

Nice To Have

Expérience avec Looker et/ou LookML.
Expérience avec Power BI, Tableau ou des outils similaires.
Connaissances des concepts ETL/ELT et des pipelines de données.
Expérience sur des plateformes cloud (GCP, Azure ou AWS).
Expérience dans un environnement industriel ou manufacturier.
Connaissances de Python pour l'analyse de données ou l'automatisation.




Profil recherché :

Formation supérieure en informatique, data, systèmes d'information, mathématiques, statistiques ou ingénierie.
Expérience en tant que Data Analyst, BI Engineer ou sur un poste similaire.
Forte capacité d'analyse et sens du détail.
Capacité à comprendre les enjeux métiers et à transformer les données en informations exploitables.




À très vite !

Title: Data Analyst for Beam Instrumentation (SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP)
Description:
Job Details

Job Description

As an experienced Data Analyst with a solid background in physics, you will join the team responsible for the LHC Longitudinal Density Monitor (commonly known as the BSRL: Beam Synchrotron Radiation Longitudinal monitor) within the Beam Instrumentation Group.

In this role, you will lead the work on the online and offline data processing pipelines, which are fully implemented in Python. The analysis results from this instrument are mission-critical, directly impacting the calibration of the LHC experiments, facilitating the troubleshooting of operational issues, and enabling detailed beam physics analysis.

Additionally, you will play an important role in R&D and optimisation, conducting advanced studies to shape the upcoming upgrades of the BSRL instrument.

Your Responsibilities


Lead the redesign and optimisation of the current online and offline Python data analysis software for the BSRL instrument to ensure scalability, performance and reproducibility.
Develop specialised offline analysis routines using BSRL data, cross-correlating with other beam instrumentation where necessary, to meet the requirements of stakeholders.
Extend the online and offline diagnostics tools, architecting automated notification systems to detect early signs of instrument degradation, anomalies, or malfunctions.
Design and implement the specific software tools, calibration routines, and data pipelines required for the rapid commissioning of the renovated system after the Long Shutdown 3 (LS3).
Contribute to the validation and performance verification of the associated upgraded hardware planned for deployment during LS3.
Evaluate and benchmark different upgrade scenarios for LS3 by combining advanced data analysis of historical run data with physics-based simulation techniques.


Your profile


Proven background in the advanced analysis of complex experimental data, preferably from physics or large-scale scientific installations.
Extensive experience in software development with a strong emphasis on clean, maintainable code.
Basic experience in Monte Carlo-based physics simulation techniques.
Basic experience with laboratory work.


Skills


In-depth knowledge of advanced statistical methods, data modelling, and data analysis techniques applied to scientific datasets.
Proven proficiency in Python, including familiarity with the standard scientific stack (such as NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, etc) for high-performance processing.
Knowledge of version control systems (Git/Gilab) and continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD)
Basic knowledge of photon counting techniques would be advantage.
Spoken and written English, with a commitment to learn French.


Eligibility Criteria


You are a national of a CERN Member or Associate Member State.
You have a professional background in Applied Physics, Data Analytics (or a related field) and have either:
a Master's degree with 2 to 6 years of post-graduation professional experience;
or a PhD with no more than 3 years of post-graduation professional experience.
You have never had a CERN fellow or graduate contract before.

Additional Information

Job closing date: 24.08.2026 at 23:59 CEST.

Contract duration: 24 months, with a possible extension up to 36 months maximum.

Working hours: 40 hours per week

Job flexibility: Hybrid

Target start date: 01-December-2026

This Position Involves


Work during nights, Sundays and official holidays, when required by the needs of the Organization.


Job reference: SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP

Field of work: Data Science & Data Analytics

Benchmark job: 200140 - Applied Physicist

Global Benefits


A monthly stipend between 6372-7004 Swiss Francs per month (tax free) depending on your degree.
30 days of paid leave per year plus 2 weeks annual closure.
Coverage by CERN’s comprehensive health insurance scheme (for yourself, your spouse and children), and membership of the CERN Pension Fund.
Family, child and infant monthly allowances depending on your individual circumstances.
A relocation package (installation grant and travel expenses) depending on your individual circumstances.
Possibility to extend your contract up to 36 months.
On-the-job and formal training including language classes.


Overview of CERN - Discover a world where the impossible is made possible!

At CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, we are pushing the frontiers of science and technology. Our groundbreaking work brings together not only physicists but also a diverse range of professionals from engineering, technical, scientific, and administrative fields. Together, we foster an environment where innovation and collaboration thrive.

Every day, we face exciting new challenges and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research that shapes our understanding of the universe. We meet these challenges through the diverse perspectives within our teams, ensuring every contribution is valued and driving our shared sense of inclusion and purpose. Diversity is a core value of CERN since its foundation, and it remains central to our mission and continued success.

If you are ready to be part of a dynamic, inclusive community pushing the boundaries of knowledge, CERN is the place where your curiosity and skills can thrive. Be part of our mission to uncover what lies at the heart of the universe! TAKE PART!

More information about us, here: careers.cern

Title: Senior Daten Analyst:in Marketing Automation
Description:
Wer dieselben Werte lebt, kann echte Veränderung vorantreiben – für eine Zukunft, in der wir gemeinsam den Schweizer Finanzplatz prägen. Wir setzen auf Vertrauen, Wertschätzung und ein Miteinander auf Augenhöhe – in einem modernen, flexiblen Arbeitsumfeld am Puls der Zeit.

Senior Daten Analyst:in Marketing Automation

Du willst dich entfalten? Wir geben dir den Raum dazu.

Als Senior Data Analyst:in Marketing Automation gestaltest du den gesamten Prozess von Datensammlung, Analyse und Interpretation für bestehende und potenzielle Kund:innen. Du überführst datenbasierte Erkenntnisse in konkrete Massnahmen und unterstützt die Weiterentwicklung unserer Marketing-Automation-Strategie. Mit deinem Know-how treibst du die Personalisierung voran und stärkst so die Wirksamkeit unserer Kampagnen. Damit leistest du einen wichtigen Beitrag zum Erfolg von Marketing und PostFinance.

Jetzt bewerben

Pensum

70-100%

Arbeitsort

Bern / Homeoffice

Anstellungsdauer

unbefristet

Lohnrange

CHF 120’000 bis CHF 140’000 pro Jahr bei 100%-Pensum

Das kannst du bewirken


Du arbeitest eng mit Kampagnenverantwortlichen zusammen, verstehst ihre Bedürfnisse und unterstützt sie bei der zielgerichteten Zielgruppenselektion
Du erstellst Ad-hoc-Analysen, bereitest Daten visuell verständlich auf und leitest daraus datenbasierte Handlungsempfehlungen für Entscheidungsträger:innen ab
Du unterstützt die Messung und Weiterentwicklung relevanter KPIs zur Erfolgskontrolle von Marketingaktivitäten
Du begleitest Projekte von der Initialisierung bis zur Umsetzung und bringst dabei eine datenbasierte und kundenzentrierte Perspektive ein
Du bringst deine Ideen proaktiv ein und arbeitest eng mit Kolleg:innen aus Data Science, Marketing und IT zusammen, um innovative Lösungen zu entwickeln


Das bringst du mit


Erfahrung in Business- und Customer-Intelligence sowie im Umgang mit BI-Tools wie SAS, Tableau oder Power BI
Analytische Kenntnisse mit starkem Fokus auf Business-Impact und datengetriebenem Mindset
Verständnis für Marketing-KPIs, Customer Journeys und Kampagnenlogiken sowie Vorteil durch Kenntnisse in Marketing-Automation-Tools
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in SQL und Excel sowie Vorteil durch Kenntnisse in Python oder R
Kommunikationsstärke, Teamorientierung sowie Sicherheit in Datenvisualisierung und Storytelling im interdisziplinären Austausch
Selbstständige, strukturierte und zielorientierte Arbeitsweise


Lohngleichheit

Offenheit und Transparenz gehören zu unserer Kultur – auch beim Lohn. Deshalb geben wir bei Stelleninseraten die Lohnrange an. Damit setzen wir ein klares Zeichen für Lohngleichheit: Gleichwertige Arbeit wird bei uns im gleichen Rahmen entlöhnt und gemäss der Lohnsystematik von PostFinance fair festgelegt.

Für diese Stelle liegt der Jahreslohn bei einem 100%-Pensum zwischen CHF 120’000 und CHF 140’000. Der konkrete Lohn richtet sich nach deinem Profil und deiner Erfahrung und kann je nach Ausprägung leicht unter oder über dieser Bandbreite liegen.

Darum startest du mit uns

Zeit für Erholung

6 Wochen Ferien und die Möglichkeit auf unbezahlten Urlaub.

Flexible Arbeitsmodelle

Teilzeit, Job- und Topsharing.

Individuelle Entwicklung

Vielfältige Aus- und Weiterbildungsangebote, temporäre Stellenwechsel und Stages.

Moderne Arbeitswelten

 Du willst dich entfalten? Wir geben dir den Raum dazu.

Title : Data Engineer in Testing

Location : Zurich, Switzerland

Duration: 6-12 months extendable

Work model : 3 days onsite/week

Rate : CHF 680/day

Exp level : 7+ years

Mandatory skills : PySpark, Python, Test Automation(Cucumber / TestNG), Azure

Desired skills : Databricks, Airflow, ALM, Xray or Squash, DevOps, Agile

JD below

Data Engineer in Testing (Minimum – 7 Years work exp – Zurich Location)

Handson experience in working with test automation of complex data transformation pipelines
Experience in Designing and optimizing data testing frameworks
Proven experience in performing functional testing to assess for accuracy, suitability, and functionality.
Ability to create test strategies and plans for manual and automated test methods.
Experienced in Python and pyspark, working knowledge on databricks. Keen on overall processing / compute cost optimization
Experienced with Microsoft Azure and other services, and Airflow
Prior working knowledge of any test management tool ALM, Xray or Squash
Experience with the overall STLC (Software Testing Lifecycle) stages

MUST HAVE SKILLS

Python and pyspark are the language used across multiple agile pod within the team
Knowledge of different types of testing and respective test phases
Test Automation Framework: Cucumber / TestNG as per project setup
Automation Frameworks: Data-Driven Frameworks as per project setup
Build Tools: Gitlab as per project setup
API Testing: Postman / SoapUI as per project setup
Big Data Testing: Python and Pyspark, general usage of spark data processing engine
Understanding of cloud platform (Azure) and DevOps pipelines
Experience in Regulatory and Compliance Domain
Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies

OTHER BAU QA SKILLS (Supporting Skills)

Experience with test data identification and creation
Have a good understanding of non-functional test concepts (e.g., scalability, performance, and security)
Experience in leveraging Gitlab
Ability to work analytically and systematically throughout the development and testing life cycle
Experience of Agile and iterative development methodologies
Well-developed business communication skills – both written and verbal
Strong personal prioritization and time management
Methodology knowhow on how to ensure that project deliverables meet industry best practices
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and synthesizing skills (should be a quick learner and know how to figure things out with very minimal external help)

Title: Impact Measurement Trainee
Description:
Department: Olympic Solidarity

Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Contract Type: Internship (12 months)

Activity Level: 100%

Apply before: 22.09.2026




The International Olympic Committee is the guardian of the Olympic Games and the leader of the Olympic Movement.




A truly global organisation, it acts as a catalyst for collaboration between all Olympic stakeholders, including the athletes, the National Olympic Committees, the International Federations, Organising Committees for the Olympic Games, the Worldwide Olympic Partners and Olympic broadcast partners. It also collaborates with public and private authorities including the United Nations and other international organisations.




About the role:

As the Impact Measurement Trainee, you will be responsible helping and supporting the implementation of Olympic Solidarity’s monitoring, evaluation, research and learning framework while developing practical experience in data management, analysis and organisational learning. Additionally, you will also be assisting with the organisation, checking and preparation of quantitative and qualitative data, and contributing to reporting and dashboard inputs under supervision.




Your responsibilities:

Support the Impact Manager and the wider Olympic Solidarity team in implementing the monitoring, evaluation, research and learning (MERL) framework.
Assist with collecting, organising, cleaning and validating quantitative and qualitative data from different programme sources.
Help review datasets for inconsistencies, missing information and formatting issues, and flag potential errors for follow-up.
Assist with preparing basic analyses, tables, statistics and summaries to support reporting and presentations.
Contribute to the preparation and maintenance of data collection tools, trackers and documentation to support data quality and consistency.
Assist with updating dashboard inputs and other visual reporting tools under guidance from the team.
Support documenting changes made to datasets, file structures and working processes in a clear and organised manner.
Help liaise with programme teams and other internal stakeholders to gather information and support timely delivery of MERL-related outputs.
Support the identification of lessons learnt, good practices and areas for improvement, while building knowledge of MERL concepts and tools.
Provide support on a range of ad hoc operational and analytical tasks, including reviewing and cleaning up open grant applications and reports, carrying out preliminary analysis of grant applications and reports.

Our requirements

Recent graduate with a university degree or equivalent higher education qualification in a relevant fields.
Initial experience through studies, research projects, student jobs, internships or administrative/data support work involving structured information or datasets is an advantage.
Fluency in English. Knowledge of French or Spanish is an asset.
Good command of Excel or similar tools, including sorting and filtering data, using basic formulas, working with tables, removing duplicates and applying clear formatting.
Good command of data visualisation tools (eg. PowerBi).
Willingness to learn MERL concepts, data processes and new digital tools through coaching, feedback and hands-on practice.
Good command of standard corporate tools and ability to follow internal processes, user rules and documentation practices.




Come and join an organisation where the spirit of inclusion, gender equality and diversity is embedded in its DNA and which focuses on what unites us – a passion for sport and its values. Work in a flexible and rewarding environment which offers competitive employee benefits and numerous incentives to live a sustainable, healthy and active life. By joining us, you become part of the Olympic Movement and help to build a better world through sport. You will have the opportunity to work with people of more than 70 nationalities.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
“Join us to unleash the power of #data, leverage our cloud platforms and deploy at scale state-of-the-art initiatives! You will be part of a young, dynamic and talented team working on analytics and artificial intelligence with real-life, impactful business applications.” — Thomas M.; Data Officer

As a Data Engineer, you’ll be responsible for building and maintaining the data and BI infrastructure that powers our analytics and ML initiatives. You’ll design scalable data pipelines, ensure data quality, enable self-service analytic capabilities and build BI products across the organization.

HOW WILL YOU MAKE AN IMPACT ?

"The data engineer sits at the foundation of the data stack, ensuring that every insight in Looker is powered by a robust, scalable, and governed pipeline."

Navigating between technical infrastructure and business intelligence, you will have the opportunity to design the data models that drive business decisions across the Maison.

YOUR MAIN MISSIONS

Within the Cartier Data Office, you will build business-ready data models, high-performance infrastructure, and large-scale reporting dashboards.

Analytics Capabilities with Looker: You will configure and maintain Looker dashboards and explores, translating complex business questions into actionable insights. You will train "viewers" and "explorers" on how to get the most out of the platform.

Data Modeling & Transformation: You will design and implement dbt models and dimensional data marts, applying software engineering best practices (version control, testing) to the analytics layer.

Pipeline Engineering: You will build and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines using Airflow/Apache Composer, ensuring seamless data ingestion into BigQuery.

Platform Operations: You will monitor data platform health, troubleshoot quality issues, and implement data governance and access controls.

Documentation: You will maintain data definitions and lineage, ensuring Looker remains a "single source of truth" for the organization.

HOW WILL YOU EXPERIENCE SUCCESS WITH US ?

You have a Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related scientific field and 3-5 years of experience in data engineering or analytics engineering.

You have a strong proficiency in Looker (LookML) or PowerBI, and SQL/DBT. You know how to build sophisticated dashboards and the transformation layers that power them.

You have a deep understanding of Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery) and are expert in SQL and Python.

You have experience building pipelines with Airflow/Apache Composer.

You like to work in a fast-paced environment, quickly delivering new features for demanding business users using an Agile/DevOps operating model.

HOW DO WE KEEP YOU SMILING ?

In a young and dynamic team focused on personal development, you will take part in Cartier’s data journey, leveraging state-of-the-art tool.

You will work with multicultural stakeholders from different business units for a leading Company in the luxury industry.

You will have the opportunity to mentor team members as our data maturity grows.

Title: Business/Data Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
Ekkiden ist eine in 2019 gegründete internationale Beratungsgruppe: sie vereint ein Ökosystem leidenschaftlicher und engagierter Beraterinnen und Berater, die Transformationsprojekte in den Bereichen Organisation, Operations und Technologie durchführen, insbesondere in IT/Digital, Industrie/F&E sowie Nachhaltigkeit, für Großunternehmen und KMU in Frankreich, der Schweiz, Spanien und Deutschland.

Über die Rolle

Als Business Analyst und/oder Data Analyst übernimmst du eine zentrale Rolle an der Schnittstelle zwischen Fachbereichen, Data/BI und IT. Du analysierst Prozesse und Daten, definierst Kennzahlen und Berichte und trägst so massgeblich zu datenbasierten Entscheidungen und zur Optimierung der Geschäftsabläufe bei.

Aufgaben


Aufnahme, Analyse und Strukturierung von fachlichen und datenbezogenen Anforderungen aus den Business Units
Analyse und Dokumentation von Geschäftsprozessen sowie der zugrunde liegenden Datenflüsse und Systeme.
Durchführung von Datenanalysen und Auswertungen (z. B. in Excel oder BI-Tools) zur Unterstützung von Management-Entscheidungen.
Definition von KPIs, Reports und Dashboards in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen und Data/BI-Teams.
Erstellung von Fachkonzepten, Spezifikationen und User Stories für datengetriebene Lösungen (Reporting, Analytics, Automatisierung).
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit IT, Data Engineers und BI-Spezialisten bei der Umsetzung der Anforderungen in Datenmodelle und Berichte.
Kontinuierliche Identifikation von Optimierungspotenzialen in Prozessen, Datenqualität und Transparenz.
Strukturierte Dokumentation von fachlichen Anforderungen, Prozessen und relevanten Datenstrukturen.


Must-have


Mehrjährige Erfahrung als Business Analyst, Data Analyst, Requirements Engineer oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle im datengetriebenen Umfeld
Sehr gute analytische Fähigkeiten und ein ausgeprägtes Zahlenverständnis.
Erfahrung in der Arbeit mit Daten und Reporting (z. B. Excel, SQL-Grundlagen oder gängige BI-Tools z.B. Power BI)
Erfahrung in der Aufnahme und Dokumentation von Anforderungen (z. B. mittels Use Cases, User Stories, Fachkonzepten).
Sicheres Auftreten und starke Kommunikationsfähigkeit gegenüber fachlichen und technischen Stakeholdern.
Strukturierte, selbständige Arbeitsweise und hohe Qualitätsorientierung.
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1) in Wort und Schrift, gute Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil.


Nice to have


Kenntnisse in Datenmodellierung, DWH- oder ETL-Konzepten.
Erfahrung in agilen Methoden (Scrum, Kanban) und Arbeit mit Tools wie Jira und Confluence.
Kenntnisse in Prozessmodellierung (z. B. BPMN, UML).


Wie sieht der Rekrutierungsprozess bei Ekkiden aus?

Bei Ekkiden sind wir bestrebt, jedem Kandidaten eine positive Erfahrung zu bieten.


Erstgespräch: Unser Rekrutierungsteam wird sich mit Ihnen in Verbindung setzen, um Ihre Motivation und Erwartungen zu besprechen.
Interviews: Je nach der Rolle, für die Sie sich bewerben, führen Sie ein oder zwei Interviews mit unseren Geschäftsleitern.
Kundengespräch: Schließlich haben Sie die Möglichkeit, den Kunden kennenzulernen.


Obwohl dies unser Standardprozess ist, können wir Anpassungen vornehmen, die auf spezifischen Umständen basieren.

Title: Data Analyst – Cloud Migration & BigQuery
Description:
Descrizione Del Lavoro

Our organization is launching a strategic initiative to migrate its data infrastructure from an on-premise Oracle Database environment to Google Cloud Platform (GCP). The project involves consolidating legacy data warehouses, re-architecting ETL/ELT pipelines, and adopting cloud-native services such as BigQuery, Cloud Storage, and Dataflow.

The selected Data Analyst will be a key contributor across every phase of the migration lifecycle: from as-is data analysis of source systems, through post-migration validation, to supporting business teams in their transition to new cloud-based analytics tools

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Mapping


Inventory and document Oracle source data (schemas, tables, stored procedures, views)

Title: Content Editor
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Content Editor based in Switzerland.

This role is designed for an editorial professional who combines strong content expertise with a passion for AI-driven marketing innovation.

You will help shape a modern content ecosystem focused on improving visibility across search engines and AI-powered platforms.

The position blends editorial excellence, content operations, AI workflow development, and digital growth strategy.

You will build scalable processes that improve content quality, accuracy, discoverability, and long-term performance.

Working closely with marketing leadership and content teams, you will influence how audiences discover and engage with valuable product insights.

This is an opportunity to join a remote-first environment where experimentation, ownership, and continuous learning are highly encouraged.

Accountabilities

The Content Editor will own content quality, AI-powered content operations, and visibility strategies while ensuring that published materials are accurate, impactful, and optimized for modern discovery channels.


Develop and execute Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and AI Optimization (AIO) strategies to improve visibility across AI-powered search platforms.
Monitor brand presence, citation performance, and AI platform visibility, transforming insights into actionable content improvements.
Build and maintain AI-assisted content workflows using tools such as Claude or similar platforms for drafting, editing, tagging, and updating content.
Establish quality standards, review processes, and checkpoints to ensure AI-generated content remains accurate, consistent, and aligned with brand guidelines.
Manage content refresh processes to maintain performance, relevance, and visibility of existing content libraries.
Recruit, coordinate, and manage freelance writers, agencies, video contributors, and AI agents supporting content production.
Create detailed content briefs, outlines, and editorial guidance for writers and contributors.
Proofread, edit, and quality-check all content before publication to ensure clarity, accuracy, and strong editorial standards.
Coach writers by providing actionable feedback and helping improve content quality over time.
Support digital community growth initiatives, including maintaining a presence in relevant online communities.
Collaborate with marketing and content stakeholders on strategy, research, topics, formats, and content priorities.


Requirements

The ideal candidate has strong editorial experience combined with hands-on knowledge of AI content workflows, SEO, and digital marketing operations. They should be detail-oriented, highly organized, and comfortable managing multiple content initiatives independently.


Proven hands-on experience using Claude or similar AI tools to create and optimize content workflows.
Practical understanding of AEO, GEO, and AIO concepts and their impact on digital visibility.
Exceptional writing, proofreading, editing, and content quality assurance skills.
Experience creating B2B SaaS content or working in a technology-focused content environment.
Strong understanding of SEO principles, keyword research, and content optimization strategies.
Experience using SEO and analytics tools such as Ahrefs, Google Search Console, and Google Analytics.
Previous experience managing freelance writers, agencies, contractors, or AI-assisted content contributors.
Strong project management skills with the ability to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines independently.
Ability to provide constructive feedback, coach contributors, and maintain high editorial standards.
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate effectively across teams.
Experience with WordPress, content management platforms, AI visibility tools, technical SEO audits, Screaming Frog, SurferSEO, or similar tools is an advantage.
Previous experience building AI-powered content operations or working in a fast-paced digital environment is preferred.


Benefits


Competitive compensation package with quarterly performance-based bonuses.
Clear career progression opportunities.
Hands-on experience working with emerging AI content technologies.
Opportunity to contribute to innovative digital marketing and content strategies.
Fully remote work environment.
15 days of paid time off plus public holidays.
14 days of paid sick leave.
Continuous learning opportunities in AI, content operations, and growth marketing.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Management Lead
Description:
Data Management Lead | Financial Services | Zurich




We are working with a financial services firm to find a Data Management Lead to front the design, implementation and governance of the firm's Enterprise Data Management framework.




Responsibilities

Develop and execute the firm's Enterprise Data Management strategy aligned with business objectives
Design, implement and enhance the enterprise-wide EDM framework, including governance, metadata management, reference data management, master data management and data quality capabilities
Define enterprise data quality metrics and dashboards measuring completeness, accuracy, consistency, timeliness and integrity of critical data
Provide regular executive-level reporting on data quality performance, risks, trends and remediation progress
Lead enterprise-wide programmes and projects to establish and mature EDM capabilities
Manage cross-functional teams delivering complex data transformation projects
Establish data governance forums and decision-making bodies
Define data ownership, stewardship and accountability models




Requirements

10+ years of experience in Enterprise Data Management, Data Governance, Data Quality or related disciplines within financial services or asset management
Master's degree in Computer Science, Information Management, Finance, Business or a related discipline
Proven experience establishing and implementing enterprise-wide data management frameworks
Experience managing complex, cross-functional programmes involving multiple business stakeholders
Deep knowledge of Enterprise Data Management concepts such as Data Governance, Data Quality Management and Data Architecture principles
Outstanding stakeholder management skills, with proven capability engaging C-suite executives

Title: Data Analyst Intern (H/F/X)
Description:
Data Analyst Intern (H/F/X) - 6-month paid internship, starting September 1, 2026, Lausanne (hybrid)

LB&Partners SA is a Swiss firm specializing in optimizing the performance of industrial manufacturers. We work with major international groups to improve their EBIT through a unique combination of business expertise, advanced methodology, and in-house technology solutions.




What you will do

In collaboration with the Programme team, you will collect, consolidate, and clean data from various sources (ERP, Excel, client databases); structure and model datasets; conduct statistical and exploratory analyses to identify trends and anomalies; build and maintain dashboards and KPI reports using BI tools; contribute to the automation of recurring reports; and prepare clear presentations for internal teams and clients.




Your profile

Bachelor's/Master's student or recent graduate in Statistics, Economics, Management, Computer Science, Data Analytics, or a related field. A first professional experience (internship included). Good command of Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables). Fluent French and professional English (C1). German is a real asset, as are Power BI (or equivalent), SQL, and Python basics.




We are looking for someone rigorous, curious, and organized, who enjoys teamwork, shows professional maturity, and is motivated to continue with us after the internship - there is a real prospect of a permanent position, depending on performance and business needs.




What we offer

A paid internship with real responsibilities on high-impact industrial data projects; flexible hours and remote work 1–2 days per week; 5 weeks of vacation per year (pro-rated); modern offices in the heart of Lausanne; a multicultural, dynamic, and supportive team.




How to apply

Send your complete application (CV, cover letter, diplomas) to hr@lb.partners.




Personal data submitted with your application is processed by LB&Partners SA exclusively for recruitment purposes, in accordance with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP) and our privacy policy available at www.lb.partners.

Title: Business Analyst - Data & Analytics
Description:
Business Analyst – Data & Analytics




We are looking for a proactive Business Analyst with a strong understanding of Data & Analytics to bridge the gap between business and technology. You will work closely with stakeholders to identify, prioritize and shape analytics capabilities, translating business needs into scalable end-to-end analytics solutions.

We are looking for someone who understands the broader analytics landscape, enjoys working closely with the business, and can help design effective data-driven solutions.




In this role, you will:

Partner closely with business stakeholders to gather, analyse and challenge business requirements for analytics solutions.
Translate business needs into clear functional requirements and scalable end-to-end solution designs.
Collaborate with technical teams to deliver analytics and reporting solutions.
Support multiple analytics initiatives by identifying business priorities and helping shape the analytics roadmap.
Coordinate testing, business validation and successful deployment of new analytics capabilities.
Proactively identify opportunities to improve existing analytics solutions and processes.
Act as the key interface between business and technology, building strong stakeholder relationships and driving collaboration.
Stay up to date with modern analytics technologies and best practices.




Requirements

3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst in Data & Analytics, Business Intelligence or a similar domain.
Strong understanding of data, analytics and reporting, with the ability to design end-to-end business solutions.
Ability to translate business requirements into technical specifications and communicate effectively with both business and technical stakeholders.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Dynamic, hands-on and proactive personality with a strong ownership mindset.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with multiple parallel initiatives.

Title: Digital Marketing Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Chiquita is a leading global company with 20,000 employees across 30 countries. We are passionate about our nutritious, high-quality, energy-rich and value-added bananas.

Chiquita's products are sold in nearly 70 countries around the world. Chiquita Brands International grows, procures, markets and sells bananas and other fresh fruits under the Chiquita brand and other names.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities


Support the Digital team in daily operations and project execution, including:
eCRM operations and project management.
Coordination with external agencies and partners.
Management and optimization of both B2C and B2B websites.
Take ownership of digital projects of varying scope and complexity, from conception to delivery.
Build campaign and promotional pages using the internal Content Management System (CMS).
Develop and implement tag plans to support tracking and analytics.
Collect and consolidate data from both online and offline sources.
Design and maintain dashboards and deliver regular performance reports using Google Data Studio and/or PowerBI.
Monitor, interpret, and communicate trends in digital metrics and KPIs to drive actionable insights.
Support the team in the implementation of process simplification and automation.
Assist the team on item management tasks (GDSN, GS1) utilizing digital content syndication platforms.


Qualifications


Experience & Background
Solid understanding of the marketing and digital landscape.
Bachelor’s or master’s degree in business, engineering, marketing, or a related field.
Minimum 18 months of hands-on experience in digital marketing, with proven expertise in several of the following areas:
CRM
UX/UI
SEO/SEM
E-commerce
Email marketing
Content management / CMS
Reporting and marketing data analyst
Experience with Amazon, Azure or GCP stack is considered an asset.
Familiarity with GDSN, 1WorldSync / Syndigo interfaces, or major DAM platforms is a strong plus.
Certifications in Google Analytics, Data Studio, or PowerBI are highly desirable.
Key Skills
Strong understanding of digital marketing metrics and KPIs.
Tech-savvy with a broad range of digital skills.
In-depth knowledge of digital marketing activities and strategies.
Demonstrated experience in process simplification and automation.
Strong sense of ownership, accountability, and initiative in managing tasks and projects.
Excellent organizational and communication skills.


Additional Information

What We Offer:


Competitive compensation and benefits package
Opportunity to work in a dynamic, international environment within the food supply chain
A collaborative team culture committed to quality, sustainability, and growth
Exposure to global markets and international trade processes

Location: Zurich
Start date: ASAP
Duration: 12 months – with possibility of extension
 
Key Responsibilities
Build and improve Power BI dashboards and reports tracking KPIs and business performance
Develop data models — relationships, measures, calculated columns
Monitor and validate data quality; help fix issues at the source
Document KPI logic, data definitions, and reporting standards
Partner with business stakeholders to turn requirements into clear reporting specs
Support troubleshooting, refresh issues, and process automation

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Statistics, Engineering, or related field
Working knowledge of SQL (queries, joins, aggregations)
Some hands-on experience with a BI tool — ideally Power BI and/or Strategy (MicroStrategy)
Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail
Fluent English, written and verbal — able to explain data to non-technical audiences
Self-starter mentality: curious, independent, and collaborative

Title: Technical Support Specialist
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Technical Support Specialist based in Switzerland.

This is an exciting opportunity to join a fast-growing, globally distributed environment at the forefront of business messaging technology. The role combines technical troubleshooting, customer interaction, and cross-functional collaboration to deliver exceptional support experiences. You will work closely with clients, partners, and internal teams to resolve complex API-related issues and help organizations maximize the value of messaging platforms. The position offers significant exposure to modern communication technologies, web development concepts, and large-scale B2B ecosystems. Ideal for technically minded professionals with strong communication skills, this role provides the chance to deepen expertise while contributing to innovative messaging solutions in a fully remote setting.

Accountabilities

The Technical Support Specialist will serve as a key point of contact for customers and partners, ensuring timely issue resolution and delivering high-quality technical support across multiple channels.


Provide technical support through tickets, chat, email, and virtual meetings, ensuring excellent customer experiences.
Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to APIs, integrations, and messaging platform functionalities.
Collaborate with second-level support teams to identify root causes and implement effective solutions.
Guide customers and partners through platform features, onboarding processes, and best practices within the business messaging ecosystem.
Work closely with internal commercial and onboarding teams to ensure smooth customer journeys and successful implementations.
Communicate with external platform representatives when required to escalate or resolve complex technical issues.
Maintain accurate documentation of support cases, resolutions, and recurring issues to improve operational efficiency.
Contribute to process improvements and help identify opportunities to enhance support quality and customer satisfaction.


Requirements

The ideal candidate combines strong technical knowledge with customer-centric communication skills and a passion for solving complex problems.


3 to 4 years of experience in technical support, customer support, or related roles.
Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong understanding of web technologies, including HTML, JavaScript, CSS, and APIs.
Hands-on experience working with REST APIs and Webhooks.
Solid knowledge of technical fundamentals and experience using helpdesk or ticketing systems.
Professional proficiency in English (C1 level or above) and fluency in at least one additional language.
Excellent troubleshooting and analytical problem-solving skills.
Strong communication and interpersonal abilities, including active listening and customer empathy.
Proven ability to manage multiple priorities effectively in fast-paced environments while maintaining attention to detail.
Interest in developing expertise within more advanced technical support environments.


Nice To Have


Experience with WhatsApp Business API or Meta Graph API.
Programming knowledge in SQL, JavaScript, Python, React, or similar technologies.
Familiarity with monitoring and observability tools such as Grafana.
Previous people management experience.
Additional language capabilities.


Benefits


Fully remote, contract-based opportunity with the flexibility to work from anywhere.
Competitive annual compensation ranging from €20,000 to €30,000, depending on experience and qualifications.
Opportunity to work within an innovative and rapidly evolving business messaging industry.
Collaboration with experienced professionals and technology experts in an international environment.
Continuous learning opportunities and exposure to modern communication technologies.
Dynamic, entrepreneurial culture with significant autonomy and ownership.
Supportive environment that encourages professional growth and knowledge sharing.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). Join a fast-paced, international recruitment team where you'll play a key role in attracting top technical talent across Data, AI, and broader technology functions. In this position, you will manage the full recruitment lifecycle, partnering closely with hiring managers to build high-performing teams while delivering an outstanding candidate experience.
